words.d: When someone is afraid, they feel fear.
words.e: The woman was afraid of what she saw.
words.zh: 當某人感到害怕時，他們會感受到恐懼。

words.d: To agree is to say “yes” or to think the same way.
words.e: A: The food is very good in that restaurant. B: I agree with you.
words.zh: 同意就是說「是」或持有相同的想法。

words.d: When someone is angry, they may want to speak loudly or fight.
words.e: She didn’t do her homework, so her father is angry.
words.zh: 當某人憤怒時，他們可能會想大聲說話或打架。

words.d: To arrive is to get to or reach some place.
words.e: The bus always arrives at the corner of my street at 4:00.
words.zh: 到達就是抵達或來到某個地方。

words.d: To attack is to try to fight or to hurt.
words.e: The man with the sword attacked the other man first.
words.zh: 攻擊就是試圖打架或傷害他人。

words.d: The bottom is the lowest part.
words.e: The bottom of my shoe has a hole in it.
words.zh: 底部是最低的部分。

words.d: When someone is clever, they can solve a hard puzzle or problem.
words.e: The clever boy thought of a good idea.
words.zh: 當一個人聰明時，他們能解決困難的謎題或問題。

words.d: When someone is cruel, they do bad things to hurt others.
words.e: The cruel man yelled at his sister.
words.zh: 當一個人殘忍時，他們會做壞事來傷害他人。

words.d: If something happens finally, it happens after a longtime or at the end.
words.e: He finally crossed the finish line after five hours of running.
words.zh: 如果某事終於發生，那是在很長時間之後或最後才發生。

words.d: To hide is to try not to let others see you.
words.e: The other children will hide while you count to 100.
words.zh: 隱藏就是試圖不讓別人看見你。

words.d: To hunt is to look for or search for an animal to kill.
words.e: Long ago, people hunted with bows and arrows.
words.zh: 狩獵是尋找或搜尋動物以殺死牠們。

words.d: A lot means a large number or amount of people, animals, things, etc.
words.e: There are a lot of apples in the basket.
words.zh: 很多指的是大量的人、動物、事物等。

words.d: The middle of something is the center or halfway point.
words.e: The Canadian flag has a maple leaf in the middle of it.
words.zh: 某物的中間是中心或中點。

words.d: A moment is a second or a very short time.
words.e: I was only a few moments late for the meeting.
words.zh: 片刻是一秒或非常短的時間。

words.d: When someone is pleased, they are happy.
words.e: She was pleased with the phone call she received.
words.zh: 當一個人感到滿意時，他們是快樂的。

words.d: To promise is to say you will do something for sure.
words.e: He promised to return my key by tomorrow.
words.zh: 承諾就是說你一定會做某件事。

words.d: To reply is to give an answer or say back to someone.
words.e: She asked him what time his meeting was. He replied, “at three.”
words.zh: 回覆就是給出答案或回應某人。

words.d: When a person is safe, they are not in danger.
words.e: Put on your seat belt in the car to be safe.
words.zh: 當一個人安全時，他們沒有危險。

words.d: A trick is something you do to fool another person.
words.e: His card trick really surprised us.
words.zh: 詭計是你用來欺騙他人的行為。

words.d: You use well to say that something was done in a good way.
words.e: The couple can dance quite well.
words.zh: 你用「好」來表示某事以良好的方式完成。

words.d: An adventure is a fun or exciting thing that you do.
words.e: Riding in the rough water was an adventure.
words.zh: 冒險是指你從事的某件有趣或刺激的事情。

words.d: To approach something means to move close to it.
words.e: The boy approached his school.
words.zh: 接近某物意味著向它靠近。

words.d: Carefully means with great attention, especially to detail or safety.
words.e: The baby carefully climbed down the stairs.
words.zh: 小心地表示非常專注，尤其注重細節或安全。

words.d: A chemical is something that scientists use in chemistry.
words.e: The scientist mixed the chemicals.
words.zh: 化學物質是科學家在化學中使用的東西。

words.d: To create means to make something new.
words.e: She created an igloo from blocks of snow.
words.zh: 創造意味著做出新的事物。

words.d: Evil describes something or someone bad or cruel, not good.
words.e: The evil figure scared us all.
words.zh: 邪惡形容某物或某人壞或殘忍，而非良善。

words.d: An experiment is a test that you do to see what will happen.
words.e: The student did an experiment in science class.
words.zh: 實驗是你為了觀察結果而進行的測試。

words.d: To kill someone or something is to make them die.
words.e: I killed the fly with a fly swatter.
words.zh: 殺死某人或某物就是使其死亡。

words.d: A laboratory is a room where a scientist works.
words.e: My mother works in a laboratory.
words.zh: 實驗室是科學家工作的房間。

words.d: Laugh is the sound made when someone is happy or a funny thing occurs.
words.e: The sound of their laugh filled the room.
words.zh: 笑聲是當人快樂或發生有趣事情時發出的聲音。

words.d: If a sound is loud, it is strong and very easy to hear.
words.e: The man’s voice was so loud that we all could hear him.
words.zh: 如果聲音很大，表示它強烈且非常容易聽見。

words.d: When a person is nervous, they think something bad will happen.
words.e: The boy became nervous when he heard the news.
words.zh: 當一個人緊張時，他們認為壞事將會發生。

words.d: A noise is an unpleasant sound.
words.e: The crying baby made a loud noise.
words.zh: 噪音是一種令人不快的聲音。

words.d: A project is a type of work that you do for school ora job.
words.e: His afternoon work project was to paint the room green.
words.zh: 專案是你在學校或工作中從事的一種工作類型。

words.d: To scare someone is to make them feel afraid.
words.e: My uncle was scared by what he saw in the room.
words.zh: 嚇唬某人就是讓他們感到害怕。

words.d: A secret is something that you do not tell other people.
words.e: The two boys were sharing a secret.
words.zh: 秘密是你不會告訴他人的事。

words.d: To shout is to say something loudly.
words.e: My boss shouted at me because I was late for work.
words.zh: 大喊就是大聲說出某事。

words.d: To smell something means to use your nose to sense it.
words.e: The two friends smelled the flower.
words.zh: 聞到某物是指用鼻子去感知它。

words.d: If something is terrible, it is very bad.
words.e: The way he treated his classmate was terrible.
words.zh: 如果某事物很可怕，表示它非常糟糕。

words.d: If something is worse, it is of poorer quality than another thing.
words.e: Business was worse this month than last month.
words.zh: 如果某事物更差，表示它的品質比另一事物低劣。

words.d: An alien is a creature from a different world.
words.e: The alien came in peace.
words.zh: 外星人是來自不同世界的生物。

words.d: If you are among certain things, they are all around you.
words.e: There was a red apple among the green ones.
words.zh: 如果你身處某些事物之中，它們就在你周圍。

words.d: A chart is a list of information.
words.e: We used a chart to see how we had improved.
words.zh: 圖表是資訊的列表。

words.d: A cloud is a group of water drops in the sky.
words.e: The sky was filled with white clouds.
words.zh: 雲是天空中一群水滴的集合。

words.d: To comprehend something is to understand it.
words.e: Henry could not comprehend the message.
words.zh: 理解某事就是明白它。

words.d: To describe is to say or write what someone or something is like.
words.e: They described their tree as colorful with gold ribbon and a star.
words.zh: 描述就是說出或寫出某人或某事物的樣子。

words.d: Ever means at any time.
words.e: Going skiing last winter was the most fun I’ve ever had.
words.zh: 「曾經」意指在任何時候。

words.d: To fail means you do not succeed in what you try to do.
words.e: Since he failed to get the job, he was sad.
words.zh: 失敗是指你未能成功做到想做的事。

words.d: Friendly is behaving in a pleasant, kind way toward someone.
words.e: The friendly animals came up to the girl.
words.zh: 友善是以愉快、親切的方式對待他人。

words.d: A grade is a score or mark given to someone’s work.
words.e: I managed to get good grades on my report card.
words.zh: 分數是對某人作品給予的評分或標記。

words.d: Instead means in place of.
words.e: He ate the carrot instead of the ice cream.
words.zh: 「Instead」意指「替代」。

words.d: A library is a place where you go to read books.
words.e: The library at school is full of books.
words.zh: 圖書館是你去閱讀書籍的地方。

words.d: A planet is a large round thing in space.
words.e: Saturn is the planet with the ring around it.
words.zh: 行星是太空中巨大的圓形物體。

words.d: A report is something students write for school.
words.e: Karen had trouble writing her report.
words.zh: 報告是學生為學校所寫的作業。

words.d: Several is more than two but not many.
words.e: He had to read several books for class.
words.zh: 「Several」指多於兩個但不算很多。

words.d: To solve something is to find an answer to it.
words.e: All the students could easily solve the math problem.
words.zh: 解決某件事就是找出它的答案。

words.d: If something happens suddenly, it happens quickly and unexpectedly.
words.e: I was suddenly surprised by the cake my friends brought me.
words.zh: 如果某事突然發生，表示它來得又快又出乎意料。

words.d: To suppose is to guess.
words.e: I suppose I should go home now.
words.zh: 「To suppose」就是猜測。

words.d: The universe is the known or supposed objects in space.
words.e: The universe is so large that it cannot be measured.
words.zh: 宇宙是太空中已知或假設存在的物體。

words.d: To view is to look at something.
words.e: Michael likes to view himself in the mirror.
words.zh: 「To view」就是觀看某物。

words.d: When a thing is appropriate, it is right or normal.
words.e: It’s appropriate to wear a suit when you go to the office.
words.zh: 當某事物是適當的，表示它是正確或正常的。

words.d: To avoid something is to stay away from it.
words.e: Avoid the broken bottle on the floor.
words.zh: 避免某事就是遠離它。

words.d: To behave is to act in a particular way, especially to be good.
words.e: She always behaves well when her father is around.
words.zh: 「To behave」是以特定方式行動，尤其是表現良好。

words.d: When someone is calm, they do not get excited or upset.
words.e: A nice warm bath makes me feel so calm.
words.zh: 當某人冷靜時，他們不會激動或煩躁。

words.d: Concern is a feeling of worry.
words.e: I was filled with concern after reading the newspaper.
words.zh: 擔憂是一種憂慮的感覺。

words.d: To be content is to be happy and not want more.
words.e: The baby looked very content sitting on the floor.
words.zh: 知足即快樂，不貪求更多。

words.d: If you expect something to happen, you believe it will happen.
words.e: I expect the bus to be here very soon.
words.zh: 若預期某事發生，即相信它會發生。

words.d: When something happens frequently, it happens often.
words.e: We meet frequently, either at the beginning or ending of the week.
words.zh: 頻繁發生之事，即經常發生。

words.d: A habit is a thing that you do often.
words.e: Smoking is a bad habit that can kill you.
words.zh: 習慣是經常做的事。

words.d: To instruct is to teach.
words.e: My teacher instructs us in several subjects.
words.zh: 指導即教導。

words.d: An issue is an important topic.
words.e: The men spoke about issues that were important to the people.
words.zh: 議題是重要的主題。

words.d: None means not any of someone or something.
words.e: He spent all his money. There is none left.
words.zh: 無意指沒有任何一個人或事物。

words.d: If a person is patient, they don’t become angry or upset easily.
words.e: I had to be patient and wait until 5 o’clock to leave.
words.zh: 若人有耐心，便不易生氣或煩躁。

words.d: If something is positive, it is good.
words.e: She has a positive future ahead of her after finishing college.
words.zh: 正面的事物即為良好。

words.d: To punish means to make someone suffer for breaking the rules or laws.
words.e: To punish me, my teacher had me stand in the corner.
words.zh: 懲罰意指因違反規則或法律而使人受苦。

words.d: To represent is to speak or act for a person or group.
words.e: My lawyer will represent me in court.
words.zh: 代表即為個人或群體發言或行事。

words.d: To shake is to move back and forth or up and down quickly.
words.e: When people shake hands, it usually means they agree.
words.zh: 搖晃指快速前後或上下移動。

words.d: To spread is to move quickly to more places.
words.e: I like to spread butter on my toast.
words.zh: 蔓延指迅速擴散至更多地方。

words.d: To stroll means to walk slowly and calmly.
words.e: My dog and I strolled through the park today.
words.zh: 漫步意指緩慢而平靜地行走。

words.d: A village is a very small town.
words.e: There are only a few houses in my village.
words.zh: 村莊是非常小的城鎮。

words.d: If you are aware of something, you know about it.
words.e: I was not aware of the ringing phone.
words.zh: 如果你意識到某事，表示你了解它。

words.d: Badly means in a severe or harmful way.
words.e: He hurt his arm badly playing with friends.
words.zh: Badly 意指以嚴重或有害的方式。

words.d: If something belongs to you, you own it.
words.e: The blue suit belongs to Paul.
words.zh: 如果某物屬於你，表示你擁有它。

words.d: To continue something is to keep doing it.
words.e: She stood under her umbrella as the rain continued to fall.
words.zh: 繼續某事就是持續做它。

words.d: An error is something you do wrong.
words.e: I made an error on my report, so my boss was angry.
words.zh: 錯誤是你做錯的事情。

words.d: An experience is something you have seen or done.
words.e: Rock climbing was a fun experience.
words.zh: 經驗是你曾經見過或做過的事。

words.d: A field is a big area of land.
words.e: The field of flowers looked so pretty.
words.zh: 田野是一大片土地。

words.d: To hurt is to do something that makes you feel pain.
words.e: She hurt her leg falling down the stairs.
words.zh: 傷害就是做出讓你感到痛苦的事情。

words.d: Judgment is the ability to form opinions or decisions.
words.e: It’s good judgment to recycle your aluminum cans.
words.zh: 判斷力是形成意見或決定的能力。

words.d: If something likely happens, it will probably happen.
words.e: I will likely stay at home and watch TV tonight.
words.zh: 如果某事很可能發生，它大概會發生。

words.d: If something is normal, it is not strange nor surprising to you.
words.e: It is normal for me to bathe every night.
words.zh: 如果某事是正常的，它對你來說既不奇怪也不意外。

words.d: If something is rare, you do not see it very often.
words.e: It is rare for him to miss his flight.
words.zh: 如果某事是罕見的，你不常見到它。

words.d: To relax is to rest.
words.e: The frog relaxed in the warm sun.
words.zh: 放鬆就是休息。

words.d: To request something is to ask for it.
words.e: The little girl requested a special gift from Santa Claus.
words.zh: 請求某事就是要求它。

words.d: To reside means to live somewhere permanently or for a long time.
words.e: My brother and his family reside in a lovely house on the beach.
words.zh: 居住意指永久或長期住在某處。

words.d: A result is something that happens because of something else.
words.e: Asa result of all the rain, the man had to climb on the roof.
words.zh: 結果是因某事物而發生的事。

words.d: To roll is to move by turning over and over.
words.e: You must roll the ball into the pins when you bowl.
words.zh: 滾動是指不斷翻轉移動。

words.d: Since is used to talk about a past event still happening now.
words.e: Since 1992, he has been driving that car.
words.zh: 「自從」用於描述過去發生且持續至今的事件。

words.d: If something is visible, it can be seen.
words.e: The moon and stars were visible in the night sky.
words.zh: 若某物可見，則能被看見。

words.d: If something is wild, it is found in nature.
words.e: You should be careful around a fox, because it is a wild animal.
words.zh: 若某物是野生的，則存在於自然中。

words.d: An advantage is something that helps you.
words.e: Being tall is an advantage to a basketball player.
words.zh: 優勢是對你有幫助的事物。

words.d: To cause is to make something happen.
words.e: The cold weather caused her to get sick.
words.zh: 導致是指使某事發生。

words.d: A choice is the act or possibility of picking something.
words.e: I had my choice of five doors to open.
words.zh: 選擇是挑選某物的行為或可能性。

words.d: A community is a group of people who live together.
words.e: The kids from my community usually play together.
words.zh: 社區是一群共同生活的人。

words.d: To be dead is to not be alive.
words.e: A dead person is usually buried in the ground.
words.zh: 死亡是指不再活著。

words.d: The distance between two things is how far it is between them.
words.e: The distance between the Earth and the Moon is 384,400 kilometers.
words.zh: 兩物之間的距離是它們相隔多遠。

words.d: To escape is to run away from something bad.
words.e: The butterfly could not escape from the cage.
words.zh: 逃脫是指逃離不好的事物。

words.d: If you face a problem, you deal with it.
words.e: My sister and I have to find a better way to face our differences.
words.zh: 若你面對問題，你便處理它。

words.d: To follow means to go behind someone and go where they go.
words.e: The little boy followed his mother home.
words.zh: 跟隨是指走在某人後面，並去他們去的地方。

words.d: Fright is the feeling of being scared.
words.e: She was filled with fright.
words.zh: 恐懼是感到害怕的感覺。

words.d: A ghost is the spirit of a dead person.
words.e: Many people are afraid of ghosts.
words.zh: 鬼魂是死者的靈魂。

words.d: An individual is one person.
words.e: Only one individual could win the bicycle race.
words.zh: 個體指的是一個人。

words.d: A pet is an animal that lives with people.
words.e: Out of all my pets, the dog is my favorite.
words.zh: 寵物是與人一起生活的動物。

words.d: To reach means to arrive at a place.
words.e: I was happy to finally reach my destination.
words.zh: 到達意指抵達某個地方。

words.d: To return is to go back to a place.
words.e: I was happy to return home to my mom after school.
words.zh: 返回就是回到某個地方。

words.d: To survive is to stay alive.
words.e: My dog survived her fall into the water.
words.zh: 生存就是繼續活著。

words.d: To be upset is to be unhappy about something.
words.e: She was upset because she broke her toy.
words.zh: 難過是指對某事感到不開心。

words.d: A voice is the sound a person makes when they talk or sing.
words.e: He used a microphone, so everyone could hear his voice.
words.zh: 聲音是人說話或唱歌時發出的聲響。

words.d: The weather is the condition of the air: hot, rainy, windy, etc.
words.e: The weather can be sunny, rainy, or cold.
words.zh: 天氣是指空氣的狀態：炎熱、多雨、颳風等。

words.d: To be wise is to use experience and intelligence to make good choices.
words.e: Many people believe that owls are very wise animals.
words.zh: 明智是指運用經驗與智慧做出好的選擇。

words.d: To allow something to happen means to let it happen.
words.e: Having a ticket will allow you to enter the show.
words.zh: 允許某事發生就是讓它發生。

words.d: To announce something is to make it known.
words.e: He announced to everyone his new idea for the company.
words.zh: 宣布某事就是讓它為人所知。

words.d: When someone or something is beside you, they are next to you.
words.e: The two brothers stood beside each other.
words.zh: 當某人或某物在你旁邊時，他們就在你身旁。

words.d: A challenge is something difficult to complete.
words.e: It was a challenge to climb to the top of the mountain.
words.zh: 挑戰是指難以完成的事情。

words.d: To claim means to say that something is true.
words.e: He claimed to know why the country’s laws were weak.
words.zh: 聲稱是指說某事是真實的。

words.d: The condition of someone or something is the state that they are in.
words.e: The patient’s condition was very good.
words.zh: 某人或某事物的狀況，指的是它們所處的狀態。

words.d: To contribute to something means to do something to make it successful.
words.e: We decided to contribute money to the new hospital.
words.zh: 為某事做出貢獻，意指採取行動使其成功。

words.d: A difference is a way that something is not like other things.
words.e: The biggest difference between the birds is the color of their feathers.
words.zh: 差異，是指某事物與其他事物不同的方式。

words.d: To divide something is to make it into smaller parts.
words.e: We divided the pizza.
words.zh: 分割某物，是將其分成較小的部分。

words.d: An expert is someone who is very good at doing something.
words.e: The wizard was an expert at magic.
words.zh: 專家，是指非常擅長做某事的人。

words.d: If someone or something is famous, they are known to many people.
words.e: The Eiffel Tower in Paris is very famous.
words.zh: 如果某人或某事物出名，他們為許多人所知。

words.d: Force is a person’s strength or power.
words.e: He used all his force to try and open the door.
words.zh: 力量，是指一個人的體力或權力。

words.d: Harm is hurt or problems caused to someone or something.
words.e: A hot iron can cause great harm if you are not careful.
words.zh: 傷害，是對某人或某事物造成的損害或問題。

words.d: To lay means to put or place in a horizontal or flat position.
words.e: Don’t lay your socks on the floor.
words.zh: 放置，意思是將物體水平或平坦地擺放。

words.d: Peace is a time without war.
words.e: A white dove is a symbol for peace.
words.zh: 和平，是指沒有戰爭的時期。

words.d: A prince is the son of a king.
words.e: The prince and the princess were married.
words.zh: 王子，是國王的兒子。

words.d: To protect someone is to stop them from getting hurt.
words.e: Firemen protect us from fires.
words.zh: 保護某人，是阻止他們受到傷害。

words.d: To sense something is to know about it without being told.
words.e: I could sense that he was watching me.
words.zh: 感知某事，是指在未被告知的情況下知道它。

words.d: When something is sudden, it happens very quickly.
words.e: He felt a sudden pain in his chest.
words.zh: 當某事突然發生時，它發生得非常快。

words.d: Therefore means for this reason.
words.e: He is fat. Therefore, he will go on a diet.
words.zh: 因此，意思是出於這個原因。

words.d: To accept something that is offered is to take it.
words.e: I accepted the girl’s very nice gift.
words.zh: 接受某樣東西就是把它收下。

words.d: To arrange things is to put them in the right place.
words.e: Please arrange the bowling pins in order so we can play.
words.zh: 整理東西就是把它們放到正確的位置。

words.d: To attend something is to go to it.
words.e: My sister and I attend the same school.
words.zh: 參加某活動就是前往該活動。

words.d: To balance something is to keep it from falling.
words.e: We saw an elephant balance itself on a ball.
words.zh: 平衡某物就是防止它倒下。

words.d: A contrast is the sharp difference between two things.
words.e: The contrast between my parents is very noticeable.
words.zh: 對比是兩件事物之間的顯著差異。

words.d: To encourage someone is to make them want to do something.
words.e: My football coach will encourage us when we are losing.
words.zh: 鼓勵某人就是讓他們想要去做某件事。

words.d: If someone or something is familiar to you, you know them well.
words.e: The two friends were very familiar with each other.
words.zh: 如果你對某人或某事感到熟悉，你就很了解他們。

words.d: To grab is to take a hold of someone or something suddenly.
words.e: I grabbed a pear from the tree.
words.zh: 抓住就是突然握住某人或某物。

words.d: To hang something is to keep it above the ground.
words.e: I drew a picture of my family, and my mother hung it on the wall.
words.zh: 懸掛某物就是讓它保持離地。

words.d: If something is huge, it is very big.
words.e: At work, my father drives a huge truck.
words.zh: 如果某物巨大，就是非常大。

words.d: If something is necessary, you must do it.
words.e: It is necessary to have a passport when you travel to a foreign country.
words.zh: 如果某件事是必要的，你就必須去做。

words.d: A pattern is a way in which something is done or organized.
words.e: My pattern of brushing my teeth is the same as most people’s.
words.zh: 模式是某件事被執行或組織的方式。

words.d: To propose something is to say that it should be done.
words.e: Santa Claus proposed that I try to be a good boy all year.
words.zh: 提議某事就是說它應該被執行。

words.d: A purpose is the reason that you do something.
words.e: The purpose of exercising is to get into shape.
words.zh: 目的是你做某件事的原因。

words.d: To release something is to stop holding it.
words.e: She released the bird from her hands.
words.zh: 釋放某物就是不再握住它。

words.d: To require something is to say that it is necessary.
words.e: We require teachers to have a university degree.
words.zh: 要求某事物，即表示其為必要。

words.d: If something is single, then there is only one.
words.e: I have a single key in my hand.
words.zh: 若某物為單一，則僅有一個。

words.d: Success is doing something well that you choose to do.
words.e: My daughter was a big success at school.
words.zh: 成功，是將你選擇之事做得圓滿。

words.d: To tear something means to pull it apart.
words.e: It is easy to tear paper.
words.zh: 撕裂某物，意指將其扯開。

words.d: A theory is an idea about how something works.
words.e: We talked about Einstein’s theory of relativity in class.
words.zh: 理論，是關於某事物運作方式的構想。

words.d: To be against something is to be touching it or opposed to it.
words.e: They both leaned against the wall.
words.zh: 反對某物，可指觸碰它或與之對立。

words.d: The beach is a sandy or rocky place by the ocean.
words.e: The little girl built a sandcastle on the beach.
words.zh: 海灘，是海洋邊緣的沙地或岩地。

words.d: To damage something is to break it.
words.e: The car was damaged in the accident.
words.zh: 損壞某物，即是將其破壞。

words.d: To discover something is to find it for the first time.
words.e: I discovered some new information in this book!
words.zh: 發現某物，意指首次找到它。

words.d: An emotion is how you feel.
words.e: Anger is a common emotion that we all feel.
words.zh: 情感，即是你感受的方式。

words.d: To fix something is to make it work.
words.e: My dad has many tools to help him fix broken things.
words.zh: 修理某物，即是使其恢復運作。

words.d: If you are frank, you are being very honest.
words.e: The teacher had a frank discussion with her students.
words.zh: 若你坦率，便是極為誠實。

words.d: To identify something is to be able to name it.
words.e: I used the file to identify his name.
words.zh: 辨識某物，即是能說出其名稱。

words.d: An island is land in the middle of water.
words.e: Japan is a group of islands.
words.zh: 島嶼，是位於水域中央的陸地。

words.d: The ocean is all of the salt water that surrounds land.
words.e: The ocean can make powerful waves.
words.zh: 海洋，是環繞陸地的所有鹹水。

words.d: Perhaps is used when you say that something could happen.
words.e: Perhaps I will eat an apple for lunch.
words.zh: 「或許」用於表示某事可能發生。

words.d: If something is pleasant, you enjoy it.
words.e: The character had a pleasant look on its face.
words.zh: 若某事令人愉快，你便會享受它。

words.d: To prevent something is to stop it from happening.
words.e: The handcuffs prevented me from moving my hands.
words.zh: 「預防」某事即阻止其發生。

words.d: A rock is a hard thing in the dirt.
words.e: I stacked rocks on top of one another.
words.zh: 「岩石」是泥土中的堅硬物體。

words.d: To save something is to keep it from being hurt.
words.e: I want to help save the world.
words.zh: 「拯救」某事即保護它免受傷害。

words.d: To step is to walk.
words.e: Be careful where you step.
words.zh: 「踏步」即行走。

words.d: Still is used when you say that a situation keeps going on.
words.e: They are still waiting in line to get tickets.
words.zh: 「仍然」用於表示某種情況持續進行。

words.d: A taste is the flavor something makes in your mouth.
words.e: The taste of the fruit was sweet.
words.zh: 「味道」是食物在口中產生的風味。

words.d: To throw something is to use your hand to make it go through the air.
words.e: The pitcher can throw the baseball very fast.
words.zh: 「拋擲」某事即用手使其在空中移動。

words.d: A wave is a line of water that moves higher than the rest of the water.
words.e: The water was filled with large blue waves.
words.zh: 「波浪」是比周圍水面更高的水線。

words.d: A benefit is a good thing.
words.e: Being able to fly is a good benefit to birds.
words.zh: 「益處」是好事。

words.d: If you are certain about something, you know it is true.
words.e: I am certain that zebras have stripes.
words.zh: 若你對某事「確定」，即你知道它為真。

words.d: A chance is an opportunity to do something.
words.e: I had a chance to see the Roman Coliseum last summer.
words.zh: 「機會」是從事某事的契機。

words.d: An effect is a change made by something else.
words.e: The medicine had a good effect on the boy.
words.zh: 「效果」是由其他事物造成的改變。

words.d: If something is essential, it is very important and necessary.
words.e: It is essential to have oxygen when you scuba dive.
words.zh: 若某事「必要」，則它非常重要且不可或缺。

words.d: If something is far, it is not close.
words.e: It’s far going from the east coast to the west coast of America.
words.zh: 如果某物很遠，它就不近。

words.d: To focus on something is to think about it and pay attention to it.
words.e: My mom always helps me to focus on my school work.
words.zh: 專注於某事物就是思考並留意它。

words.d: The function of something is what it does.
words.e: The function of a flashlight is to help you see in the dark.
words.zh: 某物的功能就是它的作用。

words.d: Grass is the green leaves that cover the ground.
words.e: The grass looked so soft and green.
words.zh: 草是覆蓋地面的綠色葉片。

words.d: To guard something is to take care of it.
words.e: The police officer will guard us from any harm.
words.zh: 守衛某物就是照顧它。

words.d: The image of something is a picture of it.
words.e: The image of her eye was very clear.
words.zh: 某物的形象就是它的圖像。

words.d: If something is immediate, it happens quickly.
words.e: An immediate response came from the pizza place.
words.zh: 如果某事物是立即的，它就會迅速發生。

words.d: If something is primary, it is the most important thing.
words.e: His primary thoughts are about money.
words.zh: 如果某事物是主要的，它就是最重要的。

words.d: If someone feels proud, they are happy about what they have done.
words.e: She is proud of the picture she drew of her house.
words.zh: 如果有人感到自豪，他們對自己所做的事感到高興。

words.d: To remain somewhere is to stay there.
words.e: My sister had to remain home since she was sick.
words.zh: 留在某處就是待在那裡。

words.d: To rest is to stop being active while the body gets back its strength.
words.e: I rested on the couch after work.
words.zh: 休息就是停止活動，讓身體恢復體力。

words.d: If two things are separate, they are not together.
words.e: New York and Los Angeles are in two separate parts of America.
words.zh: 如果兩件事物是分開的，它們就不在一起。

words.d: A site is a place.
words.e: We found the perfect site for our picnic.
words.zh: 地點就是一個地方。

words.d: A tail is a part of an animal’s body, sticking out from its rear or back.
words.e: Our dog wags its tail when it’s happy.
words.zh: 尾巴是動物身體的一部分，從其後部或背部伸出。

words.d: Trouble is a problem ora difficulty.
words.e: I have trouble working with my boss.
words.zh: 麻煩就是一個問題或困難。

words.d: Anymore means any longer.
words.e: Her old pants don’t fit her anymore.
words.zh: 「Anymore」意指「不再」。

words.d: When a person is asleep, they are not awake.
words.e: The baby has been asleep for hours.
words.zh: 當一個人睡著時，他們是醒著的。

words.d: A berry is a small round fruit that grows on certain plants and trees.
words.e: The berry looked delicious.
words.zh: 漿果是一種生長在特定植物或樹木上的小型圓形水果。

words.d: To collect things is to group them together all in one place.
words.e: I collected shells when I was younger.
words.zh: 收集東西就是把它們全部集中在一個地方。

words.d: To compete is to try to be better than someone.
words.e: He will compete with very good athletes.
words.zh: 競爭就是試圖比別人更好。

words.d: A conversation is a talk between people.
words.e: There were two conversations going on at once.
words.zh: 對話是人們之間的交談。

words.d: A creature is any living thing.
words.e: The creature we saw today was either a dolphin or a porpoise.
words.zh: 生物是指任何有生命的東西。

words.d: A decision is a choice.
words.e: He made the wrong decision.
words.zh: 決定就是一種選擇。

words.d: Either is used with “or” to say there are two or more possibilities.
words.e: You can choose to be either white or black when you play chess.
words.zh: 「Either」與「or」連用，表示有兩種或更多可能性。

words.d: A forest is a place with lots of trees and animals.
words.e: I love to go walking in the forest.
words.zh: 森林是一個有許多樹木和動物的地方。

words.d: The ground is the top part of the Earth that we walk on.
words.e: The ground under our feet was dry and brown.
words.zh: 地面是我們行走的地球頂層部分。

words.d: To introduce someone or something is to say who they are.
words.e: I introduced myself to our newest co-worker today.
words.zh: 介紹某人或某物就是說出他們是誰。

words.d: To marry is to legally become husband and wife.
words.e: Rose and Henry were married, and they lived happily.
words.zh: 結婚就是合法地成為夫妻。

words.d: To prepare is to get ready for something.
words.e: I prepared my speech to the class.
words.zh: 準備就是為某事做好準備。

words.d: To sail is to move a boat on the water.
words.e: I love to sail my boat on the lake.
words.zh: 航行就是在水上移動船隻。

words.d: When something is serious, it is bad or unsafe.
words.e: The accident was very serious.
words.zh: 當某件事嚴重時，表示它不好或危險。

words.d: To spend is to use time doing something or being somewhere.
words.e: I like to spend my free time fishing.
words.zh: 花費是指花時間做某事或待在某處。

words.d: When something is strange, it is not normal.
words.e: Joe had a strange look on his face after he saw what happened.
words.zh: 當某件事奇怪時，表示它不正常。

words.d: The truth is a fact or something that is right.
words.e: He was telling the truth about seeing a large green snake.
words.zh: 真相是事實或正確的事。

words.d: To wake is to not be sleeping anymore.
words.e: He is always full of energy when he wakes in the morning.
words.zh: 醒來是指不再睡覺。

words.d: If someone is alone, they are not with another person.
words.e: The boy wanted to be alone to think.
words.zh: 如果某人獨自一人，表示他沒有與他人在一起。

words.d: An apartment is a set of rooms in a building where people live.
words.e: She has a nice apartment in the city.
words.zh: 公寓是建築物中人們居住的一套房間。

words.d: An article is a story in a newspaper or magazine.
words.e: Did you read the article in the newspaper about the soccer game?
words.zh: 文章是報紙或雜誌中的一篇報導。

words.d: An artist is a person who paints, draws, or makes sculptures.
words.e: He went to Paris to become an artist.
words.zh: 藝術家是繪畫、素描或創作雕塑的人。

words.d: Someone’s attitude is the way they feel and think.
words.e: John has a bad attitude. He’s always angry.
words.zh: 一個人的態度是他們感受和思考的方式。

words.d: To compare means to say how two things are the same and different.
words.e: If you compare cats and dogs, you’ll see that they’re both good pets.
words.zh: 比較是指說出兩件事的相同與不同之處。

words.d: To judge something is to say if it is good or bad.
words.e: The boy was going to judge how his mother’s turkey tasted.
words.zh: 判斷某件事是指說出它是好是壞。

words.d: A magazine is a regular publication with news, stories, and articles.
words.e: She likes to read fashion magazines.
words.zh: 雜誌是定期出版、包含新聞、故事和文章的刊物。

words.d: A material is what is used to make something.
words.e: Brick is a good material for building houses.
words.zh: 材料是用來製造某物的東西。

words.d: A meal is a time when food is eaten like breakfast, lunch, or dinner.
words.e: Breakfast is my favorite meal because I enjoy breakfast foods.
words.zh: 一餐是用餐的時間，例如早餐、午餐或晚餐。

words.d: A method is the way to do something.
words.e: One method to remember things is to tie a string around your finger.
words.zh: 方法是做某事的方式。

words.d: A neighbor is a person who lives near you.
words.e: I like my neighbor because he’s very friendly.
words.zh: 鄰居是住在你附近的人。

words.d: If something is professional, it deals with work that uses special skills.
words.e: If you want to be a pilot, you must have professional training.
words.zh: 如果某事物是專業的，它涉及需要特殊技能的工作。

words.d: A profit is the extra money you make when you sell something.
words.e: I made a small profit from selling my old clothes.
words.zh: 利潤是你賣東西時賺到的額外金錢。

words.d: The quality of something is how good it is.
words.e: The quality of his car is very good.
words.zh: 某事物的品質是指它有多好。

words.d: A shape is a simple form like a square or circle.
words.e: The triangle is my favorite shape.
words.zh: 形狀是像正方形或圓形這樣的簡單形式。

words.d: A space is an empty area.
words.e: I don’t have much space for things in my small house.
words.zh: 空間是一個空曠的區域。

words.d: Stairs are the things that are used to go up in a building.
words.e: You can take the stairs to the second floor.
words.zh: 樓梯是用來在建築物中向上走的東西。

words.d: A symbol is a thing that stands for something else.
words.e: This symbol tells us that we cannot smoke in this area.
words.zh: 象徵是代表其他事物的東西。

words.d: If someone or something is thin, they are not fat.
words.e: The man was thin because he didn’t eat much.
words.zh: 如果某人或某物是瘦的，他們並不胖。

words.d: Blood is the red liquid in your body.
words.e: I cut my finger and got blood on my shirt.
words.zh: 血液是你體內的紅色液體。

words.d: To burn something is to set it on fire.
words.e: I burned some wood in the camp fire.
words.zh: 燃燒某物就是將它點火。

words.d: A cell is a small room where a person is locked in.
words.e: The jail cell was very small.
words.zh: 牢房是一個人被鎖在裡面的小房間。

words.d: To contain something is to have it inside.
words.e: The mailbox contained a letter.
words.zh: 包含某物就是讓它在裡面。

words.d: To be correct is to be right.
words.e: All of my answers on the test were correct.
words.zh: 正確就是對的。

words.d: A crop is food that a farmer grows.
words.e: Wheat is a crop that is made into bread.
words.zh: 農作物是農民種植的食物。

words.d: To demand something is to say strongly that you want it.
words.e: The workers demanded to be paid more money.
words.zh: 要求某事物，就是強烈表達想要它。

words.d: To be equal is to be the same.
words.e: Both students are equal in age.
words.zh: 相等就是相同。

words.d: To feed is to give food.
words.e: Mother feeds my baby brother everyday.
words.zh: 餵養就是給予食物。

words.d: A hole is an opening in something.
words.e: The man was going to jump into the hole in the ice.
words.zh: 洞是物體上的開口。

words.d: To increase something is to make it larger or more.
words.e: They’ve increased the price of gas by 15 cents!
words.zh: 增加某物，就是讓它變大或變多。

words.d: Long ago, a lord was a man in charge of a town.
words.e: The lord of the town was not kind.
words.zh: 很久以前，領主是掌管城鎮的男人。

words.d: To owe is to have to pay or give back something received from another.
words.e: I owed him twenty dollars, so I paid him back.
words.zh: 欠債就是必須償還或歸還從他人那裡得到的東西。

words.d: A position is the way something is placed.
words.e: How can you sit in that position?
words.zh: 位置是物體被放置的方式。

words.d: To raise something is to lift it up.
words.e: We had to work together to raise the last piece.
words.zh: 舉起某物就是將它抬高。

words.d: If a person is responsible, they do the right things.
words.e: I try to be responsible and save money.
words.zh: 如果一個人負責任，他們就會做正確的事。

words.d: A sight is something interesting to see.
words.e: I saw the pyramids of Egypt. What a sight!
words.zh: 景象是值得觀看的有趣事物。

words.d: A spot is a place where something happens.
words.e: The kitchen is a good spot to eat meals.
words.zh: 地點是某事發生的地方。

words.d: A structure is a building.
words.e: They just built a beautiful new structure downtown.
words.zh: 結構物就是建築物。

words.d: Whole means all of something.
words.e: I ate the whole pie. We don’t have any more.
words.zh: 整體指的是某物的全部。

words.d: A coach is a person who teaches sports.
words.e: My coach gets very excited during games.
words.zh: 教練是指導體育運動的人。

words.d: To control something is to make it do what you want.
words.e: To control the TV, just push the buttons.
words.zh: 控制某事物就是讓它按照你的意願運作。

words.d: A description of someone or something says what they are like.
words.e: I gave a description of the man with the gun and hat to the police.
words.zh: 對某人或某事物的描述，說明他們是什麼樣子。

words.d: If something is direct, it goes straight between two places.
words.e: The green path is a direct route to my house.
words.zh: 如果某事物是直接的，它就在兩個地點之間直線行進。

words.d: An exam is a test.
words.e: I did some practice questions for the math exam on the board.
words.zh: 考試就是測驗。

words.d: An example of something is a thing that is typical of it.
words.e: Cola is an example of a soft drink.
words.zh: 某事物的例子，就是能代表該事物的典型。

words.d: A limit is the largest or smallest amount of something that you allow.
words.e: My mother put a limit on how much I could use the phone.
words.zh: 限制是你允許某事物的最大或最小數量。

words.d: If something is local, it is nearby.
words.e: The local market in my neighborhood sells all the food we need.
words.zh: 如果某事物是當地的，它就在附近。

words.d: Magical describes a quality that makes someone or something special.
words.e: The fireworks made the night sky look so magical.
words.zh: 神奇的形容一種特質，使某人或某事物與眾不同。

words.d: Mail is letters and other things sent to people.
words.e: I get a lot of mail because I have friends all over the world.
words.zh: 郵件是寄送給人們的信件及其他物品。

words.d: A novel is a book that tells a story.
words.e: He wrote a great novel about ancient China.
words.zh: 小說是講述故事的書。

words.d: An outline is the plan for a story or essay.
words.e: Before I wrote my essay, I made an outline.
words.zh: 大綱是故事或文章的計畫。

words.d: A poet is a person who writes poems.
words.e: William Shakespeare was one of the greatest poets.
words.zh: 詩人是創作詩歌的人。

words.d: To print something is to put it onto paper.
words.e: Make sure that you print your name clearly.
words.zh: 印刷是將內容印在紙上。

words.d: A scene is one part of a book or movie.
words.e: A movie is made up of many short pieces or scenes.
words.zh: 場景是書籍或電影中的一個部分。

words.d: A sheet is a thin flat piece of paper.
words.e: I only needed a single sheet of paper to do my homework.
words.zh: 紙張是指一片薄而平坦的紙。

words.d: If someone or something is silly, they show a lack of thought.
words.e: I made a silly mistake of dropping mom’s vase.
words.zh: 若某人或某事很傻，表示缺乏思考。

words.d: A store is a place where you can buy things.
words.e: I picked up a few things at the grocery store.
words.zh: 商店是你可以購買物品的地方。

words.d: To suffer is to feel pain.
words.e: Her headache made her suffer all day.
words.zh: 受苦就是感受到疼痛。

words.d: Technology is new things made by using science.
words.e: He loves technology such as laptop computers.
words.zh: 科技是利用科學製造的新事物。

words.d: To go across something is to go to the other side of it.
words.e: He walked across the board to the other side.
words.zh: 穿越某物是指到達它的另一邊。

words.d: To breathe means to let air go in and out of your body.
words.e: We need strong healthy lungs to help us breathe well.
words.zh: 呼吸是指讓空氣進出你的身體。

words.d: A characteristic is something that shows what a person or a thing is like.
words.e: One characteristic of tigers is their black stripes.
words.zh: 特徵是顯示一個人或事物本質的東西。

words.d: To consume something means to eat or drink it.
words.e: Jack consumed a whole plate of spaghetti.
words.zh: 消耗某物是指食用或飲用它。

words.d: To excite someone means to make them happy and interested.
words.e: I heard about the school dance on Friday. This excited me.
words.zh: 讓某人興奮是指使他們感到快樂與興趣。

words.d: If something is extreme, it is in a large amount or degree.
words.e: His workout was so extreme that he was sweating heavily.
words.zh: 若某事很極端，表示其數量或程度很大。

words.d: Fear is the feeling of being afraid.
words.e: I have a great fear of skateboarding.
words.zh: 恐懼是感到害怕的情緒。

words.d: If you are fortunate, you are lucky.
words.e: I was fortunate to get a seat.
words.zh: 如果你很幸運，你就是有福氣的。

words.d: If someone happens to do something, they do it by chance.
words.e: I happened to meet some new friends at school today.
words.zh: 若某人碰巧做某事，表示他們偶然為之。

words.d: The length of something is how long it is from one end to the other.
words.e: The length of the floor is three meters.
words.zh: 某物的長度是指從一端到另一端的距離。

words.d: A mistake is something you do wrong.
words.e: My boss always yells at me when I make a mistake.
words.zh: 錯誤是指你做錯的事情。

words.d: To observe something is to watch it.
words.e: Brian observed the sun rising over the mountains.
words.zh: 觀察某物就是仔細觀看它。

words.d: An opportunity is a chance to do something.
words.e: I had an opportunity to take pictures in the jungle.
words.zh: 機會是指做某事的契機。

words.d: A prize is something of value that is given to the winner.
words.e: I won a prize for getting the best grade on my science test.
words.zh: 獎品是頒發給勝利者的有價值之物。

words.d: A race is a contest to see who is the fastest.
words.e: Paul is a fast runner, so he easily won the race.
words.zh: 比賽是為了爭奪誰最快而進行的競賽。

words.d: To realize is to suddenly understand.
words.e: After I left my house, I realized that I left the light on.
words.zh: 領悟是指突然明白某事。

words.d: To respond is to give an answer to what someone else said.
words.e: When the teacher asked the question, we all responded.
words.zh: 回應是指對他人所說的話給出答覆。

words.d: A risk is a chance of something bad happening.
words.e: I took a risk and climbed the snowy mountain.
words.zh: 風險是指發生壞事的可能性。

words.d: To wonder is to ask yourself questions or have a need to know.
words.e: The young mother wondered if she’d have a boy or a girl.
words.zh: 好奇是指向自己提問或產生求知的需求。

words.d: Yet is used to say something has not happened up to now.
words.e: We can’t go out yet; we’re still eating.
words.zh: 「尚未」用於表示某事到目前為止還沒有發生。

words.d: An academy is a special type of school.
words.e: There are many courses taught at the academy that I go to.
words.zh: 學院是一種特殊類型的學校。

words.d: If something is ancient, it is very old.
words.e: I want to see the ancient buildings in Rome.
words.zh: 如果某物是古老的，表示它非常老舊。

words.d: A board is a flat piece of wood.
words.e: The sign was made of a few wooden boards.
words.zh: 木板是一塊扁平的木頭。

words.d: A century is one hundred years.
words.e: Our company is celebrating a century of business in London.
words.zh: 一個世紀是一百年。

words.d: A clue is a fact or object that helps solve a mystery or crime.
words.e: The detective found some clues on the sidewalk.
words.zh: 線索是有助於解開謎團或罪案的真相或物品。

words.d: A concert is an event where you listen to people play music.
words.e: I enjoyed the concert last night. The band was very good.
words.zh: 音樂會是聆聽人們演奏音樂的活動。

words.d: A county is the largest political division of a state in the US.
words.e: He wanted to represent the citizens of his county.
words.zh: 郡是美國州內最大的政治劃分單位。

words.d: A dictionary is a book that tells you what words mean.
words.e: I use the dictionary to learn new words.
words.zh: 字典是解釋詞語意義的書。

words.d: To exist is to be real.
words.e: Do you really think that unicorns ever existed?
words.zh: 存在就是真實的。

words.d: Flat describes something that is level and smooth with no curved parts.
words.e: My parents bought a new flat screen TV over the weekend.
words.zh: 平坦形容某物水平光滑，沒有彎曲部分。

words.d: A gentleman is a nice man.
words.e: My grandfather is a kind and helpful gentleman.
words.zh: 紳士是友善的男性。

words.d: Hidden means to be not easily noticed or too hard to find.
words.e: The hidden camera recorded everything in the parking lot.
words.zh: 隱藏指不易察覺或難以找到。

words.d: Maybe is used to show that something is possible or may be true.
words.e: If I focus hard enough, maybe I can come up with the right answer.
words.zh: 或許用來表示某事可能發生或屬實。

words.d: An officer is a leader in the army.
words.e: The soldiers followed the orders of the officer.
words.zh: 軍官是軍隊中的領導者。

words.d: If something is original, it is the first one of that thing.
words.e: This is the original painting of the Mona Lisa.
words.zh: 如果某物是原創的，它就是該事物的第一個。

words.d: To pound something is to hit it many times with a lot of force.
words.e: He pounded the nail with the hammer.
words.zh: 猛擊某物是用大力反覆擊打它。

words.d: A process is the steps to take to do something.
words.e: Making a cake is a long process.
words.zh: 過程是完成某事所需的步驟。

words.d: To publish a book is to get it printed and ready to sell.
words.e: That company publishes daily newspapers.
words.zh: 出版一本書是將其印刷並準備銷售。

words.d: A theater is a building where you watch plays, shows, and movies.
words.e: We went to the theater to see a play.
words.zh: 劇院是觀看戲劇、表演和電影的建築。

words.d: Wealth is a large amount of money.
words.e: One of the most important things to some people is wealth.
words.zh: 財富是大量的金錢。

words.d: To appreciate something is to understand its good qualities.
words.e: I can appreciate the lovely scenery.
words.zh: 欣賞某物即是理解其優點。

words.d: If something is available, it means you can get it.
words.e: There were many seats available in the room.
words.zh: 若某物可取得，代表你能獲得它。

words.d: To beat someone means to do better than they do.
words.e: I managed to beat everyone in the race.
words.zh: 擊敗某人意味著表現比他們更好。

words.d: If something is bright, it shows a lot of light.
words.e: The bright light from the explosion hurt my eyes.
words.zh: 若某物明亮，則表示它發出大量光線。

words.d: To celebrate is to do something to show that an event is special.
words.e: We all celebrated when we heard the great news.
words.zh: 慶祝是為了彰顯某事件具有特殊意義。

words.d: To determine means to choose or make a decision.
words.e: He tried to determine which one to eat first.
words.zh: 決定意指選擇或做出決策。

words.d: To disappear means to go away or not be seen.
words.e: The top of the building is disappearing in the clouds.
words.zh: 消失意味著離去或不再被看見。

words.d: If you talk about something else, you talk about something different.
words.e: I wanted a bike for my birthday, but I got something else.
words.zh: 若你談論其他事物，便是討論不同的東西。

words.d: Fair describes treating someone in a way that is reasonable or right.
words.e: He sold me his car for a fair price.
words.zh: 公平描述以合理或正當的方式對待他人。

words.d: To flow is to move easily and continuously in one direction.
words.e: The water flowed over the rocks and into the lake.
words.zh: 流動是指順暢且持續地朝單一方向移動。

words.d: If you move forward, you move in the direction in front of you.
words.e: When he saw his mother, the baby crawled forward to her.
words.zh: 若你向前移動，便是朝你面前的方向前進。

words.d: A hill is a round area of land. It is higher than the land around it.
words.e: The sun was rising above the green hills.
words.zh: 山丘是一片圓形隆起的地面，高於周圍土地。

words.d: A level is a point on a scale that measures something.
words.e: Please check the level of the temperature.
words.zh: 等級是衡量某事物的尺度上的位置。

words.d: If someone or something is lone, they are the only one of that kind.
words.e: A lone man walked along the street.
words.zh: 若某人或某物孤獨，他們是同類中唯一的存在。

words.d: A puddle is a pool of liquid on the ground.
words.e: When the ice melted, it formed a puddle.
words.zh: 水坑是地面上的液體積聚處。

words.d: A response is the answer to a question.
words.e: He asked if I was sad. My response was “No.”
words.zh: 回應是對問題的回答。

words.d: A season is a time of the year: spring, summer, fall or winter.
words.e: Fall is a warm season, while winter is very cold.
words.zh: 季節是一年中的時節：春、夏、秋或冬。

words.d: A solution is a way to solve a problem.
words.e: There are many problems. We need solutions!
words.zh: 解決方案是解決問題的方法。

words.d: To waste means to carelessly use something all up.
words.e: Turn off the water so you don’t waste it.
words.zh: 浪費是指不小心用盡某物。

words.d: You use whether when you must choose between two things.
words.e: I could not decide whether to go left or right.
words.zh: 當你必須在兩件事之間做選擇時，使用「whether」。

words.d: To argue is to angrily speak to someone because you do not agree.
words.e: We argued about where to go for dinner.
words.zh: 爭論是因為意見不合而憤怒地與人說話。

words.d: To communicate is to give information by talking, writing, etc.
words.e: I communicated with Paul about his new ideas.
words.zh: 溝通是透過說話、書寫等方式傳遞資訊。

words.d: A crowd is a large group of people.
words.e: A large crowd had gathered on the street to see the accident.
words.zh: 人群是一大群人。

words.d: To depend on someone or something is to need them.
words.e: Children depend on their parents for many things.
words.zh: 依賴某人或某物是指需要他們。

words.d: A dish is a plate.
words.e: She put a clean white dish on the table.
words.zh: 盤子是一種碟子。

words.d: If something is empty, it does not have anything in it.
words.e: My gas tank was almost empty, so I couldn’t drive my car very long.
words.zh: 如果某物是空的，裡面沒有任何東西。

words.d: If something is exact, it is just the right amount.
words.e: Please use the exact amount of sugar for the cake.
words.zh: 如果某物是精確的，它剛好是正確的量。

words.d: If something is fresh, it is new.
words.e: I just bought these oranges. They are very fresh.
words.zh: 如果某物是新鮮的，它就是新的。

words.d: To gather is to collect several things usually from different places.
words.e: Sam gathered some flowers for his mother.
words.zh: 收集是指將幾樣東西（通常來自不同地方）集合起來。

words.d: To indicate means to show, point or make something clear.
words.e: He pointed to his eyes to indicate where he had hurt himself.
words.zh: 指示意味著顯示、指向或使某事清楚。

words.d: An item is a thing that you buy or sell.
words.e: I have many items for school in my bag.
words.zh: 物品是指你買賣的東西。

words.d: To offer is to present someone with something.
words.e: He offered me the keys to his car.
words.zh: 提供是指將某物呈現給某人。

words.d: The price of something is how much it costs.
words.e: What is the price of this item?
words.zh: 某物的價格是指它值多少錢。

words.d: A product is something that is made.
words.e: My sister has many beauty products in her room.
words.zh: 產品是指被製造出來的東西。

words.d: Property is something that someone owns.
words.e: The house is now my property.
words.zh: 財產是指某人擁有的東西。

words.d: To purchase something is to buy it.
words.e: I recently purchased a new car.
words.zh: 購買某物是指買下它。

words.d: To recommend something is to say that someone should do it.
words.e: My doctor recommended that I get some exercise.
words.zh: 推薦某物是指建議某人應該去做它。

words.d: To select something is to choose it.
words.e: I hope that I selected the right answers on the test.
words.zh: 選擇某物是指挑選它。

words.d: A tool is something that helps you do a task.
words.e: We used a tool to fix the window.
words.zh: 工具是指幫助你完成任務的東西。

words.d: To treat is to act in a certain way toward someone.
words.e: The cats treated the mouse very nicely.
words.zh: 對待是指以某種方式對待某人。

words.d: If someone or something is alive, they are not dead.
words.e: My grandparents are still alive even though they are over 90.
words.zh: 如果某人或某物活著，他們就不是死的。

words.d: A bone is a hard part of the body.
words.e: I brought home a nice bone for my dog.
words.zh: 骨頭是身體的堅硬部分。

words.d: To bother is to make the effort to do something.
words.e: No one bothered to wash the dishes today.
words.zh: 費心是指付出努力去做某事。

words.d: A captain is the person who leads a ship or airplane.
words.e: The captain sailed his ship to Australia.
words.zh: 船長是指領導船隻或飛機的人。

words.d: The conclusion of something is the final part of it.
words.e: At the conclusion of the race, the spectators cheered for the winner.
words.zh: 某物的結論是指它的最後部分。

words.d: Doubt is a feeling of not being sure.
words.e: I have doubt that the story is true.
words.zh: 懷疑是一種不確定的感覺。

words.d: To explore is to look for new places.
words.e: He wants to explore the world and see new things.
words.zh: 探索就是尋找新的地方。

words.d: If something is foreign, it is from a different country.
words.e: Mexican food is a popular foreign food.
words.zh: 如果某物是外國的，它來自不同的國家。

words.d: If you are glad, you are happy.
words.e: I am glad you came to my party.
words.zh: 如果你感到高興，你就是快樂的。

words.d: However means despite or not being influenced by something.
words.e: She is a great cook. However, she never had professional lessons.
words.zh: 「然而」表示儘管某事，或不受某事影響。

words.d: Injustice is a lack of fairness or justice.
words.e: Putting an innocent person in jail is an act of injustice.
words.zh: 不公正是缺乏公平或正義。

words.d: If something is international, it involves more than one country.
words.e: The United Nations is a powerful international organization.
words.zh: 如果某物是國際的，它涉及多個國家。

words.d: A lawyer works with the law and represents people in court.
words.e: The lawyer left the courthouse after the judge made her decision.
words.zh: 律師處理法律事務，並在法庭上代表當事人。

words.d: To mention something is to talk about it.
words.e: The doctors mentioned the problems that the patient was having.
words.zh: 提到某事物就是談論它。

words.d: A policy is a rule.
words.e: He told us that his policy was to put customers first.
words.zh: 政策是一種規則。

words.d: If something is social, it is about many people in a community.
words.e: People should come together and fix the world’s social problems.
words.zh: 如果某物是社會性的，它關乎社區中的許多人。

words.d: A speech is something said to a group of people.
words.e: She gave a speech to the class.
words.zh: 演講是對一群人說的話。

words.d: A staff is a group of people working together in a company.
words.e: My dad has a staff of four people to help him at the office.
words.zh: 員工是公司中一起工作的一群人。

words.d: If you go toward something, you go closer to it.
words.e: Santa walked toward my house with a special tree.
words.zh: 如果你朝某物前進，你就更靠近它。

words.d: Wood is the thing that trees are made of.
words.e: I put the pieces of wood in a pile.
words.zh: 木頭是構成樹木的物質。

words.d: To achieve something is to successfully do it after trying hard.
words.e: I was happy that I could achieve my goal.
words.zh: 達成某事是指在努力嘗試後成功做到。

words.d: To advise someone is to tell them what to do.
words.e: My mother often advises people about their money.
words.zh: 建議某人是指告訴他們該做什麼。

words.d: If something happens already, it happens before a certain time.
words.e: It is already time for the movie to start. Let’s go in.
words.zh: 如果某事已經發生，則是指在某個時間之前發生。

words.d: If something is basic, it is very simple or easy.
words.e: I learned some basic English skills in school today.
words.zh: 如果某事是基本的，則表示它非常簡單或容易。

words.d: A bit is a small amount of something.
words.e: I ate a bit of chocolate before I went to bed.
words.zh: 一點是指某物的少量。

words.d: To consider something means to think about it.
words.e: Pete didn’t like his job. He considered getting a new one.
words.zh: 考慮某事是指思考它。

words.d: To destroy means to damage something so badly that it cannot be used.
words.e: The glass was destroyed.
words.zh: 毀壞是指嚴重損壞某物，使其無法使用。

words.d: To entertain someone is to do something that they enjoy.
words.e: The clown entertained the kids at the party.
words.zh: 娛樂某人是指做他們喜歡的事情。

words.d: If something is extra, it is more than what is needed.
words.e: The squirrel had extra nuts for the winter.
words.zh: 如果某事是多餘的，則表示超過所需。

words.d: A goal is something you work toward.
words.e: Her goal was to become a doctor.
words.zh: 目標是你努力追求的對象。

words.d: To lie is to say or write something untrue to deceive someone.
words.e: Whenever Pinocchio lied to his father, his nose grew.
words.zh: 說謊是指說或寫不真實的話來欺騙某人。

words.d: Meat is food made of animals.
words.e: This piece of meat I’m eating tastes very good.
words.zh: 肉類是由動物製成的食物。

words.d: An opinion is a thought about a person or a thing.
words.e: Meg told me her opinion of my story. She said it was not funny.
words.zh: 意見是對某人或某事的想法。

words.d: If something is real, it actually exists.
words.e: The handbag has a stamp on it, so it’s real.
words.zh: 如果某事是真實的，則它確實存在。

words.d: To reflect is when a surface sends back light, heat, sound or an image.
words.e: Her face was reflected on the smooth glass.
words.zh: 反射是指表面將光、熱、聲音或影像返回。

words.d: To regard someone or something is to think of them in a certain way.
words.e: The boy regarded the girl as a good friend.
words.zh: 看待某人或某事，就是以某種方式去理解他們。

words.d: To serve someone is to give them food or drinks.
words.e: He served us our drinks quickly.
words.zh: 招待某人，就是為他們提供食物或飲料。

words.d: A vegetable is a plant used as food.
words.e: Carrots are my favorite vegetable.
words.zh: 蔬菜是一種用作食物的植物。

words.d: A war is a big fight between two groups of people.
words.e: Many young men died in the war.
words.zh: 戰爭是兩群人之間的大規模衝突。

words.d: If something is worth an amount of money, it costs that amount.
words.e: Our house is worth a lot of money.
words.zh: 如果某物價值一定金額，就代表它要價那麼多。

words.d: To appear is to seem.
words.e: She appeared to be sad. She was crying.
words.zh: 出現，就是看起來像某種樣子。

words.d: The base is the bottom of something.
words.e: The base of the table has three legs.
words.zh: 基底是某物的底部。

words.d: The brain is the organ in your head that lets you think.
words.e: You must use your brain to solve the problem.
words.zh: 大腦是你頭部裡讓你思考的器官。

words.d: A career is a job that you do for a large part of your life.
words.e: He was in the hospitality business for most of his career.
words.zh: 職業是你人生大部分時間從事的行業。

words.d: A clerk is a type of worker. Clerks in a store help customers.
words.e: The clerk added up her bill for the groceries.
words.zh: 店員是一種工作者。商店裡的店員會協助顧客。

words.d: Effort is hard work or an attempt to do something.
words.e: He always puts a lot of effort into his studies.
words.zh: 努力是辛勤的工作，或是嘗試做某件事。

words.d: To enter a place is to go into it.
words.e: Two guards greeted me as I entered the front door.
words.zh: 進入某個地方，就是走進去。

words.d: When something is excellent, it is very good.
words.e: I got an excellent score on my school test.
words.zh: 當某事物極佳時，代表它非常好。

words.d: A hero is a brave person who does things to help others.
words.e: To children, the man in the blue and red costume was a real hero.
words.zh: 英雄是勇敢的人，會做幫助他人的事。

words.d: To hurry is to do something quickly.
words.e: I hurried home on my bike.
words.zh: 匆忙，就是快速做某件事。

words.d: To inform someone is to tell them about something.
words.e: I called and informed her about my idea.
words.zh: 告知某人就是告訴他們某件事。

words.d: Later means after the present, expected, or usual time.
words.e: She missed the train, so she’ll arrive a little later than expected.
words.zh: 稍後是指在現在、預期或通常時間之後。

words.d: To leave means to go away from someone or something.
words.e: He packed his bag and was ready to leave for home.
words.zh: 離開是指遠離某人或某物。

words.d: To locate something is to find it.
words.e: I could not locate my keys in the house.
words.zh: 找到某物就是發現它。

words.d: A nurse is a person who helps sick people in the hospital.
words.e: A nurse helped me get better.
words.zh: 護士是在醫院幫助病人的人。

words.d: An operation is when a doctor replaces or removes something in the body.
words.e: The operation on my arm was a success.
words.zh: 手術是指醫生更換或移除體內某物的過程。

words.d: Pain is the feeling that you have when you are hurt.
words.e: His head was full of pain.
words.zh: 疼痛是你受傷時的感覺。

words.d: To refuse something is to say “no” to it.
words.e: The dog refused to play with the cat.
words.zh: 拒絕某物就是對它說「不」。

words.d: Though is used when the second idea makes the first seem surprising.
words.e: Though he was overweight, he liked to be active.
words.zh: 雖然用於第二個想法讓第一個想法顯得令人驚訝時。

words.d: If something is various, there are many types of it.
words.e: She owned shoes of various styles.
words.zh: 如果某物是多樣的，表示它有很多種類。

words.d: Actual means that something is real or true.
words.e: This is the actual sword that the King owned, not a fake one.
words.zh: 實際的意思是某物是真實或正確的。

words.d: To amaze someone is to surprise them very much.
words.e: The news in the paper amazed Jack.
words.zh: 讓某人驚奇就是讓他們非常驚訝。

words.d: A charge is the price to pay for something.
words.e: The charge for the shirts was $15.00.
words.zh: 費用是為某物支付的價格。

words.d: To comfort someone means to make them feel better.
words.e: I wanted to comfort my friend after I heard the bad news.
words.zh: 安慰某人意味著讓他們感覺更好。

words.d: To contact someone is to speak or write to them.
words.e: I contacted Sue about my party.
words.zh: 聯繫某人就是與他們說話或寫信。

words.d: A customer is a person who buys something at a store.
words.e: The customer put a few items in a bag.
words.zh: 顧客是指在商店購買商品的人。

words.d: To deliver something is to take it from one place to another.
words.e: The man delivered Chinese food to my house.
words.zh: 運送是指將物品從一個地方送到另一個地方。

words.d: To earn means to get money for the work you do.
words.e: He earns his living as a chef in a great restaurant.
words.zh: 賺取是指因工作而獲得金錢。

words.d: A gate is a type of door. Gates are usually made of metal or wood.
words.e: We want to put up a wooden gate around our house.
words.zh: 大門是一種門，通常由金屬或木材製成。

words.d: To include something means to have it as part of a group.
words.e: Does this meal include a soft drink?
words.zh: 包含某物是指將其視為群體的一部分。

words.d: To manage something means to control or be in charge of it.
words.e: I had to manage the meeting myself.
words.zh: 管理某物是指控制或負責該事物。

words.d: A mystery is something that is difficult to understand or explain.
words.e: The path on the map was a complete mystery to me.
words.zh: 謎團是指難以理解或解釋的事物。

words.d: To occur means to happen.
words.e: When did the thunderstorm occur?
words.zh: 發生是指事件出現。

words.d: If A is the opposite of B, A is completely different from B.
words.e: The opposite of black is white.
words.zh: 如果A是B的相反，則A與B完全不同。

words.d: A plate is a flat round thing that you put food on.
words.e: I put my plate down so I could put some food on it.
words.zh: 盤子是一種扁平圓形、用來盛放食物的器具。

words.d: To receive something is to get it.
words.e: I received a present on my birthday.
words.zh: 接收某物是指獲得它。

words.d: A reward is something given in exchange for good behavior or work.
words.e: He was given a reward for his excellent performance.
words.zh: 獎勵是因良好行為或工作而給予的東西。

words.d: To set something is to put it somewhere.
words.e: Please set the dice down on the table.
words.zh: 放置某物是指將其放在某個地方。

words.d: To steal is to take something that is not yours.
words.e: The men tried to steal money from the bank.
words.zh: 偷竊是指拿走不屬於你的東西。

words.d: A thief is someone who quietly takes things that do not belong to them.
words.e: A thief broke into our home and took my mother's jewelry.
words.zh: 小偷是指悄悄拿走不屬於他們物品的人。

words.d: To advance is to go forward.
words.e: He advanced up the ladder slowly.
words.zh: 前進即是向前行。

words.d: An athlete is a person who plays sports.
words.e: Some athletes can play many sports very well.
words.zh: 運動員是從事體育運動的人。

words.d: If something is average, it is at a normal level.
words.e: I’m not rich or poor; I’m average.
words.zh: 若某事物為平均，即處於正常水準。

words.d: Your behavior is the way you act.
words.e: Their behavior was good this semester. They didn’t cause trouble.
words.zh: 行為是指你表現出來的方式。

words.d: Behind means to be at the back of something.
words.e: The little girl was hiding behind a tree.
words.zh: 後方意指位於某物的背後。

words.d: A course is a class in school.
words.e: I took a P.E. course in school this year.
words.zh: 課程是學校中的一堂課。

words.d: To lower something is to make it go down.
words.e: The chart shows how his production has lowered over the year.
words.zh: 降低某物即是使其下降。

words.d: To match is to be the same or similar.
words.e: The two shoes matched. They looked the same.
words.zh: 匹配是指相同或相似。

words.d: A member is a person who is part of a group.
words.e: Julie is the newest member of our team.
words.zh: 成員是屬於某群體的人。

words.d: If something is mental, it has to do with your mind.
words.e: I made a mental picture of the room.
words.zh: 若某事物屬於心智，即與你的思維有關。

words.d: A passenger is a person who rides in a car, train, or airplane.
words.e: One passenger was standing in the subway train.
words.zh: 乘客是乘坐汽車、火車或飛機的人。

words.d: Your personality is what you are like and how you behave.
words.e: John has a bad personality.
words.zh: 性格是指你的為人及行為方式。

words.d: A poem is a short kind of writing.
words.e: William Shakespeare wrote many poems.
words.zh: 詩歌是一種簡短的寫作形式。

words.d: A pole is a long thin stick made of wood or metal that supports things.
words.e: The flag was hanging from the flag pole.
words.zh: 柱子是支撐物品的細長木棍或金屬棒。

words.d: To remove something is to take it away.
words.e: I removed the nail from the board.
words.zh: 移除某物即是將其拿走。

words.d: Safety means to be the condition of being safe and free from danger.
words.e: For his own safety, he was placed in a car seat.
words.zh: 安全是指處於無危險且受到保護的狀態。

words.d: To shoot is to fire something like a bullet at someone or something.
words.e: The hunter raised his gun to shoot at the target.
words.zh: 射擊是指向某人或某物發射子彈等物體。

words.d: To sound means to make a noise.
words.e: The alarm clock sounded and woke us all up.
words.zh: 發出聲音是指製造聲響。

words.d: To swim is to move through water.
words.e: I love to swim in the ocean.
words.zh: 游泳是指在水中移動。

words.e: Mom cleaned the spider webs out of the garage.
words.zh: 蜘蛛網；網絡

words.d: A block is a solid piece of wood, stone or ice.
words.e: I saw a block of ice on the floor.
words.zh: 方塊是一塊實心的木頭、石頭或冰塊。

words.d: To cheer is to give a loud shout of approval or encouragement.
words.e: The crowd all cheered when the home team won.
words.zh: 歡呼是指大聲表達贊同或鼓勵。

words.d: If something is complex, it has many small parts. It is hard to understand.
words.e: A jig-saw puzzle can be complex because it has so many pieces.
words.zh: 如果某事物複雜，代表它由許多小部分組成，難以理解。

words.d: A critic is someone who give their opinions about movies, books, plays.
words.e: The wine critic tasted the wine so he could give his opinion.
words.zh: 評論家是指對電影、書籍、戲劇發表意見的人。

words.d: An event is something that happens, especially something important.
words.e: Finishing high school was a major event in his life.
words.zh: 事件是指發生的事情，尤其指重要的事。

words.d: To exercise is to run or play sports so that you can be healthy.
words.e: You should exercise every day.
words.zh: 運動是指跑步或從事體育活動以保持健康。

words.d: If something fits, it is small enough or the right size to go there.
words.e: The colorful eggs fit into the box.
words.zh: 如果某物合適，代表它夠小或尺寸剛好能放入某處。

words.d: Friendship is the relationship between people who are friends.
words.e: Michael and Lisa have a very strong friendship with each other.
words.zh: 友誼是指朋友之間的關係。

words.d: A guide is someone who shows you where to go.
words.e: We followed a guide at the park.
words.zh: 導遊是指帶領你去某處的人。

words.d: If there is a lack of something, there is not enough of it.
words.e: His only problem is a lack of money.
words.zh: 如果缺乏某物，代表數量不足。

words.d: A passage is a long area with walls that goes from one place to another.
words.e: The long passage led us to the other side of the field.
words.zh: 通道是指有牆壁的長形區域，連接兩個地方。

words.d: To perform is to do something in front of people who watch.
words.e: He will perform a song for the class.
words.zh: 表演是指在眾人面前進行某項活動。

words.d: Pressure is what you apply to make someone do something.
words.e: They put pressure on him to change his mind.
words.zh: 壓力是用來促使某人做某事的手段。

words.d: If something is probable, it is likely to happen.
words.e: It is probable that you will get a good grade if you study for the test.
words.zh: 若某事是可能的，則表示它很可能發生。

words.d: If something is public, it is meant for everyone to use.
words.e: I went to the public park to play with my friends.
words.zh: 若某事是公開的，則表示它供所有人使用。

words.d: To strike someone or something is to hit them.
words.e: She struck the other girl in the face.
words.zh: 擊打某人或某物就是對其進行撞擊。

words.d: To support something is to like it and help it be successful.
words.e: Everyone at work supports the new plan.
words.zh: 支持某事是指喜歡它並幫助它成功。

words.d: A task is work that someone has to do.
words.e: Who has the task of building the brick wall?
words.zh: 任務是某人必須完成的工作。

words.d: A term is a word for something.
words.e: I often use the term “oops” when I make a mistake.
words.zh: 術語是指某事物的名稱。

words.d: To unite is to get together to do something.
words.e: If we unite, we can finish our project faster.
words.zh: 團結是指聚集在一起共同行動。

words.d: To associate means to connect something with a person or thing.
words.e: Most people associate birthday parties with having fun.
words.zh: 聯想是指將某事物與人或物聯繫起來。

words.d: The environment is the place where people work or live.
words.e: Keeping our environment clean is important to our health.
words.zh: 環境是指人們工作或生活的地方。

words.d: A factory is a building where things are made or put together.
words.e: We have only one factory in our town.
words.zh: 工廠是用來製造或組裝物品的建築物。

words.d: A feature is an important part of something.
words.e: The cell phone has many features.
words.zh: 特徵是某事物的重要組成部分。

words.d: An instance is an example of something.
words.e: I have never experienced an instance of hate. Have you?
words.zh: 實例是某事物的例子。

words.d: To involve means to be actively taking part in something.
words.e: The whole family was involved in playing the game.
words.zh: 參與意味著積極投入某件事。

words.d: Medicine is something you take to feel better or treat an illness.
words.e: The doctor gave me medicine for my cold.
words.zh: 藥物是你為了改善身體或治療疾病而服用的東西。

words.d: A mix is different things put together.
words.e: The green mix we made in science class spilled onto the table.
words.zh: 混合物是將不同東西放在一起。

words.d: To organize is to plan or get ready for an event.
words.e: Make a list to help you organize the things you need.
words.zh: 組織就是為某個活動做計劃或準備。

words.d: A period is an amount of time when something happens.
words.e: In one period in Europe, there were many knights.
words.zh: 時期是某事發生的一段時間。

words.d: If people populate an area, they live there.
words.e: Billions of people populate the Earth.
words.zh: 如果人們聚居在某個區域，他們就住在那裡。

words.d: To produce something is to make or grow it.
words.e: This tree produces apples every year.
words.zh: 生產某物就是製造或種植它。

words.d: A range is a number or a set of similar things.
words.e: I saw a range of cars to choose from.
words.zh: 範圍是一組相似的數字或事物。

words.d: To recognize something is to know it because you have seen it before.
words.e: I recognized an old friend from many years ago.
words.zh: 認出某物是因為你之前見過它而知道它。

words.d: If something is regular, it happens often and in equal amounts of time.
words.e: Our regular lunch time is around noon.
words.zh: 如果某件事是規律的，它經常發生且間隔時間相等。

words.d: A sign is a notice giving information, directions, a warning, etc.
words.e: The sign indicated that today would be a bad day.
words.zh: 標誌是提供資訊、方向、警告等的告示。

words.d: A tip is a pointed end of something.
words.e: The tip of his pen was very sharp.
words.zh: 尖端是某物的尖頭部分。

words.d: A tradition is something people have been doing for a long time.
words.e: Marriage is a tradition all over the world.
words.zh: 傳統是人們長期以來一直在做的事情。

words.d: Trash is waste material or unwanted or worthless things.
words.e: Please take out the trash; it smells bad.
words.zh: 垃圾是廢棄物或不需要、無價值的東西。

words.d: If something is wide, it is large from side to side.
words.e: The door was as wide as my arms.
words.zh: 如果某物很寬，它的左右距離很大。

words.d: Advice is an opinion about what to do.
words.e: I don’t know how to study for my exams. Can you give me some advice?
words.zh: 建議是關於該做什麼的意見。

words.d: Along means to move from one part of a road, river, etc. to another.
words.e: Walk along this tunnel for ten minutes, and you’ll see a door on the left.
words.zh: 沿著意指從道路、河流等的一部分移動到另一部分。

words.d: Attention is the notice, thought, or consideration of someone.
words.e: His work got the attention of two of his co-workers.
words.zh: 注意是指對某人或某事的關注、思考或考量。

words.d: To attract means to make a person or thing come closer or be interested.
words.e: The magnet attracted the metal.
words.zh: 吸引意指使某人或某物靠近或產生興趣。

words.d: To climb means to use your hands and feet to go up on something.
words.e: The girls climbed to the top of the mountain.
words.zh: 攀爬意指用手腳爬上某物。

words.d: To drop is to fall or allow something to fall.
words.e: A small amount of water dropped from the bottle.
words.zh: 掉落是指墜落或讓某物落下。

words.d: If something is final, it is the last part.
words.e: In the final part of the film, the man and the woman got married.
words.zh: 若某事是最終的，則表示它是最後一部分。

words.d: Further is used to say something is from a distance or time.
words.e: The escalator is further along than I thought.
words.zh: 進一步用於表示某事物來自距離或時間上的更遠。

words.d: To imply something is to suggest it without saying it.
words.e: The man implied that he wanted the job, but he didn’t say so.
words.zh: 暗示某事是指不直接說出而間接表達。

words.d: To maintain means to make something stay the same.
words.e: The balls maintain constant movement.
words.zh: 維持意指使某事物保持不變。

words.d: You use neither to connect two negative statements.
words.e: Neither the pass on the left nor the pass on the right will lead us home.
words.zh: 你用「既不」來連接兩個否定陳述。

words.d: Otherwise means different or in another way.
words.e: It’s good to stay active; otherwise, you’ll gain weight.
words.zh: 否則意指不同或以另一種方式。

words.d: If something is physical, it is related to your body and not your mind.
words.e: Biking is good for your physical health.
words.zh: 若某事是身體上的，則與你的身體而非心智有關。

words.d: To prove something is to show that it is true.
words.e: My teacher proved the answer on the board.
words.zh: 證明某事是指顯示它是真實的。

words.d: To react is to act in a certain way because of something that happened.
words.e: James reacted badly to the news.
words.zh: 反應是指因某事發生而以某種方式行動。

words.d: To ride something is to travel on it. You can ride an animal, a bike, etc.
words.e: I will ride a roller-coaster for the first time today.
words.zh: 騎乘某物意指搭乘其上，可騎動物、腳踏車等。

words.d: If something is situated somewhere, it is in that place.
words.e: The white board is situated between the two men.
words.zh: 若某物位於某處，即指其處於該地點。

words.d: Society is people and the way that they live.
words.e: Society expects people to be good and honest.
words.zh: 社會指人群及其生活方式。

words.d: A standard is what people consider normal or good.
words.e: This older model TV is below our store’s standards.
words.zh: 標準是人們視為正常或良好的事物。

words.d: To suggest something means to give an idea or plan about it.
words.e: He suggested that we go to see his boss.
words.zh: 建議某事意指提出相關想法或計畫。

words.d: Actually means in fact or really.
words.e: My dad looks a little mean, but actually he’s very kind.
words.zh: 「實際上」意為事實上或真正地。

words.d: Bite is the act of using your teeth to cut and tear into something.
words.e: The boy took a big bite out of his hamburger.
words.zh: 咬是指用牙齒切開或撕裂某物的動作。

words.d: The coast is the land by an ocean.
words.e: I stayed on the southern coast of Australia.
words.zh: 海岸是海洋旁的陸地。

words.d: A deal is an agreement that you have with another person.
words.e: I made a deal with the other company to give us some money.
words.zh: 協議是你與他人達成的約定。

words.d: The desert is an area of land without many plants or water.
words.e: Not many plants grow in the desert.
words.zh: 沙漠是缺乏植物與水分的陸地區域。

words.d: An earthquake is a shaking movement of the ground.
words.e: The building shook during the earthquake.
words.zh: 地震是地面的晃動。

words.d: If something is effective, it works well.
words.e: Swimming is an effective way to stay healthy.
words.zh: 若某物有效，則表示其運作良好。

words.d: To examine something is to look at it carefully.
words.e: The doctor examined my eyes today.
words.zh: 檢查某物意指仔細觀察它。

words.d: If something is false, it is not correct.
words.e: If you think the answer is false, press the red button.
words.zh: 若某物為假，即表示其不正確。

words.d: A gift is something you give someone.
words.e: Dave received many gifts for Christmas.
words.zh: 禮物是你贈予他人的東西。

words.d: Hunger is the feeling that you get when you need to eat.
words.e: After playing all day long, he was filled with hunger.
words.zh: 飢餓是當你需要進食時所感受到的感覺。

words.d: To imagine something is to think of it in your mind.
words.e: Sally imagined herself winning lots of money.
words.zh: 想像某事就是在腦海中思考它。

words.d: A journey is a long trip.
words.e: I went on a journey across the country with my parents.
words.zh: 旅程是一段長途旅行。

words.d: A puzzle is something that is hard to understand.
words.e: The question was a puzzle to him.
words.zh: 謎題是難以理解的事物。

words.d: Quite is used to say that something is complete or very much.
words.e: I think typing on a keyboard is quite easy.
words.zh: 「Quite」用來表示某事完全或非常。

words.d: Rather is used when you want to do one thing but not the other.
words.e: I would rather have the red one than the blue one.
words.zh: 「Rather」用於當你想做一件事而不想做另一件事時。

words.d: If something is specific, it is precise or exact.
words.e: Please choose a specific place on the map.
words.zh: 如果某事是具體的，它就是精確或確切的。

words.d: A tour is a short trip in which you see many sights.
words.e: I took a tour of Asia and Europe.
words.zh: 遊覽是一段短暫的行程，途中會看到許多景點。

words.d: A trip is a journey to a certain place.
words.e: Ken took a trip to the city yesterday.
words.zh: 旅行是前往某個地方的旅程。

words.d: If something has value, it is worth a lot of money.
words.e: Matthew found a treasure of very high value.
words.zh: 如果某物有價值，它就值很多錢。

words.d: A band is a group of people who play music.
words.e: My brother is in a rock band.
words.zh: 樂團是一群演奏音樂的人。

words.d: Barely means by the smallest amount, almost not.
words.e: I barely had enough money to pay for my bus ticket.
words.zh: 「Barely」意指以最小的程度，幾乎沒有。

words.d: If something is boring, it is not fun.
words.e: I think the Internet is boring.
words.zh: 如果某事無聊，它就不有趣。

words.d: To cancel means to decide that an event or a request will not happen.
words.e: She cancelled the rest of her plans because of the rain.
words.zh: 取消意指決定某個活動或請求不會發生。

words.d: A driveway is a short private road that leads to a person’s home.
words.e: The long driveway led us to their new house.
words.zh: 車道是一條通往住宅的短私人道路。

words.d: Garbage is waste material like unwanted or spoiled food, bottles, paper, etc.
words.e: The boy cleaned up the garbage around his house.
words.zh: 垃圾是廢棄物，例如不想要或腐壞的食物、瓶子、紙張等。

words.d: An instrument is something designed to do a certain task like music.
words.e: My favorite musical instrument is the piano.
words.zh: 樂器是為執行特定任務（如音樂）而設計的物品。

words.d: A list is a record of information printed with an item on each line.
words.e: My mom makes a list of groceries to buy.
words.zh: 清單是每行列印一項資訊的記錄。

words.d: Magic is the power to do impossible things.
words.e: The magician used magic to pull a rabbit out of his hat.
words.zh: 魔法是做到不可能之事的力量。

words.d: A message is a set of words that you send to someone.
words.e: I left a message for you in the envelope.
words.zh: 訊息是你傳送給他人的一組文字。

words.d: To notice something is to see it for the first time.
words.e: Did you notice the view?
words.zh: 注意到某事物是指第一次看見它。

words.d: To own something means to have it. That thing belongs to you.
words.e: My grandfather owns that house.
words.zh: 擁有某物意味著它是你的，屬於你。

words.d: To predict something is to say that it will happen.
words.e: She predicted that I would get married next year.
words.zh: 預測某事物是指說它將會發生。

words.d: A professor is a person who teaches in college.
words.e: Mike’s science professor knows a lot about physics.
words.zh: 教授是在大學教書的人。

words.d: To rush is to go somewhere or do something very quickly.
words.e: Nancy rushed to finish her homework.
words.zh: 匆忙是指非常快速地前往某處或做某事。

words.d: A schedule is a plan that tells you when to do things.
words.e: What is your class schedule for today?
words.zh: 時間表是告訴你何時做事的計畫。

words.d: To share something is to give some of it to another person.
words.e: Jimmy shared his apple with me.
words.zh: 分享某物是指將它的一部分給予他人。

words.d: A stage is a place where actors or musicians act or sing.
words.e: A large screen was on the stage.
words.zh: 舞台是演員或音樂家表演或唱歌的地方。

words.d: A storm is very bad weather. There is a lot of rain or snow.
words.e: Did that storm wake you up last night?
words.zh: 暴風雨是非常惡劣的天氣，伴隨大量降雨或降雪。

words.d: You use within to say that something is inside another thing.
words.e: Within the box, there was a pizza.
words.zh: 你用「在……之內」來表示某物在另一物體裡面。

words.d: To advertise is to tell people about something on TV, radio, etc.
words.e: They used a rabbit to help them advertise their product.
words.zh: 廣告是指在電視、廣播等媒介上向人們介紹某事物。

words.d: To assign something to someone is to tell them to do it.
words.e: I assigned the worker an important task.
words.zh: 指派某人某事是指告訴他們去執行。

words.d: An audience is a group of people who watch something together.
words.e: There was a large audience at the game.
words.zh: 觀眾是指一起觀看某事物的群體。

words.d: Breakfast is the first meal of the day.
words.e: I eat breakfast at 8:00 every morning.
words.zh: 早餐是一天中的第一餐。
