words.d: A competition is a contest to see who is the best at something.
words.e: He won the running competition over the weekend.
words.zh: 比賽是為了看誰在某方面最優秀而進行的競賽。

words.d: If the weather is cool, it is a little bit cold.
words.e: The weather is cool in the fall.
words.zh: 如果天氣涼爽，表示稍微有點冷。

words.d: If you gain something, you get more of it.
words.e: I gained some weight over the summer.
words.zh: 如果你獲得某物，就是得到更多。

words.d: Importance means the quality or condition of being needed or valued.
words.e: VIPs are people of great importance.
words.zh: 重要性是指被需要或被重視的性質或狀態。

words.d: Knowledge is information that you have about something.
words.e: A music teacher should have good knowledge of music.
words.zh: 知識是你對某事物所擁有的資訊。

words.d: If something is major, it is big or important.
words.e: I have a major problem. My boss wants me to redo my project!
words.zh: 如果某事物是主要的，表示它很大或很重要。

words.d: Mean describes someone who is unkind or cruel.
words.e: My co-worker is a very mean person. He gets angry very quickly.
words.zh: 刻薄形容某人無情或殘酷。

words.d: If you prefer something, you want it more than something else.
words.e: I prefer to take the path that will lead me to a bright future.
words.zh: 如果你偏好某事物，表示你更想要它而非其他。

words.d: A president is the leader of a country.
words.e: The president of our country made an important announcement.
words.zh: 總統是一個國家的領導人。

words.d: Progress is the act of getting closer to doing or finishing something.
words.e: Our company made financial progress this year.
words.zh: 進步是指更接近完成或達成某事物的過程。

words.d: Respect is a good opinion of someone because they are good.
words.e: I have great respect for firemen.
words.zh: 尊重是因為某人優秀而對其產生的良好評價。

words.d: Ifyou are rich, you have a lot of money.
words.e: He’s rich. He can buy anything he wants.
words.zh: 如果你富有，你擁有許多金錢。

words.d: A skill is the knowledge and ability that allows you to do something well.
words.e: A snowboarder must have the right skills to do well.
words.zh: 技能是讓你能夠做好某事的知識與能力。

words.d: Somehow means in a way or by some means which is not known.
words.e: He somehow had to find a way to reach the life preserver.
words.zh: 不知何故意指以某種未知的方式或方法。

words.d: Strength is the physical power that you have.
words.e: Exercising will increase your strength and give you more power.
words.zh: 力量是你所擁有的體能。

words.d: To vote is to officially choose between two or more things.
words.e: I voted for Billy to be our class president.
words.zh: 投票是指在兩個或多個選項之間正式做出選擇。

words.d: If something is above, it is at a higher level than something else.
words.e: He straightened the sign that was above the crowd.
words.zh: 如果某物在上方，它處於比另一物更高的位置。

words.d: If something is ahead of something else, it is in front of it.
words.e: The blue car drove on ahead of us.
words.zh: 如果某物在另一物之前，它就在其前方。

words.d: An amount is how much there is of something.
words.e: Can I use my card to pay for the entire amount?
words.zh: 數量是指某物的多少。

words.d: A belief is a strong feeling that something is correct or true.
words.e: A preacher or priest should have a strong belief in God.
words.zh: 信念是一種強烈的感覺，認為某事是正確或真實的。

words.d: The center of something is the middle of it.
words.e: The center of a dart board is the most important spot.
words.zh: 某物的中心就是它的中間部分。

words.d: If something is common, it happens often or there is much of it.
words.e: It is common for snow to fall in the winter.
words.zh: 如果某事很常見，表示它經常發生或數量很多。

words.d: To cost is to require expenditure or payment.
words.e: These designer shoes cost more than the regular ones.
words.zh: 花費是指需要支出或付款。

words.d: To demonstrate something is to show how it is done.
words.e: She demonstrated her plan to her co-workers.
words.zh: 示範某事是指展示如何完成它。

words.d: Different describes someone or something that is not the same as others.
words.e: Each of my sisters has a different hair style from one another.
words.zh: 不同的用來描述與其他不同的人或事物。

words.d: Evidence is a fact or thing that you use to prove something.
words.e: He used the pictures as evidence that UFOs are real.
words.zh: 證據是你用來證明某事的事實或事物。

words.d: Honesty means the quality of being truthful or honest.
words.e: A courtroom should be a place of honesty.
words.zh: 誠實意指真實或正直的品質。

words.d: An idiom is a phrase with a meaning different from its words.
words.e: The idiom “when pigs fly” means that something will never happen.
words.zh: 成語是含義與字面不同的詞組。

words.d: If something is independent, it is not controlled by something else.
words.e: She chose to live an independent life in the country.
words.zh: 若某事物是獨立的，則不受其他事物控制。

words.d: Inside means the inner part, space or side of something.
words.e: The inside of the box was empty.
words.zh: 內部指某物的內在部分、空間或內側。

words.d: A master is a person who is very good at something.
words.e: My brother is a master of taekwondo.
words.zh: 大師是在某方面非常擅長的人。

words.d: A memory is something you remember.
words.e: The memory of my first time in the city will always be the best.
words.zh: 記憶是你所記得的事物。

words.d: If something is proper, it is right.
words.e: It is not proper to throw your garbage on the road.
words.zh: 若某事物是恰當的，即為正確。

words.d: To scan something is to look at it very carefully.
words.e: You must scan each person closely.
words.zh: 掃描某物即仔細觀察它。

words.d: A section is a part of something larger.
words.e: The green section of the graph is the most important part.
words.zh: 區塊是較大整體的一部分。

words.d: The surface of something is the top part or outside of it.
words.e: The surface of the moon is very rough.
words.zh: 某物的表面是其頂部或外部。

words.d: When a person is anxious, they worry that something bad will happen.
words.e: She was anxious about not making her appointment on time.
words.zh: 當人焦慮時，他們擔心壞事會發生。

words.d: When something is awful, it is very bad.
words.e: Her performance last night was awful.
words.zh: 當某事物糟糕時，它非常不好。

words.d: To consist of is to be made of parts or things.
words.e: Today’s choices for lunch consisted of pizza, hamburgers, and hot dogs.
words.zh: 由……組成即由部分或事物構成。

words.d: To desire is to want something.
words.e: My sister desires a big house and lots of money.
words.zh: 渴望即想要某事物。

words.d: When a person is eager about something, they are excited about it.
words.e: The man was eager to talk about the good news.
words.zh: 當人對某事熱切時，他們對此感到興奮。

words.d: A household is all the people who live in one house.
words.e: Our household is made up of my father, my mother and me.
words.zh: 家庭是指同住在一間房子裡的所有人。

words.d: An intent is a plan to do something.
words.e: Her intent is to visit Italy next summer.
words.zh: 意圖是指打算做某事的計畫。

words.d: A landscape is how an area of land looks.
words.e: The landscape of the country is very green.
words.zh: 景觀是指一片土地的外觀。

words.d: To lift something is to move it higher.
words.e: The man tried to lift the box.
words.zh: 舉起某物是指將它移得更高。

words.d: To load is to put objects into something.
words.e: The man loaded the boxes into a truck.
words.zh: 裝載是指將物體放入某物中。

words.d: A lung is the organ in the body that fills with air when breathing.
words.e: Having strong lungs is necessary for a healthy life.
words.zh: 肺是身體中呼吸時會充滿空氣的器官。

words.d: A motion is a movement that someone makes.
words.e: The police officer made a motion with his hand.
words.zh: 動作是指某人做出的一種移動。

words.d: The pace of something is the speed at which it happens.
words.e: I ran the race at a slower pace than my friend.
words.zh: 某事物的速度是指它發生的快慢。

words.d: When someone is polite, they are acting in a thoughtful way.
words.e: The boy was very polite: he behaved very thoughtfully.
words.zh: 當一個人很有禮貌時，他們的行為表現得體貼周到。

words.d: To possess something is to have it or own it.
words.e: My uncle possesses three sheep, a chicken, a cow and a dog.
words.zh: 擁有某物是指持有或佔有它。

words.d: When something happens rapidly, it happens very fast.
words.e: The train moved rapidly on the tracks.
words.zh: 當某事迅速發生時，它發生得非常快。

words.d: To remark is to say something.
words.e: The teacher remarked on how quickly the students were learning.
words.zh: 評論是指說出某句話。

words.d: To seek is to look for something.
words.e: If I have a problem, I seek my sister’s advice.
words.zh: 尋找是指搜尋某物。

words.d: To shine is to make a bright light.
words.e: The candles are shining in the dark room.
words.zh: 發光是指發出明亮的光芒。

words.d: To spill is to have something fall out of its container.
words.e: I spilled the coffee on the table.
words.zh: 溢出是指容器中的東西掉落出來。

words.d: To bring means to take someone or something to a person or place.
words.e: Oscar will bring in all the boxes.
words.zh: 帶去意味著將某人或某物帶到某人或某處。

words.d: A castle is a building with strong walls where royalty usually lives.
words.e: The castle was built many years ago.
words.zh: 城堡是有堅固牆壁的建築，通常是皇室居住的地方。

words.d: To command someone is to tell them to do something.
words.e: The general commanded his army to attack.
words.zh: 命令某人就是告訴他們去做某事。

words.d: To counsel someone is to listen and give advice about a problem.
words.e: The teacher counseled the girl about her troubles.
words.zh: 輔導某人是指傾聽並針對問題給予建議。

words.d: To ensure is to make certain that something happens.
words.e: Eating good food ensures that we stay healthy.
words.zh: 確保就是確定某事會發生。

words.d: An explosion is a violent burst, usually with a loud sound.
words.e: A nuclear explosion can cause great damage.
words.zh: 爆炸是劇烈的爆裂，通常伴隨巨大聲響。

words.d: Jewelry is something that people wear like earrings or necklaces.
words.e: The woman looked beautiful with her nice dress and jewelry.
words.zh: 珠寶是人們佩戴的物品，如耳環或項鍊。

words.d: To land means to come to the ground usually from a ship or aircraft.
words.e: The parachutist landed safely on the ground.
words.zh: 登陸通常指從船隻或飛機上到達地面。

words.d: A meteor is a rock from outer space that falls to Earth.
words.e: We saw a meteor in the sky last night.
words.zh: 流星是從外太空墜落到地球的岩石。

words.d: A monster is any imaginary frightening creature that looks strange.
words.e: The monster scared everyone in the village.
words.zh: 怪物是任何想像中長相怪異的可怕生物。

words.d: If something is northern, it is in the direction of north.
words.e: It is much colder in northern countries than it is here.
words.zh: 如果某物是北方的，它就在朝北的方向。

words.d: If something is remote, it is very far away and difficult to get to.
words.e: It took many days to get to the remote island.
words.zh: 如果某物是偏遠的，它非常遙遠且難以到達。

words.d: If something is southern, it is in the direction of south.
words.e: Taegu is located in the southern part of the Korean peninsula.
words.zh: 如果某物是南方的，它就在朝南的方向。

words.d: A statue is an image of a person or animal made of stone or metal.
words.e: I saw a beautiful statue of a lion today.
words.zh: 雕像是用石頭或金屬製成的人或動物形象。

words.d: Steam is water that has become hot and has turned into a misty gas.
words.e: We could see the steam rise from the boiling water.
words.zh: 蒸氣是水受熱後轉變成的霧狀氣體。

words.d: To submit to someone is to agree to do what they tell you to do.
words.e: She submitted to her mother’s wishes and did her homework.
words.zh: 順從某人，就是同意按照他們的要求去做。

words.d: A temple is a building that is used for religious purposes.
words.e: The people visited the temple to pray.
words.zh: 寺廟是用於宗教目的的建築物。

words.d: Upper means higher in position or place.
words.e: A home’s attic is at the upper part of the structure.
words.zh: 上方的意思是位置或地方較高。

words.d: A weed is a plant, especially one that is not useful or wanted.
words.e: The yard of the house was full of ugly weeds.
words.zh: 雜草是一種植物，尤其指沒有用處或不受歡迎的。

words.d: A wing is the part of an animal that lets it fly.
words.e: The bird used its wings to fly across the water.
words.zh: 翅膀是動物用來飛行的部位。

words.d: An arrow is a thin, straight stick shot from a bow.
words.e: The arrow flew through the air and hit the target.
words.zh: 箭是從弓射出的細長直棍。

words.d: A battle is a fight between two armies during a war.
words.e: The battle lasted for many days.
words.zh: 戰役是戰爭中兩支軍隊之間的戰鬥。

words.d: A bow is a weapon made of curved wood and string that shoots arrows.
words.e: He went hunting with a bow and arrow.
words.zh: 弓是由彎曲的木頭和繩索製成、能發射箭的武器。

words.d: When people are brave, they are not afraid to face pain or danger.
words.e: The brave firefighter saved the girl from the burning building.
words.zh: 當人們勇敢時，他們不畏懼面對痛苦或危險。

words.d: A chief is the leader of a group of people.
words.e: The chief led the people through the mountains.
words.zh: 首領是一群人的領導者。

words.d: A disadvantage is a situation where someone is likely to lose.
words.e: Mike had a disadvantage in the race since he hurt his knee.
words.zh: 劣勢是指某人可能失敗的情況。

words.d: An enemy is a country that is fighting another country during a war.
words.e: The enemy prepared to attack the kingdom.
words.zh: 敵人是戰爭中與另一國交戰的國家。

words.d: An entrance is a place where someone can enter an area.
words.e: The gate was locked, so Bill had to find a different entrance.
words.zh: 入口是讓人能夠進入某個區域的地方。

words.d: If something hardly happens, it almost does not happen at all.
words.e: I hardly saw the concert since I had to leave early.
words.zh: 如果某事幾乎不發生，就表示它幾乎完全沒有發生。

words.d: To intend to do something means to plan to do it.
words.e: I intend to finish college in three years.
words.zh: 意圖做某事即表示計劃去做。

words.d: Laughter is the sound produced by laughing about something funny.
words.e: Susan’s joke made her classmates burst into laughter.
words.zh: 笑聲是因有趣之事而發出的聲音。

words.d: A log is a thick piece of wood that is cut from a tree.
words.e: The fire was too small, so we added another log to it.
words.zh: 圓木是從樹上砍下來的厚木塊。

words.d: The military is the armed forces of a country.
words.e: I joined the military after I finished high school.
words.zh: 軍隊是一個國家的武裝力量。

words.d: To obey means to follow what a law or a person says you must do.
words.e: My little sister did not obey my mother. Now she is in trouble.
words.zh: 服從意味著遵循法律或他人要求你做的事。

words.d: To secure something means to get it after a lot of effort.
words.e: I was able to secure a good grade on my test after weeks of studying.
words.zh: 獲取某物指經過一番努力後得到它。

words.d: When someone or something is steady, they are under control.
words.e: The problem was hard but she remained steady and solved it.
words.zh: 當某人或某物穩定時，表示處於可控狀態。

words.d: To trust is to believe that someone is honest and will do what is right.
words.e: I trust my friends; they don’t tell my secrets to other people.
words.zh: 信任是相信某人誠實且會做正確的事。

words.d: To twist something is to wrap it around itself or another thing.
words.e: She twisted the spaghetti around her fork.
words.zh: 扭轉某物是將其纏繞自身或另一物體。

words.d: Unless means if not or except when.
words.e: Unless you clean your room, you cannot play with your friends.
words.zh: 除非意指「如果不」或「除了……時候」。

words.d: A weapon is an object that can be used to hurt people.
words.e: Swords have been used as weapons for thousands of years.
words.zh: 武器是可用來傷害人的物品。

words.d: The chest is the front part of a person’s body under the neck.
words.e: The water in the lake was as high as my chest.
words.zh: 胸部是頸部以下人體的前部。

words.d: If you have confidence about something, you are sure of it.
words.e: I have confidence that I did well on the test.
words.zh: 若對某事有信心，表示你對它很確定。

words.d: A consequence is a result of a choice or action.
words.e: Asa consequence of missing my bus, I had to find another way to work.
words.zh: 後果是選擇或行動的結果。

words.d: A disaster is a really bad thing that happens.
words.e: When the car crashed, it was a disaster.
words.zh: 災難是發生的非常糟糕的事。

words.d: To disturb someone means to upset them.
words.e: The loud noise disturbed me while I was working.
words.zh: 打擾某人意味著使他們心煩。

words.d: To estimate something means to make a guess about it.
words.e: The boy estimated that he was one meter tall.
words.zh: 估計某事物意味著對它進行猜測。

words.d: When you honor people or things, you show respect for them.
words.e: I honor my science teacher by working hard in class.
words.zh: 當你尊敬人或事物時，你對他們表示尊重。

words.d: To impress someone means to make that person proud.
words.e: He was able to impress the girls with his new dance.
words.zh: 讓某人印象深刻意味著使那個人感到驕傲。

words.d: A marathon is a very long foot race just over 42 kilometers.
words.e: I ran a marathon, and now my legs are tired.
words.zh: 馬拉松是一項超過42公里的長距離賽跑。

words.d: When something is narrow, it is very thin.
words.e: The bridge is too narrow for a car to drive over.
words.zh: 當某物狹窄時，它非常細窄。

words.d: When something is pale, it does not have a bright color.
words.e: The girl’s skin was very pale.
words.zh: 當某物蒼白時，它沒有鮮豔的顏色。

words.d: When something is rough, it is not even or smooth.
words.e: The rough ground hurt my feet.
words.zh: 當某物粗糙時，它不平整或不光滑。

words.d: To satisfy someone means to make them happy.
words.e: It will satisfy my teacher if I finish all my homework.
words.zh: 滿足某人意味著使他們開心。

words.d: To scream means to make a loud noise with your mouth.
words.e: The girl saw a spider and screamed.
words.zh: 尖叫意味著用嘴巴發出很大的聲音。

words.d: When someone or something is sensitive, they are easily hurt.
words.e: My teeth are sensitive to cold things.
words.zh: 當某人或某物敏感時，他們容易受傷。

words.d: The shade is a dark area that something makes when it blocks the sun.
words.e: It was hot outside, so the boy sat in the shade of a tree.
words.zh: 陰影是物體遮擋陽光時形成的黑暗區域。

words.d: To supplement something is to add something else to it in a good way.
words.e: He supplements his diet with fresh fruits.
words.zh: 補充某物是以好的方式添加其他東西。

words.d: When someone feels terror, they are scared.
words.e: I felt a sense of terror when the tiger chased me.
words.zh: 當某人感到恐懼時，他們很害怕。

words.d: A threat is something bad that might happen.
words.e: Due to the dark clouds, there was a threat of a bad storm.
words.zh: 威脅是可能發生的壞事。

words.d: A victim is a person that had something bad happen to them.
words.e: I was a victim of a robbery.
words.zh: 受害者是指遭遇不幸事件的人。

words.d: An ancestor is a family member from the past.
words.e: My ancestors came from Germany.
words.zh: 祖先是指過去的家族成員。

words.d: An angle is the direction from which you look at something.
words.e: The giraffe turned its head to see from another angle.
words.zh: 角度是指你觀看某事物的方向。

words.d: A boot is a heavy shoe that goes over your ankle.
words.e: He wore boots so that his feet wouldn’t get wet.
words.zh: 靴子是一種包覆腳踝的厚重鞋子。

words.d: A border is the edge of an area.
words.e: The postcard had a pretty green border of pine needles.
words.zh: 邊界是指一個區域的邊緣。

words.d: To congratulate someone is to tell them that you are happy for them.
words.e: Bill and Angela congratulated each other on a job well done.
words.zh: 祝賀某人是指告訴他們你為他們感到高興。

words.d: A frame is a border for a picture or mirror.
words.e: I have to get a frame for my friend’s picture.
words.zh: 框架是指圖片或鏡子的邊框。

words.d: Heaven is the place that some people believe people go when they die.
words.e: When I die, I hope that I go to heaven.
words.zh: 天堂是指某些人相信人死後會去的地方。

words.d: If someone or something is incredible, it is hard to believe they are true.
words.e: I have an incredible story to tell you about my vacation.
words.zh: 如果某人或某事物難以置信，是指很難相信其真實存在。

words.d: A legend is a story from the past.
words.e: There is a well-known legend about a king and his queen.
words.zh: 傳說是指來自過去的故事。

words.d: To praise is to show that you like someone or something.
words.e: The coach praised both athletes after a good practice.
words.zh: 讚美是指表達你喜歡某人或某事物。

words.d: To proceed is to go in a certain direction.
words.e: My son and I proceeded to the beach so we could go fishing.
words.zh: 前進是指朝著某個方向移動。

words.d: If something is pure, it is very clear and beautiful.
words.e: The rose was pure. It had no dirt or imperfections.
words.zh: 如果某事物純淨，是指它非常清澈且美麗。

words.d: A relative is a family member.
words.e: My relatives came by to see the new baby.
words.zh: 親戚是指家庭成員。

words.d: If one is senior, they are the oldest or have been there the longest.
words.e: Because he got his job first, Bob is the senior chef.
words.zh: 如果某人年長，是指他們年齡最大或待得最久。

words.d: If someone or something is silent, they make no sound.
words.e: Since no one was home, the house was silent.
words.zh: 若某人或某物沉默，則不發出聲音。

words.d: To sink into something is to slowly fall into it.
words.e: The boat had a hole in it, and it sank into the ocean.
words.zh: 陷入某物是指緩慢地掉入其中。

words.d: If someone or something is superior, they are better than another.
words.e: I think cooking outdoors is superior to cooking indoors.
words.zh: 若某人或某物優越，則比另一個更好。

words.d: To surround something is to close in on it from all sides.
words.e: We surrounded the suspect on all four sides.
words.zh: 包圍某物是指從四面八方將其圍住。

words.d: If something is thick, it is wide and solid.
words.e: The fog was so thick that I couldn’t see through it.
words.zh: 若某物厚實，則寬闊且堅固。

words.d: To wrap is to cover something on all sides.
words.e: I wrapped his gift and put a bow on it.
words.zh: 包裹是指從各面覆蓋某物。

words.d: If someone goes or travels abroad, they go to another country.
words.e: My brother wants to go abroad next year.
words.zh: 若某人出國或旅行至國外，即前往另一個國家。

words.d: To anger someone is to make them mad.
words.e: It angers me when people are rude.
words.zh: 激怒某人是指使其生氣。

words.d: A bride is a woman who is getting married or has just gotten married.
words.e: The bride looked beautiful in her wedding dress.
words.zh: 新娘是指即將結婚或剛結婚的女子。

words.d: If something is brief, it only lasts fora short time.
words.e: The meeting this afternoon was very brief.
words.zh: 若某事物短暫，則只持續很短時間。

words.d: To chase someone or something is to follow them in order to catch them.
words.e: I was chased by an angry native.
words.zh: 追逐某人或某物是指跟隨他們以抓住他們。

words.d: To disappoint is to make one feel sad or unsatisfied.
words.e: I do not want to disappoint my family, so I try to do well at school.
words.zh: 使人失望是指讓人感到悲傷或不滿足。

words.d: To dive is to jump into water.
words.e: I will dive into the lake once we get there.
words.zh: 跳水是指跳入水中。

words.d: To exchange means to give something for another thing in return.
words.e: I exchanged my foreign money for American dollars.
words.zh: 交換是指用一物換取另一物作為回報。

words.d: A favor is something you do for someone to help them.
words.e: Can you do me a favor and turn off the lights?
words.zh: 幫忙是指你為幫助他人而做的事。

words.d: A fee is an amount of money that a person or company asks for a service.
words.e: I had to pay an hourly fee to speak with my lawyer.
words.zh: 費用是指個人或公司因提供服務而收取的金額。

words.d: If something lasts forever, it means it lasts for all time.
words.e: The young couple promised that they would love each other forever.
words.zh: 如果某事物永遠持續，表示它永無止境。

words.d: A guy is an informal way to call a man.
words.e: The guy at the flower shop was really helpful today.
words.zh: 「傢伙」是對男性的非正式稱呼。

words.d: If people or things are lovely, they are good-looking or beautiful.
words.e: The trees look lovely in the fall.
words.zh: 若人或事物可愛，意指他們好看或美麗。

words.d: A mood is the way someone is feeling.
words.e: I am in a good mood because I did well on my math test.
words.zh: 心情是一個人當下的感受狀態。

words.d: A palace is a very large building. It is often the home of a royal family.
words.e: The king and queen live in a beautiful palace.
words.zh: 宮殿是非常宏偉的建築，通常是皇室家族的居所。

words.d: To permit something is to let someone do it.
words.e: I was sick, so my mother permitted me to stay home from school.
words.zh: 允許某事就是同意讓某人去做。

words.d: To protest something is to argue about it with someone.
words.e: The people protested the decision of the president.
words.zh: 抗議某事就是與他人就此爭論。

words.d: A sculpture is a piece of art that is made from wood, clay, or stone.
words.e: We saw an old sculpture of Buddha at the museum.
words.zh: 雕塑是以木材、黏土或石材製成的藝術品。

words.d: A tribe is a group of people who live in the same culture.
words.e: There’s a small tribe of people who live in the mountains of Spain.
words.zh: 部落是指一群共享相同文化的人。

words.d: Youth is a time in people’s lives when they are young.
words.e: My mother wanted to be a nurse in her youth.
words.zh: 青春是人們生命中年輕的時期。

words.d: To do something on time’s basis is how often you do it.
words.e: My grandfather gets his hearing checked on a yearly basis.
words.zh: 按時間基礎做某事，指的是做這件事的頻率。

words.d: Biology is the study of living things.
words.e: We learned about the human heart in biology class.
words.zh: 生物學是研究生物的學科。

words.d: A cage is something that holds an animal so it cannot leave.
words.e: We put the parrots in their cage at night.
words.zh: 籠子是限制動物無法離開的容器。

words.d: A colleague is somebody you work with.
words.e: My colleague helped me finish the job.
words.zh: 同事是與你一起工作的人。

words.d: A colony is a country controlled by another country.
words.e: The USA was at one time a colony of Great Britain.
words.zh: 殖民地是指由另一個國家所控制的國家。

words.d: To debate is to seriously discuss something with someone.
words.e: The husband and wife debated over which TV to buy.
words.zh: 辯論是指與某人認真討論某事。

words.d: To depart is to leave some place so you can go to another place.
words.e: The plane departed for Italy at 3:00 this afternoon.
words.zh: 出發是指離開某個地方，以便前往另一個地方。

words.d: To depress someone is to make them sad.
words.e: The bad news from work depressed the man.
words.zh: 使某人沮喪是指讓他們感到難過。

words.d: When something is factual, it is true.
words.e: John learns about history from factual books.
words.zh: 當某事是事實性的，它就是真實的。

words.d: To fascinate someone is to make them really like something.
words.e: The kitten was fascinated by the ball of yarn.
words.zh: 使某人著迷是指讓他們非常喜歡某事物。

words.d: A mission is an important job that is sometimes far away.
words.e: The woman’s mission was to help sick people.
words.zh: 任務是一項重要的工作，有時在遙遠的地方進行。

words.d: You use nevertheless to show that something goes against a fact.
words.e: He is usually friendly. Nevertheless, he wasn’t this afternoon.
words.zh: 你用「然而」來表示某事與某個事實相悖。

words.d: An occupation is a person’s job.
words.e: My father’s occupation is a dentist.
words.zh: 職業是指一個人的工作。

words.d: If you go overseas, you go to a country on the other side of an ocean.
words.e: John often goes overseas for vacations.
words.zh: 如果你出國，你是指前往海洋另一邊的國家。

words.d: To persuade someone is to make them agree to do something.
words.e: The children persuaded their parents to buy them gifts.
words.zh: 說服某人是指讓他們同意做某事。

words.d: A route is the way you go from one place to another.
words.e: I saw many new houses along the route to the city.
words.zh: 路線是你從一個地方到另一個地方所走的路徑。

words.d: Ruins are old buildings that are not used anymore.
words.e: I visited some interesting ruins in Greece.
words.zh: 廢墟是指不再使用的古老建築物。

words.d: A scholar is a person who studies something and knows much about it.
words.e: The scholar knew much about art history.
words.zh: 學者是指研究某事物並對其了解很多的人。

words.d: When someone or something is significant, they are important.
words.e: I read many significant novels as a literature major in university.
words.zh: 當某人或某事具有重要性時，他們是重要的。

words.d: A volcano is a mountain with a hole on top where hot liquid comes out.
words.e: When the volcano erupted, smoke and heat filled the air.
words.zh: 火山是山頂有洞、可噴出高溫液體的山。

words.d: If something is broad, it is wide.
words.e: The river is very long and broad.
words.zh: 若某物寬闊，即表示其寬廣。

words.d: A bush is a plant with many thin branches. It is smaller than a tree.
words.e: My dad and I planted some small bushes around the house.
words.zh: 灌木是枝葉細密的植物，比樹木矮小。

words.d: If someone or something is capable of something, they can do it.
words.e: The Olympic athlete is capable of lifting a lot of weight.
words.zh: 若某人或某物有能力做某事，即表示能做到。

words.d: To cheat is to be dishonest so that you can win or do well.
words.e: They cheated on the test by sharing answers.
words.zh: 作弊是為了取勝或表現良好而採取不誠實的手段。

words.d: To concentrate on someone or something is to give your full attention.
words.e: I could not concentrate on my homework because the room was so loud.
words.zh: 專注於某人或某事，即全心投入注意力。

words.d: To conclude is to arrive at a logical end by looking at evidence.
words.e: I saw crumbs on my dog’s face, so I concluded that he ate my cookie.
words.zh: 推論是根據證據得出合乎邏輯的結論。

words.d: Confident means that one believes they can do something without failing.
words.e: She was confident she could climb the mountain due to her training.
words.zh: 自信意指相信自己能成功而不會失敗。

words.d: If something is considerable, it is large in size, amount or extent.
words.e: They paid a considerable amount of money for that car.
words.zh: 若某物可觀，即表示其體積、數量或程度龐大。

words.d: To convey is to communicate or make ideas known.
words.e: That picture of a crying child conveys a feeling of sadness.
words.zh: 傳達是溝通或讓想法為人所知。

words.d: If something is definite, it is certain or sure to be true.
words.e: There is a definite connection between hard work and success.
words.zh: 若某事物明確，即確定無疑或必然為真。

words.d: Delight is a feeling of being very happy with something.
words.e: He felt such delight after getting a promotion at work.
words.zh: 喜悅是對某事感到非常快樂的情緒。

words.d: A destination is the place where someone or something is going to.
words.e: The destination of this plane is Munich, Germany.
words.zh: 目的地是某人或某物將前往的地點。

words.d: To dictate something is to read it aloud so it can be written down.
words.e: He dictated his speech so his secretary could write it down.
words.zh: 口述某事是將其大聲朗讀以便記錄下來。

words.d: The edge of something is the part of it that is farthest from the center.
words.e: He ran to the edge of the cliff.
words.zh: 某物的邊緣是離中心最遠的部分。

words.d: A path is a way from one place to another that people can walk along.
words.e: We followed a path through the woods.
words.zh: 小徑是連接兩地、可供人行走的道路。

words.d: To resort to something is to depend on it in order to solve a problem.
words.e: I hope they don’t resort to violence to end the argument.
words.zh: 訴諸某事是指依賴它來解決問題。

words.d: A shadow is the dark area that is made when something blocks light.
words.e: The man’s shadow was taller than he was.
words.zh: 陰影是物體阻擋光線時形成的黑暗區域。

words.d: To succeed is to complete something that you planned or tried to do.
words.e: He will continue to work on the robot until he succeeds.
words.zh: 成功是指完成你計劃或嘗試要做的事。

words.d: To suspect something is to believe that it is true.
words.e: I suspect that those kids stole the money.
words.zh: 懷疑某事是指相信它是真的。

words.d: A valley is a low area of land between two mountains or hills.
words.e: We looked at the valley below from the top of the mountain.
words.zh: 山谷是兩座山或丘陵之間的低窪地帶。

words.d: To admire someone is to like them for what they do.
words.e: I admire my brother for his hard work.
words.zh: 欽佩某人是指因他們的行為而喜歡他們。

words.d: To aid someone is to help them when they need something.
words.e: The doctor aided the boy after his accident.
words.zh: 援助某人是指在他們需要時提供幫助。

words.d: To attempt something is to try to do that thing.
words.e: I am attempting to learn English.
words.zh: 嘗試某事是指努力去做那件事。

words.d: Authority is the power that someone has because of their position.
words.e: The policeman has authority on the streets.
words.zh: 權威是指某人因職位而擁有的權力。

words.d: A capital is an important city where a country’s leaders live and work.
words.e: We will visit the capital to learn about our government.
words.zh: 首都是一個國家領袖居住和工作的重要城市。

words.d: To cooperate is to work together to do something.
words.e: The students cooperated to clean up the classroom.
words.zh: 合作是指共同協力完成某事。

words.d: To defend someone or something is to protect them from attack.
words.e: The soldiers defended the town from the invaders.
words.zh: 捍衛某人或某物是指保護他們免受攻擊。

words.d: Destruction is damage to something so bad that it can’t be fixed.
words.e: After the big fire, there was much destruction in the city.
words.zh: 破壞是指對某物造成無法修復的損害。

words.d: Disorder is a lack of order, or a complete mess.
words.e: The teacher’s desk had many papers in disorder.
words.zh: 混亂是指缺乏秩序，或完全一團糟。

words.d: A division is the act of making smaller groups out of a larger one.
words.e: The chart had six divisions which all had different colors.
words.zh: 劃分是將較大的群體分成較小群體的行為。

words.d: To enable a person is to make it possible for them to do something.
words.e: Having the key enabled us to open the door.
words.zh: 賦能某人，就是使其有可能做到某件事。

words.d: To frustrate is to prevent someone from fulfilling their desire.
words.e: The machine frustrated me because I could not fix it.
words.zh: 使受挫，就是阻止某人實現其願望。

words.d: To govern is to control the public business of a country, state, or city.
words.e: The United States is governed from the White House.
words.zh: 治理，就是掌管國家、州或城市的公共事務。

words.d: To have plenty of something is to have more than you need.
words.e: The school had plenty of books for the students to read.
words.zh: 擁有充足的某物，就是擁有超過你所需的數量。

words.d: To relieve someone is to make them feel less pain.
words.e: The medicine relieved the sick boy.
words.zh: 減輕某人的痛苦，就是使其感覺較不疼痛。

words.d: Reputation is the opinion that people have about someone.
words.e: The doctor had a reputation for helping people.
words.zh: 名聲，是人們對某人的看法。

words.d: Royal describes something that belongs to a king or queen.
words.e: The king sat upon the royal throne.
words.zh: 王室的，描述屬於國王或女王的事物。

words.d: A slave is a person who is not free and must work for someone else.
words.e: The slave worked very hard all day long.
words.zh: 奴隸，是沒有自由、必須為他人工作的人。

words.d: To struggle is to fight against someone or something.
words.e: The kids struggled with each other for the toy.
words.zh: 掙扎，就是與某人或某事對抗。

words.d: When someone is stupid, they lack intelligence.
words.e: He said something stupid that made everyone angry at him.
words.zh: 當某人愚蠢時，他們缺乏智慧。

words.d: A citizen is someone who lives in a certain town or city.
words.e: Carlos was born in Spain. He is a Spanish citizen.
words.zh: 公民，是居住在特定城鎮或城市的人。

words.d: A council is a group of people who run a city or town.
words.e: The council met to discuss the new laws for the city.
words.zh: 議會，是管理城市或城鎮的一群人。

words.d: To declare is to say something officially.
words.e: I declared my love for him.
words.zh: 宣布，就是正式地說出某事。

words.d: When people or things are enormous, they are very large.
words.e: My dog looks enormous next to yours.
words.zh: 當人或事物巨大時，它們非常龐大。

words.d: When someone or something are extraordinary, they are amazing.
words.e: The fireman who rescued the girl was extraordinary.
words.zh: 當某人或某事物非凡出眾時，他們是令人驚嘆的。

words.d: Fog is a thick cloud that is near the ground or water.
words.e: I did not want to drive in the thick fog.
words.zh: 霧是靠近地面或水面的濃厚雲層。

words.d: A funeral is a ceremony that takes place after a person dies.
words.e: They had a funeral for the soldier who died during the war.
words.zh: 葬禮是一個人去世後舉行的儀式。

words.d: When people or things are giant, they are very big.
words.e: The giant truck got in my way.
words.zh: 當人或事物是巨大的，他們非常龐大。

words.d: An impression is the way of thinking about someone or something.
words.e: Most people’s first impression of Dr. Giani is that he is mean.
words.zh: 印象是對某人或某事物的看法。

words.d: Income is how much money a person or business makes.
words.e: Her company pays her a fairly good income.
words.zh: 收入是一個人或企業賺取的金錢數額。

words.d: When someone is mad, they are angry.
words.e: Mother got mad when I didn’t listen to her.
words.zh: 當某人發怒時，他們是生氣的。

words.d: If someone ought to do something, then it is the right thing to do.
words.e: I ought to take my library books back.
words.zh: 如果有人應該做某事，那麼這是正確該做的事。

words.d: To resist something is to fight against it.
words.e: He resisted the treatment at the hospital.
words.zh: 抵抗某事就是與之對抗。

words.d: To reveal is to show something.
words.e: I will reveal where I hid the candy bar.
words.zh: 揭露就是展示某事物。

words.d: To rid is to make a place free from something or someone.
words.e: We rid our home of mice by using traps.
words.zh: 清除是使某個地方脫離某人或某事物。

words.d: A sword is a long sharp weapon.
words.e: They used to use swords in battles in ancient times.
words.zh: 劍是一種長而鋒利的武器。

words.d: A tale is a story.
words.e: She told her two friends about the wild tale of her day.
words.zh: 故事就是一個敘述。

words.d: To trap people or animals is to capture them so they cannot get away.
words.e: We trapped butterflies in a net.
words.zh: 陷阱捕捉人或動物，使他們無法逃脫。

words.d: A trial is the way a court discovers if a person is guilty or innocent.
words.e: He went on trial for robbing the bank.
words.zh: 審判是法院判定一個人有罪或無罪的方式。

words.d: When people are violent, they want to hurt someone.
words.e: The man was put into jail because he was violent.
words.zh: 當人們暴力相向時，他們想要傷害某人。

words.d: Admission is the act of allowing to enter a place.
words.e: The admission ticket to the movie was $5.
words.zh: 准入是指允許進入某個場所的行為。

words.d: Astronomy is the study of the stars and planets.
words.e: Harold loved watching the stars, so he decided to study astronomy.
words.zh: 天文學是研究恆星與行星的學問。

words.d: To blame someone for something bad is to say they did it.
words.e: My mom blamed me for something I didn’t do.
words.zh: 將某件壞事歸咎於某人，就是說那是他們做的。

words.d: Chemistry the study of and reaction to substances.
words.e: In chemistry class, the professor taught us about chemical reactions.
words.zh: 化學是研究物質及其反應的學科。

words.d: If something happens despite what you do, it happens anyway.
words.e: We still played the game despite the cold weather.
words.zh: 如果某事儘管你做了努力還是發生了，那它就是照樣發生了。

words.d: A dinosaur is a very big animal that lived millions of years ago.
words.e: I like to see the dinosaur bones at the museum.
words.zh: 恐龍是一種生活在數百萬年前的巨大動物。

words.d: An exhibit is a display of interesting things.
words.e: There was an animal exhibit at the fair.
words.zh: 展覽是展示有趣事物的陳列。

words.d: Fame is reputation one has gained among the public.
words.e: He had fame and fortune, but he was not happy.
words.zh: 名聲是一個人在公眾中獲得的聲譽。

words.d: A forecast is an idea about what the weather will be like in the future.
words.e: The forecast says that it will rain all week.
words.zh: 預報是關於未來天氣狀況的推測。

words.d: A genius is a very smart person.
words.e: Since she was a genius, she easily passed all of her school exams.
words.zh: 天才是指非常聰明的人。

words.d: Someone who is gentle is kind and calm.
words.e: He is very gentle with the baby.
words.zh: 溫柔的人是指和善且冷靜的人。

words.d: Geography is the study of where things are.
words.e: I had to draw a map for geography class.
words.zh: 地理學是研究事物所在位置的學問。

words.d: To interfere is to cause problems and keep something from happening.
words.e: My little sister always interferes when I’m trying to study.
words.zh: 干擾就是製造問題並阻止某事發生。

words.d: To do something lightly is to not push very hard.
words.e: Draw lightly so you do not tear your paper.
words.zh: 輕輕地做某事，就是不用力氣去推。

words.d: A principal is a person in charge of a school.
words.e: My school’s principal can be very strict with the rules.
words.zh: 校長是負責管理學校的人。

words.d: A row is a line of things.
words.e: James put all of his toy soldiers into neat rows.
words.zh: 一排是指一行物品。

words.d: A shelf is a place on a wall where you put things.
words.e: I keep my clothes on a shelf in my closet.
words.zh: 架子是牆上用來放置物品的地方。

words.d: If you do something out of spite, you want to be mean.
words.e: He snuck into his sister’s room and stole her bag out of spite.
words.zh: 如果你出於惡意做某事，就是想表現刻薄。

words.d: Super means really good.
words.e: My dad said I did a super Job cleaning the house.
words.zh: 「超級」表示非常好。

words.d: If something is wet, it has water on it.
words.e: Since my dog was wet, he tried to shake all the water off his body.
words.zh: 如果某物是濕的，表示上面有水。

words.d: To abuse someone or something means to hurt them on purpose.
words.e: The mean man abused his dog when it barked too loudly.
words.zh: 虐待某人或某物是指故意傷害他們。

words.d: To afford something means you have enough money to pay for it.
words.e: I’ve been saving my money, so I can afford to buy a new bike.
words.zh: 負擔得起某物表示你有足夠的錢支付。

words.d: To bake means to cook food with heat.
words.e: My sister is a good cook. She bakes delicious cakes.
words.zh: 烘烤是指用加熱的方式烹調食物。

words.d: A bean is a plant seed that is good to eat.
words.e: There are many different kinds of beans to eat.
words.zh: 豆子是一種可食用的植物種子。

words.d: A candle is a stick of wax that is lit on fire for light or heat.
words.e: When the lights went out, we lit some candles.
words.zh: 蠟燭是一根點燃後可發光或發熱的蠟棒。

words.d: To convert something means to change it into something else.
words.e: The man converted his messy field into a garden of flowers.
words.zh: 轉換某物是指將其改變成別的東西。

words.d: A debt is an amount of money that a person owes.
words.e: I have not paid my gas bill. I owe a debt to the gas company.
words.zh: 債務是一個人所欠的金額。

words.d: To decrease something is to make it less than it was before.
words.e: Hiring more police officers has decreased crime in the city.
words.zh: 減少某物是指使其比原來更少。

words.d: A fault is a mistake.
words.e: It is my fault that the cat ran away. I left the door open.
words.zh: 過錯是指一個錯誤。

words.d: A fund is an amount of money that people have.
words.e: We all put money into our club’s fund.
words.zh: 基金是人們擁有的資金總額。

words.d: When someone is generous, they like to give things to people.
words.e: The generous man donated several new computers to our school.
words.zh: 當一個人慷慨時，他們喜歡給予他人東西。

words.d: An ingredient is something that is part of a food dish.
words.e: The main ingredients in cake are eggs, sugar and flour.
words.zh: 食材是組成一道菜餚的一部分。

words.d: To insist means to be firm in telling people what to do.
words.e: I insist that you try some of these cookies.
words.zh: 堅持意味著堅定地告訴別人該做什麼。

words.d: A mess is a condition that is not clean or neat.
words.e: Heather’s room was a complete mess.
words.zh: 混亂是一種不乾淨或不整齊的狀態。

words.d: Metal is a strong material people use to build things.
words.e: Steel is a common metal that is used to build buildings.
words.zh: 金屬是人們用來建造東西的堅固材料。

words.d: To monitor people or things is to watch them closely.
words.e: The teacher monitors the students when they take tests.
words.zh: 監視人或事物就是密切觀察他們。

words.d: To oppose something means to dislike it or act against it.
words.e: I want to be a police officer because I oppose crime.
words.zh: 反對某事意味著不喜歡它或採取行動對抗它。

words.d: If a person is passive, they do not take action to solve problems.
words.e: Marcie is so passive that she never solves her own problems.
words.zh: 如果一個人被動，他們不會採取行動解決問題。

words.d: A quantity is a certain amount of something.
words.e: I have a small quantity of milk in my glass.
words.zh: 數量是某物的特定份量。

words.d: To sue someone is to take them to court for something wrong they did.
words.e: I sued the company after I slipped on a banana peel in their hallway.
words.zh: 起訴某人是指因他們做錯事而將他們告上法庭。

words.d: When someone has anxiety, they have a lot of worries and fear.
words.e: When I have to climb to high places, I’m filled with anxiety.
words.zh: 當一個人感到焦慮時，他們有許多擔憂和恐懼。

words.d: An army is a large group of people who fight in wars.
words.e: The army protects all the people in the country.
words.zh: 軍隊是一大群參與戰爭的人。

words.d: A billion is a very large number: 1,000,000,000.
words.e: There are billions of stars in outer space.
words.zh: 十億是一個非常大的數字：1,000,000,000。

words.d: To carve means to cut into something.
words.e: My father usually carves the turkey for Thanksgiving.
words.zh: 雕刻意味著切入某物。

words.d: To consult someone means to ask them for help.
words.e: I will consult my accountant to find a way to pay for my bills.
words.zh: 諮詢某人意味著向他們尋求幫助。

words.d: An emergency is a time when someone needs help right away.
words.e: There is a huge fire in my house! This is an emergency!
words.zh: 緊急情況是指某人需要立即幫助的時刻。

words.d: When someone has good fortune, it means they have luck.
words.e: I have good fortune when I play cards.
words.zh: 當某人擁有好運時，意味著他們運氣很好。

words.d: To guarantee means to know something will happen.
words.e: I guarantee that the sun will come up in the morning.
words.zh: 保證是指確信某事會發生。

words.d: To hike means to walk in the mountains or forest.
words.e: I always bring plenty of equipment with me when I hike.
words.zh: 健行是指在山上或森林裡行走。

words.d: When something is initial, it is the first thing.
words.e: The initial step when writing a paper is to find a good topic.
words.zh: 當某事是初始的，它便是第一件事。

words.d: If something is intense, it is very strong.
words.e: The skunk made an intense odor that filled the air.
words.zh: 如果某事是強烈的，它便非常劇烈。

words.d: To lend something is to give it to someone for a short time.
words.e: My sister lost her pen, so I will lend her mine.
words.zh: 借出某物是指將它短暫交給他人。

words.d: The peak is the very top of a mountain.
words.e: There is snow on the peaks of those mountains.
words.zh: 山頂是山的最高處。

words.d: Potential means capable of being but not yet in existence.
words.e: I’ve thought of some potential problems with your idea.
words.zh: 潛力是指有能力存在但尚未實現。

words.d: When you have pride, you are happy with yourself.
words.e: I take pride in getting good grades.
words.zh: 當你感到驕傲時，你對自己感到滿意。

words.d: Proof is a fact that shows something is real.
words.e: They used his fingerprint as proof for the crime he committed.
words.zh: 證據是顯示某事為真的事實。

words.d: To quit something means to stop doing it.
words.e: I quit running because I got tired.
words.zh: 放棄某事意味著停止做它。

words.d: To spin is to turn around in circles.
words.e: The boy kept spinning until he fell down.
words.zh: 旋轉是指繞著圓圈轉動。

words.d: When people or things are tiny, they are very small.
words.e: A baby’s hand is tiny.
words.zh: 當人或事物是微小的，它們便非常小。

words.d: A tutor is someone who gives lessons in a certain subject.
words.e: My sister is bad at math. So my mother hired a tutor to help her.
words.zh: 導師是在某個科目授課的人。

words.d: If something is apparent, it is easy to see.
words.e: Her happiness was apparent from the smile on her face.
words.zh: 如果某事物是顯而易見的，就很容易看到。

words.d: When people are blind, they cannot see.
words.e: The blind man didn’t see the hole and almost fell in.
words.zh: 當人們失明時，他們無法看見。

words.d: To calculate is to find an answer using math.
words.e: I calculated how much money I would need to buy the car.
words.zh: 計算就是用數學找出答案。

words.d: To chat is to talk with someone.
words.e: Even though they were far apart, the couple chatted every day.
words.zh: 聊天就是與某人交談。

words.d: To commit to something is to promise to do it.
words.e: Seth wanted to go home, but he had committed to finishing the job.
words.zh: 對某事做出承諾就是答應去做它。

words.d: To compose something is to make it from smaller parts.
words.e: Tonya composed her report using many sources of information.
words.zh: 創作某事物就是將較小的部分組合起來。

words.d: A dormitory is a school building where students live.
words.e: I will move into the dormitory at the beginning of the school year.
words.zh: 宿舍是學生居住的學校建築。

words.d: To exhaust someone is to make them tired.
words.e: John exhausted himself by swimming all day.
words.zh: 使某人筋疲力盡就是讓他們感到疲倦。

words.d: A greenhouse is a small glass building that is used to grow plants.
words.e: We have a small greenhouse in our backyard where we grow plants.
words.zh: 溫室是用來種植植物的小型玻璃建築。

words.d: To ignore something is to act like you do not see or hear it.
words.e: I ignored the message he was making and kept studying.
words.zh: 忽略某事物就是假裝沒看到或沒聽到它。

words.d: If something is obvious, it is clear and easily seen.
words.e: It was obvious that he was tired. He kept falling asleep.
words.zh: 如果某事物是明顯的，它就是清楚且容易看見的。

words.d: Physics is a science that deals with energy and how it affects things.
words.e: In physics class, we used Newton’s Cradle to learn about energy.
words.zh: 物理學是研究能量及其如何影響事物的科學。

words.d: A portion of something is a part of it.
words.e: I only ate a small portion of the pizza.
words.zh: 某事物的一部分就是它的其中一份。

words.d: To remind someone is to tell them to remember to do something.
words.e: Nick’s dad reminded him to do his homework.
words.zh: 提醒某人就是告訴他們記得去做某事。

words.d: A secretary is a person who works in an office.
words.e: Rebecca asked her secretary to type a report.
words.zh: 秘書是在辦公室工作的人。

words.d: If something is severe, it is very bad or serious.
words.e: After hitting his hand with the hammer, Sam was in severe pain.
words.zh: 如果某事物是嚴重的，表示它非常糟糕或重大。

words.d: If someone has a talent, they are naturally able to do it well.
words.e: Maria has a talent for playing the piano.
words.zh: 如果某人有天賦，表示他們天生擅長做某事。

words.d: A thesis is an idea that needs to be proved.
words.e: She did not support her thesis very well.
words.zh: 論文是需要被證明的觀點。

words.d: A uniform is a piece of clothing worn by people of the same group.
words.e: All the members of our marching band wear matching uniforms.
words.zh: 制服是同一群體的人所穿的服裝。

words.d: Vision is the act of seeing.
words.e: The eye doctor tested my vision.
words.zh: 視覺是觀看的行為。

words.d: To absorb a liquid means to take it inside.
words.e: He used a sponge to absorb the water on the floor.
words.zh: 吸收液體意味著將其吸入內部。

words.d: A boss is a person in charge of other people at work.
words.e: My boss is a nice person.
words.zh: 老闆是在工作中負責管理他人的人。

words.d: A committee is a group of people who meet together to make decisions.
words.e: The school’s committee agreed on a new dress code for students.
words.zh: 委員會是一群聚在一起做決定的人。

words.d: A contract is a written agreement between two people.
words.e: The woman signed a contract when she bought the house.
words.zh: 合約是兩人之間的書面協議。

words.d: A crew is a group of workers.
words.e: My father has a crew. They help him build houses.
words.zh: 工作團隊是一群工作者。

words.d: To devote time to something means to spend a lot of time doing it.
words.e: She devotes two hours a day to playing the piano.
words.zh: 投入時間於某事意味著花費大量時間去做它。

words.d: To dig is to make a hole in the ground.
words.e: My dog digs in the yard so he can hide his bones.
words.zh: 挖掘是在地上挖洞。

words.d: To dine means to eat dinner.
words.e: The young couple dined at their home.
words.zh: 用餐意味著吃晚餐。

words.d: To donate is to give something to a charity or organization.
words.e: We donate money to Christmas charities every year.
words.zh: 捐贈是將某物給予慈善機構或組織。

words.d: If something is double, it is twice as much, or twice as many.
words.e: I paid almost double the amount for that shirt.
words.zh: 若某物是雙倍的，即數量或份量為兩倍。

words.d: To elevate something is to put it at a higher level.
words.e: The man elevated the picture so he could see it better.
words.zh: 提升某物即是將其置於更高層次。

words.d: A flavor is the taste of food or drinks.
words.e: The flavor of the ice cream was very good.
words.zh: 風味是指食物或飲品的味道。

words.d: A foundation is a group that provides money for research.
words.e: The foundation raised money to give scholarships to students.
words.zh: 基金會是提供研究經費的組織。

words.d: A generation is a group of people who live at the same time.
words.e: My grandparents are from a different generation than me.
words.zh: 世代是指同一時期生活的一群人。

words.d: A handle is the part of an object people hold while using it.
words.e: The pot is very hot. So pick it up by the handle.
words.zh: 把手是物品上供人握持的部分。

words.d: A layer covers over something or is between two things.
words.e: There was a layer of snow on the tops of the houses this morning.
words.zh: 覆蓋層覆蓋於某物之上或介於兩物之間。

words.d: Mud is soft, wet dirt.
words.e: My brother played rugby in the mud. Now he’s dirty.
words.zh: 泥巴是柔軟潮濕的泥土。

words.d: If something is smooth, it has no bumps.
words.e: The baby’s skin felt very smooth.
words.zh: 若某物光滑，則表面無凹凸。

words.d: Soil is the top layer of land on the Earth.
words.e: The boy planted flowers in the soil and watered them every day.
words.zh: 土壤是地球陸地的表層。

words.d: If people or things are unique, they are not like the others.
words.e: Her dog is unique. I’ve never seen one quite like it.
words.zh: 若人或事物獨特，即與眾不同。

words.d: A chamber is an old word for a bedroom.
words.e: The girl was tired. She went to her chamber fora nap.
words.zh: 寢室是臥室的古稱。

words.d: To deny something is to say it is not true.
words.e: The boy denied that he broke the window.
words.zh: 否認某事即是說其不屬實。

words.d: A document is an official piece of writing.
words.e: He was given an official document proving he was a citizen.
words.zh: 文件是正式的書面資料。

words.d: To emphasize is to give importance or attention to something.
words.e: She emphasized the key points by circling them in red ink.
words.zh: 強調即是賦予某事重要性或關注。

words.d: A fever is a high body temperature that people get when they are sick.
words.e: Lydia had a high fever so she didn’t go to school.
words.zh: 發燒是指人生病時體溫升高的狀態。

words.d: The flu is a type of sickness that makes you feel weak or your body hurt.
words.e: Since he had the flu, he felt miserable.
words.zh: 流感是一種會讓人感到虛弱或身體疼痛的疾病。

words.d: To freeze is to become very cold.
words.e: If you don’t wear your coat in winter, you will freeze.
words.zh: 凍結是指變得非常寒冷。

words.d: A gesture is a movement of the hands or body.
words.e: My teacher makes a lot of gestures when she speaks.
words.zh: 手勢是手部或身體的動作。

words.d: To interrupt is to briefly stop someone when they are doing something.
words.e: My mother interrupted me when I was trying to listen to music.
words.zh: 打斷是指在某人正在做某事時短暫地阻止他們。

words.d: To last is to continue or go on for an amount of time.
words.e: The football match lasted for nearly two hours.
words.zh: 持續是指繼續進行一段時間。

words.d: Likeness means the state of being like, or resemblance.
words.e: Michelle bears a strong likeness to her older sister Kate.
words.zh: 相似性是指與某物相似或相像的狀態。

words.d: Moreover means besides or in addition to something.
words.e: It’s cold outside. Moreover, the wind is very strong.
words.zh: 此外是指除了某事物之外，還有其他。

words.d: A perspective is the way you think about something.
words.e: The man’s speech gave me a new perspective on our country.
words.zh: 觀點是你對某件事的看法。

words.d: When something is rational, it is normal or practical.
words.e: It is hard to think in a rational way when you are scared.
words.zh: 當某事物是理性的，它便是正常或實際的。

words.d: To recover is to go back to normal after something bad happens.
words.e: I hope the city will recover soon after the flood.
words.zh: 康復是指在壞事發生後恢復正常。

words.d: To rely on something or someone is to trust or depend on them.
words.e: The boy relied on his older brother to help him.
words.zh: 依賴某人或某物是指信任或依靠他們。

words.d: To shock people is to surprise them.
words.e: The man was shocked by the news.
words.zh: 使人震驚是指讓他們感到驚訝。

words.d: When people are shy, they are nervous around people strange to them.
words.e: The girl was too shy to try out for the play.
words.zh: 當人們害羞時，他們在面對陌生人時會感到緊張。

words.d: To stare at something is to look at it for a long time.
words.e: The young couple stared into each other’s eyes.
words.zh: 凝視某物是指長時間地看著它。

words.d: Thus means as a result or for that reason.
words.e: The sun was shining. Thus, I wore my sunglasses.
words.zh: 因此表示結果或基於該理由。

words.d: An aim is a goal someone wants to make happen.
words.e: My aim is to become a helicopter pilot.
words.zh: 目標是指某人希望實現的目的。

words.d: To attach is to put two things together.
words.e: I attached the socks to the clothesline to dry.
words.zh: 附著是將兩個物體結合在一起。

words.d: To bet is to risk money on the result of a game or a business.
words.e: How much will you bet that your horse will win?
words.zh: 賭博是冒著金錢風險參與遊戲或商業結果的行為。

words.d: A carriage is a vehicle pulled by a horse.
words.e: We took a carriage ride in the park.
words.zh: 馬車是由馬匹拉動的交通工具。

words.d: If something is classic, it is typical.
words.e: The athlete made a classic mistake-he started running too soon.
words.zh: 若某事物是經典的，則代表其具有典型性。

words.d: To commute is to travel a long distance to get to work.
words.e: I usually commute to work on the train.
words.zh: 通勤是長途跋涉前往工作地點。

words.d: To confirm is to make sure something is correct.
words.e: Winning the game confirmed that James was a good player.
words.zh: 確認是確保某事物正確無誤。

words.d: To criticize is to say you do not like someone or something.
words.e: He criticized his wife forspending too much money.
words.zh: 批評是表達不喜歡某人或某事。

words.d: To differ is to not be the same as another person or thing.
words.e: I differ from my brother: he is short, while I am tall.
words.zh: 差異是指與他人或他物不同。

words.d: An expense is the money that people spend on something.
words.e: She wrote down all the expenses for her trip.
words.zh: 開銷是人們花費在某事物上的金錢。

words.d: If something is formal, it is done in an official way.
words.e: It was a formal dinner, so we wore our best clothes.
words.zh: 若某事物是正式的，則是以官方方式進行。

words.d: Height is how tall someone or something is.
words.e: My height is 168 centimeters.
words.zh: 高度是指某人或某物的垂直尺寸。

words.d: To invent something is to create something that never existed before.
words.e: My grandfather has invented some interesting things.
words.zh: 發明某物是創造從未存在過的事物。

words.d: If someone is junior in their job, they do not have a lot of power.
words.e: When she started at the company, she was only a junior manager.
words.zh: 若某人在工作中資歷較淺，則其權力較小。

words.d: Labor is the act of doing or making something.
words.e: Building the house took a lot of labor.
words.zh: 勞動是執行或創造某事的行為。

words.d: A mechanic is someone who fixes vehicles or machines.
words.e: We took the car to the mechanic to be fixed.
words.zh: 技工是修理車輛或機器的人。

words.d: If something is prime, it is the most important one.
words.e: Dirty air is a prime cause of illness.
words.zh: 若某事是首要的，則表示它是最重要的。

words.d: To shift to something is to move into a new place or direction.
words.e: He shifted to the other side of the table to eat his breakfast.
words.zh: 轉向某事即移動到新的位置或方向。

words.d: A signal is a sound or action that tells someone to do something.
words.e: The coach blew his whistle as a signal to begin the game.
words.zh: 信號是告知某人做某事的声音或動作。

words.d: When people are sincere, they tell the truth.
words.e: He sounded sincere when he apologized to me.
words.zh: 當人們真誠時，他們會說實話。

words.d: Ability is the quality of a person being able to do something well.
words.e: His swimming abilities let him cross the entire lake.
words.zh: 能力是一個人能做好某事的特質。

words.d: Agriculture is the growing of food and animals.
words.e: The farmer studied agriculture in college.
words.zh: 農業是種植糧食與飼養動物。

words.d: A cartoon is a funny drawing.
words.e: Sometimes, people draw cartoons for the newspaper.
words.zh: 卡通是一種有趣的圖畫。

words.d: The ceiling is the top of a room.
words.e: He painted the ceiling with a special roller.
words.zh: 天花板是房間的頂部。

words.d: To convince someone means to make them sure of something.
words.e: She convinced me to buy the house.
words.zh: 說服某人意味著使他們確信某事。

words.d: When you are curious about something, you want to know more about it.
words.e: I opened up the clock because I was curious about how it worked.
words.zh: 當你對某事好奇時，你會想了解更多。

words.d: To delay means to wait to do something.
words.e: I was delayed at the airport for over two hours.
words.zh: 延遲意味著等待再做某事。

words.d: A diary is a book in which people write their personal experiences.
words.e: I do not let anybody read my diary.
words.zh: 日記是人們記錄個人經歷的書本。

words.d: An element of something is a particular part of it.
words.e: Tackling an opponent is Johnny’s favorite element of American footbal.
words.zh: 某事的元素是它的特定部分。

words.d: When people have faith in something, they believe in it and trust it.
words.e: The sick girl had faith in doctors. She knew they would make her better.
words.zh: 當人們對某事物懷有信心時，他們會相信並信任它。

words.d: Grain is food crops such as wheat, corn, rice or oats.
words.e: The farmer planted two fields of grain this year.
words.zh: 穀物是指小麥、玉米、稻米或燕麥等糧食作物。

words.d: To greet someone means to meet and welcome them.
words.e: When my friend came over, I greeted him at the door.
words.zh: 問候某人意味著與他們見面並表示歡迎。

words.d: To investigate means to search for something or learn about it.
words.e: The detective went to investigate the crime.
words.zh: 調查是指搜尋某事物或了解它。

words.d: Joy is a feeling you get when you are really happy.
words.e: I love baseball. I feel joy when I play.
words.zh: 喜悅是當你非常快樂時所感受到的情感。

words.d: A label is a tag that tells about something.
words.e: The label on the back of your shirt will tell you what size it is.
words.zh: 標籤是用來說明某事物的標記。

words.d: A monk is a religious person who lives a simple life.
words.e: The monks knew a lot about religion.
words.zh: 僧侶是過著簡樸生活的宗教人士。

words.d: When something is odd, it is unusual.
words.e: Her cat is odd. It walks on two feet.
words.zh: 當某事物奇怪時，它就是不尋常的。

words.d: To pause means to stop doing something for a while.
words.e: Since she was so hungry, she paused to make a snack.
words.zh: 暫停是指暫時停止做某件事。

words.d: A priest is a person trained to perform religious duties.
words.e: The priest taught us about God.
words.zh: 牧師是受過訓練執行宗教職責的人。

words.d: A profession is a person’s job.
words.e: He loved sailing, so he chose to work on ships as a profession.
words.zh: 職業是指一個人的工作。

words.d: To adopt someone is to make them as a part of one’s own family.
words.e: The girl was adopted by the couple when she was three.
words.zh: 收養某人是指將他們視為自己家庭的一員。

words.d: To beg is to ask for something one really wants.
words.e: The man begged for some money.
words.zh: 乞求是指請求自己非常想要的東西。

words.d: If A is beyond B, A is farther away.
words.e: John’s house is beyond that lake.
words.zh: 如果A在B之外，則A距離更遠。

words.d: A costume is a set of clothes people wear for a particular occasion.
words.e: The woman wore a mask with her costume.
words.zh: 服裝是人們為特定場合所穿的一套衣服。

words.d: To exclaim is to say something loudly, usually due to being excited.
words.e: “Look at her dress!” Sara exclaimed.
words.zh: 驚呼是指大聲說話，通常是因為興奮。

words.d: To extend is to stretch out or reach.
words.e: The boy extended his hand to catch the ball.
words.zh: 延伸是指伸展或觸及。

words.d: A fool is someone who makes unwise choices.
words.e: The girl was a fool for playing too close to the water.
words.zh: 愚人是指做出不明智選擇的人。

words.d: To forbid is to tell someone they cannot do something.
words.e: My father forbids watching TV while we’re eating dinner.
words.zh: 禁止是指告訴某人不能做某事。

words.d: To illustrate is to show something by drawing a picture.
words.e: The executive illustrated the decreasing profits of the company.
words.zh: 說明是指透過畫圖來展示某事。

words.d: Indeed means truly or really.
words.e: The birthday party was indeed fun last night.
words.zh: 確實意指真實或真正。

words.d: To interpret is to explain what something means.
words.e: The woman interpreted what her co-worker was trying to say.
words.zh: 解釋是指說明某事物的意義。

words.d: If people do something kindly, they do it in a nice way.
words.e: The stranger kindly cared for the hurt man.
words.zh: 若人們友善地做某事，他們是以和善的方式進行。

words.d: A motive is the reason someone does something.
words.e: His motive for studying so hard is to get into a good college.
words.zh: 動機是某人做某事的原因。

words.d: A nest is a place where a bird lays its eggs.
words.e: The bird laid her eggs in the nest that she made.
words.zh: 鳥巢是鳥類產卵的地方。

words.d: The origin of someone or something is where they come from.
words.e: The origin of the honey that we eat is from a beehive.
words.zh: 某人或某物的起源是他們來自何處。

words.d: A reception is a party to welcome a person or celebrate an event.
words.e: We all danced and had a good time at the wedding reception.
words.zh: 歡迎會是為了歡迎某人或慶祝活動而舉辦的派對。

words.d: To reject is to refuse something because you do not want it.
words.e: The girl rejected the broken cup.
words.zh: 拒絕是指因為不想要而拒絕某事物。

words.d: Silence is complete quiet.
words.e: The man asked for silence while he worked on the problem.
words.zh: 寂靜是完全的安靜。

words.d: A stream is a small river.
words.e: The boy caught a fish in the stream.
words.zh: 溪流是一條小河。

words.d: Tone is the sound of someone’s voice. It shows how they feel.
words.e: My father’s tone told me I had broken the rule.
words.zh: 語調是指一個人說話的聲音，能表達他們的情緒。

words.d: To accomplish something means to finish it.
words.e: He accomplished his goal of running ten miles.
words.zh: 完成某事意味著將它做完。

words.d: To approve of something means you like it or are happy about it.
words.e: Her co-workers approved her new plan.
words.zh: 贊同某事代表你喜歡它或對它感到滿意。

words.d: Approximate means to be close to an exact amount, number or time.
words.e: My approximate height is two meters.
words.zh: 近似是指接近某個精確的數量、數字或時間。

words.d: A barrier is something that is in your way.
words.e: The Great Wall was a barrier between China and its enemies.
words.zh: 障礙物是擋在你面前的東西。

words.d: To detect something means to notice or find something.
words.e: The boy ran to the kitchen when he detected the smell of cookies.
words.zh: 偵測某事意味著注意到或發現某事物。

words.d: A duty is something that a person has to do.
words.e: It is parents’ duty to take care of their children.
words.zh: 責任是一個人必須要做的事情。

words.d: When something is elementary, it is the first or most simple thing.
words.e: Children go to elementary school before high school.
words.zh: 當某事物是基礎的，它指的是最初或最簡單的部分。

words.d: A failure happens when you do not do something right.
words.e: My cooking ended in failure because I burned the food.
words.zh: 失敗發生在你沒有正確完成某事的時候。

words.d: When something is gradual, it happens slowly.
words.e: Children learn to read at a gradual pace. They do not learn right away.
words.zh: 當某事物是漸進的，它代表緩慢發生。

words.d: An immigrant is a person who moves to a different country.
words.e: My parents were immigrants. They came from Poland.
words.zh: 移民是指移居到另一個國家的人。

words.d: To insert something means to put it in something else.
words.e: The mailman inserted the letter into the mailbox.
words.zh: 插入某物意味著把它放進另一個東西裡面。

words.d: An instant is a very short amount of time.
words.e: A microwave oven cooks food in an instant.
words.zh: 瞬間是非常短暫的一段時間。

words.d: Poverty is the state of being poor.
words.e: Poverty is a problem in many countries around the world.
words.zh: 貧窮是指貧困的狀態。

words.d: To pretend means to make believe something is real.
words.e: The boy liked to pretend he was a king.
words.zh: 假裝意味著把某事物當作是真的來表現。

words.d: A person’s rank is their place in an order of people.
words.e: The man got to the rank of captain in the navy.
words.zh: 一個人的階級是指他在人群順序中的位置。

words.d: Recognition is getting praise from other people.
words.e: The hero got recognition for his brave deed.
words.zh: 認可就是獲得他人的讚揚。

words.d: To refrigerate something means to make it cold.
words.e: Grocery stores refrigerate fruit to make it last long.
words.zh: 冷藏某物意指使其變冷。

words.d: Rent is the money people pay to someone to live in a certain place.
words.e: To live in this house, I have to pay rent at the start of each month.
words.zh: 租金是人們為居住在某處而支付的費用。

words.d: To retire is to leave a job, usually because of old age.
words.e: My father is sixty-five years old. He is about to retire from work.
words.zh: 退休是指離開工作，通常是因年邁。

words.d: A statistic is a number that tells a fact about something.
words.e: The statistics showed that we did just as well this year as last year.
words.zh: 統計數字是揭示某事物真相的數據。

words.d: An astronaut is a person that goes into outer space.
words.e: The astronaut was walking on the moon.
words.zh: 太空人是指進入外太空的人。

words.d: When you are awake, you are not asleep.
words.e: Sometimes I lay awake in bed because I am not tired.
words.zh: 當你清醒時，你並非在睡覺。

words.d: When you have courage, you are not afraid.
words.e: The man had the courage to touch the lion.
words.zh: 當你有勇氣時，你並不害怕。

words.d: To float is to move on top of water without sinking.
words.e: The boy’s toy boat floated in the pool.
words.zh: 漂浮是指在水面上移動而不下沉。

words.d: To grant something is to allow someone to have it.
words.e: The teacher granted us a break after studying hard all day.
words.zh: 准許某物是指允許某人擁有它。

words.d: Gravity is the force that makes things fall to Earth.
words.e: There is no gravity in space.
words.zh: 重力是使物體墜向地球的力量。

words.d: A jewel is a beautiful stone that is worth a lot of money.
words.e: A diamond is one of the most expensive jewels in the world.
words.zh: 寶石是一種美麗且價值高昂的石頭。

words.d: A miner is a person who works in a mine.
words.e: The miner was looking for gold.
words.zh: 礦工是在礦場工作的人。

words.d: A mineral is a type of substance found in the Earth.
words.e: Rocks are made up of different kinds of minerals.
words.zh: 礦物是存在於地球中的一種物質。

words.d: To participate is to take part in something.
words.e: The students participated in the school play.
words.zh: 參與即是指加入某件事。

words.d: Permission means the act of allowing the doing of something.
words.e: I have permission to drive my mom’s car.
words.zh: 許可意指允許做某事的行為。

words.d: To pour a liquid means to make it come out of a container.
words.e: I poured some milk into my sister’s cup.
words.zh: 倒出液體是指讓它從容器中流出。

words.d: Someone or something’s presence is the fact they are there.
words.e: The presence of dark clouds meant it would rain.
words.zh: 某人或某物的存在，即是指他們在場的事實。

words.d: If a material is raw, it is natural and has not been processed.
words.e: The company dumped raw sewage into the river.
words.zh: 若材料是未經加工的，即表示它是天然的且尚未處理過。

words.d: A satellite is something that is sent into space to get information.
words.e: The satellite was traveling around the earth.
words.zh: 衛星是指被發射到太空中以獲取資訊的物體。

words.d: The scale of something is its size, especially when it is very large.
words.e: I was surprised by the scale of the buildings in the downtown area.
words.zh: 某事物的規模是指其大小，尤其當它非常巨大時。

words.d: To skip something is to not do it.
words.e: He skipped work to get more sleep.
words.zh: 跳過某事即是不去做它。

words.d: To stretch is to make your arms or legs reach out.
words.e: She stretched her body before exercising.
words.zh: 伸展是指將手臂或腿向外伸長。

words.d: A telescope is a tool people use to look at the stars.
words.e: With a telescope, you can see the moon and stars easily.
words.zh: 望遠鏡是人們用來觀看星星的工具。

words.d: When something is underground, it is below the surface of the Earth.
words.e: Subway trains travel underground.
words.zh: 當某物在地下時，即是指它位於地球表面之下。

words.d: An alarm is something that warns people of danger.
words.e: When the students heard the fire alarm, they left the building.
words.zh: 警報是用來警告人們危險的東西。

words.d: When people or things are apart, they are not close together.
words.e: The couple decided to live apart from each other.
words.zh: 當人或事物分開時，即是指它們彼此不靠近。

words.d: To arrest someone means to catch them for doing something bad.
words.e: The man was arrested for breaking the law.
words.zh: 逮捕某人是指因為他們做了壞事而將其抓獲。

words.d: An award is a prize someone gets for doing something well.
words.e: He got an award for having the best grades in class.
words.zh: 獎項是指某人因表現優異而獲得的獎勵。

words.d: A breed is a group of animals within a species.
words.e: I like small dog breeds, such as terriers.
words.zh: 品種是指同一物種內的一群動物。

words.d: A bucket is a round containerto put things in.
words.e: I filled the bucket with water.
words.zh: 水桶是一種圓形容器，用來裝東西。

words.d: A contest is a game or a race.
words.e: The girls had a contest to see who could jump higher.
words.zh: 比賽是一種遊戲或競賽。

words.d: To convict someone means to prove that they did a bad thing.
words.e: He was convicted of the crime and sent to jail.
words.zh: 定罪某人是指證明他們做了壞事。

words.d: A garage is the part of a house where people put their cars.
words.e: My car does not get dirty because I keep it in the garage.
words.zh: 車庫是房子的一部分，人們用來停放汽車。

words.d: A journalist is a person who writes news stories.
words.e: The journalist took notes for a story he was writing.
words.zh: 記者是撰寫新聞報導的人。

words.d: A pup is a young dog.
words.e: All the girl wanted for her birthday was a pup.
words.zh: 小狗是指年幼的狗。

words.d: To qualify is to get, or to be declared, adequate or good enough.
words.e: He qualified to go to the final match by beating the opponent.
words.zh: 合格是指達到或被認定為足夠好或符合標準。

words.d: To repair something is to fix it.
words.e: I repaired the flat tire on my car.
words.zh: 修理某物是指將其修復。

words.d: To resume something means to start it again after taking a break.
words.e: I put the newspaper down to eat breakfast. Then I resumed reading.
words.zh: 重新開始某件事是指在休息後再次啟動。

words.d: To rob a person or place is to take their property by using force.
words.e: A thief has robbed me of my passport.
words.zh: 搶劫某人或某地是指用武力奪取其財物。

words.d: To slip means to slide and fall down.
words.e: The man slipped on the wet floor.
words.zh: 滑倒是指滑行並跌倒。

words.d: Somewhat means to some degree, but not to a large degree.
words.e: James was somewhat upset when he had to move some boxes.
words.zh: 稍微是指某種程度，但並非很大程度。

words.d: When something is stable, it will not fall over.
words.e: The chair is stable. Its legs are strong.
words.zh: 當某物穩定時，它不會倒下。

words.d: A tissue is a soft piece of paper people use to wipe their noses.
words.e: There was a box of tissue on the table.
words.zh: 紙巾是人們用來擦鼻子的柔軟紙張。

words.d: A yard is the ground just outside of a person’s house.
words.e: The girls jumped rope in the yard.
words.zh: 庭院是指房屋外緊鄰的地面。

words.d: If two things or people are alike, they are similar in some way.
words.e: People often think my sister and I look alike.
words.zh: 若兩件事物或兩個人相似，則表示它們在某些方面相同。

words.d: To annoy someone means to bother them.
words.e: The flies buzzing around John’s head annoyed him very much.
words.zh: 惹惱某人意味著打擾他們。

words.d: Architecture is the style or way a building is made.
words.e: The architecture of the church is amazing.
words.zh: 建築風格是指建築物的建造方式或樣式。

words.d: If something is artificial, it is made to look like something natural.
words.e: That soccer field has artificial grass, but it looks real.
words.zh: 若某物是人造的，則表示它被製成模仿自然物的樣子。

words.d: A chain is a series of connected loops often used to keep things in place.
words.e: The scary dog was secured with a chain.
words.zh: 鏈條是一系列相連的環，常用來固定物品。

words.d: If something is distinct, it is easily noticed or different from other things.
words.e: That girl has distinct pink hair.
words.zh: 若某物是獨特的，則表示它容易引人注意或與其他事物不同。

words.d: To distinguish is to recognize differences between things.
words.e: The twins look exactly the same. It is hard to distinguish between them.
words.zh: 區分是指辨識事物之間的差異。

words.d: Dust is very tiny pieces of dirt or other matter.
words.e: In the old house, the dust was thick on the floor and chairs.
words.zh: 灰塵是非常微小的污垢或其他物質顆粒。

words.d: Excitement means to feel a lot of happiness about something.
words.e: Did you see the excitement in her when the team scored?
words.zh: 興奮意味著對某事感到非常快樂。

words.d: To heal means to make a part of the body healthy again after injury.
words.e: After my broken arm heals, I can play baseball again.
words.zh: 癒合是指使身體部位在受傷後恢復健康。

words.d: To inherit is to get something from someone who has died.
words.e: She inherited her mother’s gold earrings.
words.zh: 繼承是指從已故者那裡獲得某物。

words.d: A manner is the way someone does something.
words.e: His manner of not looking at someone while speaking, is a bit rude.
words.zh: 方式是指某人做某事的方法。

words.d: Something mounts when it becomes stronger over time.
words.e: His fear mounted as the monster crawled out from under his bed.
words.zh: 某事物增強是指它隨著時間變得更加強烈。

words.d: A roof is the outside top part of a building.
words.e: The roof was covered with snow.
words.zh: 屋頂是建築物外部頂端的部分。

words.d: A shortage is a lack of something you need or want.
words.e: Since there was a shortage of food, people were hungry.
words.zh: 短缺是指缺乏你所需或想要的東西。

words.d: If something is solid, it is made of firm material.
words.e: The safe looked to be quite solid.
words.zh: 如果某物是固體的，它是由堅實的材料製成的。

words.d: A stock is a stored amount of something.
words.e: They keep a stock of rice so they’ll always have something to eat.
words.zh: 庫存是指儲存起來的某種東西的數量。

words.d: A substance is any specific material or mixture of materials.
words.e: Water is an important substance for all living things.
words.zh: 物質是指任何特定的材料或材料的混合物。

words.d: A tomb is a grave where a dead person is buried.
words.e: Their whole family is buried inside the tomb.
words.zh: 墳墓是埋葬死者的地方。

words.d: A wound is an injury to the body.
words.e: He had many wounds after the car accident.
words.zh: 傷口是身體受到的損傷。

words.d: A bath is water in a tub. People take a bath to get clean.
words.e: After playing in the dirt, the boy took a bath.
words.zh: 浴缸裡的水稱為洗澡水。人們洗澡是為了清潔身體。

words.d: To bend is to move something so it is not straight.
words.e: Lee bent over and picked up the paper on the ground.
words.zh: 彎曲是指移動某物使其不再筆直。

words.d: To chew is to move your mouth to break up food.
words.e: I always chew my food carefully before swallowing it.
words.zh: 咀嚼是指動用嘴巴將食物咬碎。

words.d: When a person is disabled, they cannot do what a normal person can do.
words.e: The disabled man used a wheelchair to move around.
words.zh: 當一個人殘障時，他們無法做到正常人能做的事。

words.d: If something is fantastic, it is really good.
words.e: The student did a fantastic job on his project and got an award.
words.zh: 如果某物很奇妙，那它真的很好。

words.d: Fiction is a story that is not true.
words.e: I enjoy reading works of fiction because they are very entertaining.
words.zh: 虛構故事是指不真實的故事。

words.d: A flag is a piece of colored cloth that represents something.
words.e: Our country has a beautiful flag.
words.zh: 旗幟是一塊有顏色的布，代表某種意義。

words.d: To inspect is to look at something carefully.
words.e: The mechanic inspected our car to see if it had any problems.
words.zh: 檢查是指仔細觀察某物。

words.d: A journal is a type of magazine that deals with an academic subject.
words.e: Mi-young was busy working on an article for an art journal.
words.zh: 期刊是一種處理學術主題的雜誌。

words.d: A liquid is a substance that is neither solid nor gas.
words.e: Water is the most important liquid for life.
words.zh: 液體是既非固體也非氣體的物質。

words.d: To marvel at something is to feel surprise and interest in it.
words.e: We marveled at her excellent piano playing.
words.zh: 對某事感到驚奇，是指對它產生驚訝與興趣。

words.d: A nutrient is something that a living thing needs to keep it alive.
words.e: Vegetables are full of important nutrients.
words.zh: 營養素是生物維持生命所需的物質。

words.d: To overcome a problem is to successfully fix it.
words.e: She overcame her shyness and spoke in front of the class.
words.zh: 克服問題是指成功解決它。

words.d: To recall something is to remember it.
words.e: She was trying to recall what she had told her friend.
words.zh: 回憶某事是指記起它。

words.d: To regret something is to wish that it didn’t happen.
words.e: I regret that I was mean to my sister.
words.zh: 後悔某事是指希望它未曾發生。

words.d: A soul is a person’s spirit.
words.e: Some people believe that the soul lives after the body dies.
words.zh: 靈魂是一個人的精神。

words.d: When something is sufficient, you have enough of it.
words.e: After eating a sufficient amount of food, I left the table.
words.zh: 當某物足夠時，你擁有充足的數量。

words.d: Surgery is medical treatment when the doctor cuts open your body.
words.e: I needed surgery to repair my leg after the accident.
words.zh: 手術是醫生切開你身體進行治療的醫療行為。

words.d: If something is tough, it is difficult.
words.e: The man passed his driving test even though it was very tough.
words.zh: 如果某事很艱難，代表它不容易。

words.d: A tube is a pipe through which water or air passes.
words.e: The pile of tubes was going to be put in the ground.
words.zh: 管子是讓水或空氣通過的管道。

words.d: To admit something means to say that it is true.
words.e: I had to admit that I stole his idea.
words.zh: 承認某事是指說出它是真實的。

words.d: A bin is a container that holds things.
words.e: He put his trash in the bin.
words.zh: 容器是盛裝物品的器具。

words.d: A bowl is a deep, round dish that holds food or liquid.
words.e: I ate a bowl of cereal for breakfast.
words.zh: 碗是一種深而圓的器皿，用來盛裝食物或液體。

words.d: A cabin is a small house made of wood.
words.e: My grandfather owns a small cabin in the country.
words.zh: 小屋是指用木頭建造的小房子。

words.d: Cash is money in the form of paper or coins.
words.e: I was able to save up enough cash to buy my mother a gift.
words.zh: 現金是以紙幣或硬幣形式存在的錢。

words.d: A criminal is a person who does something against the law.
words.e: The police made sure the criminal couldn’t move his hands.
words.zh: 罪犯是指做出違法行為的人。

words.d: A dozen is a group of twelve things.
words.e: Mom brought home a dozen donuts for us as a treat.
words.zh: 一打是指十二個物品組成的單位。

words.d: An elder is a person who is older than the people around them.
words.e: Uncle Ray is my elder so I always try to be respectful.
words.zh: 長輩是指比周圍的人年齡更大的人。

words.d: If something is facial, it is related to a person’s face.
words.e: I could tell from his facial features that he was angry.
words.zh: 若某物是臉部的，則與人的臉有關。

words.d: A fence is a structure around a house or field.
words.e: The farmer put up a fence so that no one could walk on her field.
words.zh: 圍欄是圍繞房屋或田地的結構。

words.d: To inspire means to make a person want to do something.
words.e: I was inspired to write a poem after watching the sunrise.
words.zh: 啟發是指讓人想要去做某事。

words.d: When something is mere, it is small or not important.
words.e: We lost the game by a mere two points.
words.zh: 當某事僅是微不足道時，表示它很小或不重要。

words.d: If something is neat, it is very clean or organized well.
words.e: My sister always keeps her bedroom very neat.
words.zh: 若某物整潔，則表示非常乾淨或整理有序。

words.d: An occasion is a time when something important happens.
words.e: Her graduation was an occasion to have a good time.
words.zh: 場合是指重要事件發生的時刻。

words.d: A penalty is a punishment, given when someone breaks a rule or law.
words.e: I had to pay a $100 penalty for parking in the wrong spot.
words.zh: 罰則是當有人違反規則或法律時給予的懲罰。

words.d: When someone is rude, they are mean and not polite.
words.e: The children at the bus stop were very rude.
words.zh: 當某人粗魯時，他們表現得刻薄且不禮貌。

words.d: To settle a problem means to end it by finding a solution.
