words.d: A strap is a thin long piece of fabric used to fasten, carry, or hold something.
words.e: She put the strap of her purse over her shoulder and walked out of the door.
words.zh: 帶子是一條用於繫緊、攜帶或固定物品的細長布料。

words.d: A tangle is something or many things twisted together.
words.e: The laces of his shoes were in such a tangle that he could not untie them.
words.zh: 纏結是指許多東西糾結在一起的狀態。

words.d: Vanity is excessive pride or love of one’s own appearance or things one has done.
words.e: Her vanity won’t allow her to pass a mirror without looking at herself.
words.zh: 虛榮是對自身外貌或成就的過度驕傲或喜愛。

words.d: To vie for something means to compete against others for it.
words.e: The three boys vied for the prize in the chemistry contest.
words.zh: 爭奪某物意味著與他人競爭以獲得它。

words.d: If something or someone is vulgar, then they are rude or lacking in style.
words.e: Her vulgar behavior got her into trouble with her parents.
words.zh: 若某人或某事粗俗，則其無禮或缺乏品味。

words.d: An attic is a room just below a house’s roof.
words.e: There were two windows in their attic.
words.zh: 閣樓是位於房屋屋頂下方的房間。

words.d: A chunk is a thick, solid piece of something.
words.e: When they broke open the rock, they saw it was filled with chunks of gold.
words.zh: 厚塊是某物的厚實固體部分。

words.d: If something is civic, then it is related to a town or city, especially its government.
words.e: Most of the important civic buildings are located downtown.
words.zh: 若某事物是市政的，則其與城鎮或城市（尤其其政府）相關。

words.d: A descent is a movement downwards.
words.e: During the space shuttle’s descent, the Earth became larger and larger.
words.zh: 下降是指向下的移動。

words.d: A din is loud, unpleasant, and extended noise.
words.e: The din of the rusty machinery made the factory a horrible place to work.
words.zh: 喧囂是指大聲、令人不悅且持續的噪音。

words.d: To dissatisfy someone means to fail to please them.
words.e: He was dissatisfied with his meal.
words.zh: 使某人不滿意味著未能取悅他們。

words.d: A fuss is excited or annoyed behavior that is not useful in any way.
words.e: The child made an awful fuss because she didn’t want to go to bed.
words.zh: 大驚小怪是指激動或惱怒的行為，且毫無用處。

words.d: If food is gourmet, then it is nicer and more expensive than regular food.
words.e: In order to get a gourmet meal, you have to eat at an expensive restaurant.
words.zh: 如果食物是美食級的，那麼它比普通食物更精緻且更昂貴。

words.d: If something happens hence, then it happens as a result of something.
words.e: John forgot the key. Hence, we couldn’t open the door.
words.zh: 如果某事因此發生，則表示它是某事的結果。

words.d: If something is intrinsic, then it is related to the basic nature of that thing.
words.e: Paper money has no intrinsic value. It is useful simply because society says it is.
words.zh: 如果某物是本質的，那麼它與該事物的基本性質相關。

words.d: A kettle is a large metal pot used for boiling liquids or cooking food.
words.e: The soup was being cooked in a large kettle.
words.zh: 水壺是一種用於煮沸液體或烹飪食物的大型金屬鍋。

words.d: A ministry is a government department.
words.e: The Ministry of Trade is responsible for taxing imports and exports.
words.zh: 部會是政府的一個部門。

words.d: An ordeal is a bad experience.
words.e: Driving in the snowstorm was an ordeal she’d never forget.
words.zh: 磨難是一種糟糕的經歷。

words.d: If someone is outspoken, then they are not afraid to say what they think.
words.e: She was an outspoken critic about the new banking laws.
words.zh: 如果有人直言不諱，那麼他們不怕說出自己的想法。

words.d: To overwork someone means to make them tired with too much work.
words.e: After working for three weeks with only one day of rest, Judy was overworked.
words.zh: 使某人過勞意味著用過多的工作讓他們疲憊。

words.d: If something is particular, then it is a single, important part of a group of things.
words.e: The dress shop didn’t have the particular dress she was looking for.
words.zh: 如果某物是特定的，那麼它是一組事物中單一且重要的部分。

words.d: If something is pungent, then it tastes or smells strong and sharp.
words.e: The garlic made the soup taste so pungent that the chef wouldn’t serve it.
words.zh: 如果某物是辛辣的，那麼它的味道或氣味強烈而刺鼻。

words.d: To snore means to make a loud noise each time a sleeping person breathes.
words.e: It was impossible to get to sleep because my husband snored.
words.zh: 打鼾是指睡覺的人每次呼吸時發出很大的噪音。

words.d: If something is done soundly, then it is done in the best or most complete way.
words.e: The nearest opponent was ten meters behind. She won the race soundly.
words.zh: 若某事做得徹底，即是以最佳或最完整的方式完成。

words.d: A superintendent is a person who runs a certain department or building.
words.e: The superintendent decided to close the schools because of the weather.
words.zh: 主管是負責管理某個部門或建築物的人。

words.d: To alternate means to switch back and forth between two things.
words.e: The best exercise alternates muscle and heart strengthening.
words.zh: 交替是指在兩件事之間來回切換。

words.d: If someone is apologetic, they are sorry about something.
words.e: The boy felt apologetic after losing his sister’s favorite toy.
words.zh: 若某人感到抱歉，即表示他們對某事心懷歉意。

words.d: If something or someone is benign, they do not hurt anyone.
words.e: Many spiders look scary, but most are actually benign.
words.zh: 若某事物或某人是良性的，則不會傷害任何人。

words.d: To char means to burn something so that it turns black.
words.e: While Frank went inside to get the mustard, he accidentally charred the hotdogs.
words.zh: 燒焦是指將某物燒至變黑。

words.d: To clarify means to make something easier to understand by explaining it.
words.e: Drew tried to clarify all the functions of Michelle’s new computer.
words.zh: 釐清是指透過解釋使某事更容易理解。

words.d: When someone’s actions are dogged, they try hard to continue something.
words.e: Her dad bought her a new jacket after her dogged requests for one.
words.zh: 當某人的行動堅持不懈時，他們會努力持續進行某事。

words.d: Distress is the feeling of being upset or worried.
words.e: Failing a class caused the student a lot of distress.
words.zh: 苦惱是感到沮喪或擔憂的情緒。

words.d: To ensue means to happen after something.
words.e: After a few minutes of lightening, thunder ensued.
words.zh: 隨後發生是指某事之後接著發生。

words.d: To gasp means to make a noise by quickly breathing in when surprised.
words.e: John always gasps when watching a scary movie.
words.zh: 倒抽一口氣是指在驚訝時快速吸氣而發出聲音。

words.d: To negotiate means to try to make an agreement through discussion.
words.e: Mario and Joe took a long time negotiating the contract between the companies.
words.zh: 協商是指透過討論試圖達成協議。

words.d: An overdose is an instance of taking or having too much of something.
words.e: John’s skin was burned from an overdose of sunshine.
words.zh: 過量是指攝取或擁有過多某物的情況。

words.d: Persuasion is the act of making someone do or believe something.
words.e: The persuasion of his argument convinced the customer to buy the laptop.
words.zh: 說服是使某人做某事或相信某事的行為。

words.d: A relay is a race in which teams of runners or swimmers race against each other.
words.e: Jerry was the fastest on his team, so he ran the last part of the relay.
words.zh: 接力賽是由跑步或游泳選手組隊相互競賽的比賽。

words.d: Reluctance is a feeling of not wanting to do something.
words.e: Jesse took out the trash with great reluctance.
words.zh: 不情願是一種不想做某事的感覺。

words.d: To restate something means to say it again or in a different way.
words.e: Mrs. Jones restated the test question to the class.
words.zh: 重述某件事意味著再次或以不同方式說出來。

words.d: Sesame is an herb that is grown for its small seeds and its oil.
words.e: I used the buns with the sesame seeds on them.
words.zh: 芝麻是一種因其小籽和油脂而種植的草本植物。

words.d: To sip something means to drink a small amount at a time.
words.e: Liza relaxed on the beach, sipping fruit juice through a straw.
words.zh: 小口喝某物意味著一次只喝少量。

words.d: The verge is the point at which something is about to happen.
words.e: Joan was on the verge of leaving her house when the phone rang.
words.zh: 邊緣是某事即將發生的臨界點。

words.d: If someone is wary, they are cautious or mistrusting.
words.e: She was wary of going to school because she hadn’t done her homework.
words.zh: 如果某人警惕，他們是謹慎或不信任的。

words.d: To waver is to be unable to decide between two choices.
words.e: I wavered between eating the apple or the cake for a snack.
words.zh: 猶豫不決是無法在兩個選擇之間做出決定。

words.d: If something goes ashore, it goes from water to the land.
words.e: After a long day of fishing, Glen pulled his boat ashore, so it wouldn’t float away.
words.zh: 如果某物上岸，它從水中移動到陸地。

words.d: To contradict means to state the opposite of what someone else has.
words.e: Ken was always fighting with his little sister because she kept contradicting him.
words.zh: 反駁意味著說出與他人相反的陳述。

words.d: A counterpart is something that is very similar to something else in what it does.
words.e: Our manager will meet our rival company’s counterpart later today.
words.zh: 對應物是在功能上與某物非常相似的事物。

words.d: When something or someone is devoid of a thing, they are missing it.
words.e: The movie was devoid of any violence, so it was a perfect movie for the family.
words.zh: 當某物或某人缺乏某樣東西時，他們缺少它。

words.d: To diverge is to become different or to follow a different direction.
words.e: The road diverged into two paths that led to our houses.
words.zh: 分歧是變得不同或走向不同方向。

words.d: To elude means to avoid being caught by something.
words.e: The rabbit eluded the wolf by hiding in a bush.
words.zh: 逃避意味著避免被某物抓住。

words.d: An embryo is a human or animal that is still growing inside its mother.
words.e: Some doctors say that what a mother eats has a big effect on her embryo.
words.zh: 胚胎是仍在母體內生長的人類或動物。

words.d: To fend off something means to push it away and avoid it.
words.e: Dave spent all night fending off bugs instead of sleeping.
words.zh: 抵擋某物意味著將其推開並避免它。

words.d: When something is fictitious, it is made up and not real.
words.e: The author said that the characters in her book were completely fictitious.
words.zh: 虛構的事物是編造出來的，並非真實。

words.d: A gazette is a newspaper.
words.e: Alice wants to write for a gazette when she’s older.
words.zh: 公報是一種報紙。

words.d: If something is homogeneous, it is made up of things which are all the same.
words.e: All of the houses on Victor’s block were boring and homogenous.
words.zh: 同質的事物由完全相同的成分組成。

words.d: To obstruct something means to get in its way.
words.e: The car broke down on the road and obstructed traffic for hours.
words.zh: 阻礙某事物意味著擋住它的去路。

words.d: To plunge means to move down into something very quickly.
words.e: The water in the pool was very cold, but the boy plunged in anyway.
words.zh: 猛衝是指非常快速地向下進入某物。

words.d: To prolong means to make something last for a longer time.
words.e: Sandy walked slowly across the beach, trying to prolong her lunch break.
words.zh: 延長是指讓某事物持續更長時間。

words.d: To publicize is to make something get a lot of attention.
words.e: The company publicized the job positions in the newspaper.
words.zh: 宣傳是讓某事物獲得大量關注。

words.d: If something is sparse, there is not very much of it in a big area.
words.e: Rob spent Saturday in his garden, clearing out the sparse weeds.
words.zh: 稀疏的事物在廣大區域中數量不多。

words.d: A surplus is an extra amount of something.
words.e: The store sold their surplus items on sale.
words.zh: 盈餘是指某事物的多餘數量。

words.d: To theorize means to develop ideas about something.
words.e: They enjoyed theorizing about how things worked.
words.zh: 理論化是指發展關於某事物的想法。

words.d: To verify means to find out if something is true.
words.e: Julian called the movie theater to verify that the movie started at nine.
words.zh: 驗證是指查明某事物是否真實。

words.d: If something or someone is vigorous, they use a lot of energy.
words.e: Henry uses vigorous exercise to keep himself in shape.
words.zh: 充滿活力的人或事物會運用大量能量。

words.d: An altar is a table used in churches.
words.e: The altar had many lit candles on it.
words.zh: 祭壇是教堂中使用的一種桌子。

words.d: Arthritis is an illness causing pain and swelling in a person’s joints.
words.e: Agatha couldn’t play the piano anymore because of her arthritis.
words.zh: 關節炎是一種導致關節疼痛腫脹的疾病。

words.d: Botany is the study of plants.
words.e: Gardens are the best places for botany.
words.zh: 植物學是研究植物的學科。

words.d: If something or someone is credible, they can be believed or trusted.
words.e: Dick gave a credible reason for being late and didn’t get in any trouble.
words.zh: 若某人或某事可信，即能被相信或信任。

words.d: If someone is deceased, they are dead.
words.e: We visited the graves of our deceased grandparents.
words.zh: 若某人已故，即已死亡。

words.d: Deception is the act of lying or tricking someone.
words.e: The magic looked very real, but it was only deception.
words.zh: 欺騙是指說謊或誤導他人的行為。

words.d: To decipher writing is to figure out what it says.
words.e: My teacher complained that she couldn’t decipher my essay.
words.zh: 解讀文字即理解其含義。

words.d: Dung is solid waste material produced by animals.
words.e: There was cow dung all over the field.
words.zh: 糞便是動物產生的固體廢物。

words.d: Dusk is the time in the evening when it begins to get dark.
words.e: After dusk, Hannah would catch fireflies in the park.
words.zh: 黃昏是傍晚天色開始變暗的時分。

words.d: To gratify someone means to please them.
words.e: Bonnie was gratified after receiving her gift from her parents.
words.zh: 滿足某人即令其愉悅。

words.d: To hone something is to improve it and make it very good.
words.e: Lisa honed her chess skills by hours and hours of practice.
words.zh: 磨練某物是使其精進至臻。

words.d: To mash something is to crush it so that it is soft.
words.e: We mashed the hard potatoes and served them for dinner.
words.zh: 搗碎某物是將其壓成柔軟狀態。

words.d: If something is ornate, it is decorated with a lot of fancy things.
words.e: Kim’s prom dress was very expensive since it was so ornate.
words.zh: 若某物華麗，即飾有許多精美之物。

words.d: Pneumonia is a dangerous illness causing the lungs to fill with liquid.
words.e: Elaine got pneumonia after playing outside in the rain without a coat.
words.zh: 肺炎是導致肺部積液的危險疾病。

words.d: When someone is psychic, they know what will happen or what people think.
words.e: I think my grandmother has psychic abilities because she can predict anything.
words.zh: 有通靈能力者能預知未來或他人想法。

words.d: If someone is psychotic, they have a very serious mental illness.
words.e: The psychotic patient believed he saw things that weren’t real.
words.zh: 若某人精神錯亂，即患有嚴重精神疾病。

words.d: The scope of something is how many people or things it relates to.
words.e: Modern history has a wide scope.
words.zh: 某事的範圍指其關聯的人或事物數量。

words.d: If something or someone is sinister, they are evil.
words.e: Bad guys in most movies have sinister laughs.
words.zh: 若某人或某事邪惡，即為險惡。

words.d: Strife is disagreement or fighting between people or groups.
words.e: There was a lot of strife between Jim and Lisa about what TV show to watch.
words.zh: 衝突是指人與人或群體之間的不和或爭鬥。

words.d: If something is therapeutic, it helps to cure a disease or makes you healthier.
words.e: After a stressful day of working, Phoebe would take a therapeutic bath.
words.zh: 若某事物具有療癒作用，則有助於治療疾病或促進健康。

words.d: If something is congested, it is full or blocked.
words.e: Tom didn’t get home until after dark because the road was so congested.
words.zh: 若某事物擁擠，則表示其充滿或受阻。

words.d: A courier is someone who takes and delivers mail or packages.
words.e: Before trains, most couriers used horses to travel.
words.zh: 快遞員是負責運送郵件或包裹的人。

words.d: To deform something means to change it from its correct or original shape.
words.e: The computer program deformed the building’s picture into an unreal sight.
words.zh: 使某物變形，即改變其正確或原始形狀。

words.d: Etiquette is the group of rules about how to be polite.
words.e: When in Asian countries, bowing is a form of etiquette.
words.zh: 禮儀是一套關於如何表現禮貌的規則。

words.d: If something is exclusive, it is expensive and only for rich people.
words.e: The golf course was so exclusive that most people hadn’t even heard of it.
words.zh: 若某事物是專屬的，則價格昂貴且僅限富人使用。

words.d: Freight is a set of items carried on a train, boat, or airplane.
words.e: Trade ships only carried valuable freight like silk and spices.
words.zh: 貨物是指火車、船隻或飛機上運載的一批物品。

words.d: A garment is a piece of clothing.
words.e: The business man had all of his garments cleaned before the important meeting.
words.zh: 服裝是一件衣物。

words.d: Insomnia is a condition in which a person has difficulty sleeping.
words.e: Nate’s insomnia prevented him from getting enough rest.
words.zh: 失眠是一種難以入睡的狀況。

words.d: Intuitive is knowing about something without naturally having support or proof.
words.e: Rhonda had an intuitive feeling that Shane wasn’t coming to school today.
words.zh: 直覺是指在沒有自然證據或支持的情況下了解某事。

words.d: If something is liable to happen, it is very likely that it will happen.
words.e: During the summer months, hikers in the forest are liable to see deer and elk.
words.zh: 若某事很可能發生，則其發生的可能性極高。

words.d: To obsess about something means to think about it all of the time.
words.e: After watching the Star Wars movies, Ike obsessed about becoming a Jedi.
words.zh: 對某事著迷，意指無時無刻都在思考它。

words.d: When something is overboard, it is over the side of a boat and in the water.
words.e: Tom and Gary slipped on the wet floor and fell overboard.
words.zh: 當某物落水時，即指其越過船舷掉入水中。

words.d: A premium is a payment that is higher than average.
words.e: Tony paid for premium gas because it made his car run the best.
words.zh: 溢價是指高於平均水準的付款。

words.d: A privilege is a special right given to only a certain person or group of people.
words.e: Only the best employee had the privilege of parking in that spot.
words.zh: 特權是僅授予特定個人或群體的特別權利。

words.d: To propel something means to push or move it somewhere.
words.e: The strong wind propelled the leaf through the air and across the street.
words.zh: 推動某物意味著將其向前推或移動。

words.d: To socialize is to have a good time with people.
words.e: I like to socialize with my classmates after school.
words.zh: 社交是指與他人共度愉快時光。

words.d: To suppress something means to prevent it from happening.
words.e: She suppressed her urge to scream because she didn’t want to be noticed.
words.zh: 壓制某事物意味著阻止其發生。

words.d: A tram is a vehicle like a streetcar that runs on electricity above ground.
words.e: I took the tram to Eighth Avenue.
words.zh: 電車是一種像有軌電車一樣、在地面以上靠電力運行的車輛。

words.d: To unsettle someone means to make them anxious or worried.
words.e: The dark clouds in the sky unsettled Beth.
words.zh: 使某人不安意味著讓他們感到焦慮或擔憂。

words.d: To warp means to become bent into the wrong shape.
words.e: The woman put the clock above the fireplace, and the heat warped it.
words.zh: 變形是指彎曲成錯誤的形狀。

words.d: An artery is a tube that takes blood from the heart to the rest of the body.
words.e: Eating healthily keeps your arteries clean, so blood can flow with ease.
words.zh: 動脈是將血液從心臟輸送到身體其他部位的管道。

words.d: Deterioration is the act of becoming worse.
words.e: The lack of care led to the deterioration of the house.
words.zh: 惡化是指變得越來越糟的過程。

words.d: If something or someone is elusive, they are hard to find.
words.e: The elusive fish only came out at night.
words.zh: 如果某物或某人難以捉摸，就很難找到。

words.d: To forage means to look around for food.
words.e: Many animals hide food in the fall since little can be foraged in winter.
words.zh: 覓食是指四處尋找食物。

words.d: To impede something means to keep it from moving or going forward.
words.e: The barrier impeded our ability to get closer to the building.
words.zh: 阻礙某事物意味著阻止其移動或前進。

words.d: To induce something means to make it happen.
words.e: Running without good shoes may induce leg pain.
words.zh: 誘發某事物意味著使其發生。

words.d: If two things are inseparable, they can’t be separated.
words.e: Denise and Diana have been inseparable since they first met.
words.zh: 如果兩件事密不可分，就無法將它們分開。

words.d: If something is invalid, it is not correct.
words.e: The way to winning debates is to avoid invalid arguments.
words.zh: 如果某事物無效，就是不正確的。

words.d: To magnify something means to make it look bigger than it really is.
words.e: Astronomers use telescopes to magnify far-away stars.
words.zh: 放大某物意指使其看起來比實際更大。

words.d: The mainstream is a group of ideas that are considered normal and accepted.
words.e: Before punk rock music became part of the mainstream, only a few people liked it.
words.zh: 主流是指被視為正常且廣為接受的觀念群體。

words.d: A microbe is a very small living thing that often makes people sick.
words.e: The first step in curing a disease is finding the microbe that causes the disease.
words.zh: 微生物是一種極微小、常使人致病的生物。

words.d: If something is negligible, it is extremely small and not important.
words.e: The amount she owed was negligible. It was less than ten cents.
words.zh: 若某事物微不足道，即表示其極微小且不重要。

words.d: Paralysis is the loss of the ability to move all or part of your body.
words.e: His paralysis meant that he had to use a wheelchair to get around.
words.zh: 癱瘓是指身體全部或部分失去活動能力。

words.d: A pest is an animal or insect that hurts plants or food.
words.e: Many farmers put up scarecrows to keep pests off of their corn.
words.zh: 害蟲是會損害植物或食物的動物或昆蟲。

words.d: To prevail means to be accepted or very common.
words.e: Unfortunately, injustice still prevails in the country’s court system.
words.zh: 盛行意指被廣泛接受或非常普遍。

words.d: Respiration is the act of breathing.
words.e: Artificial respiration is the best thing to do if someone stops breathing.
words.zh: 呼吸是指吸氣與呼氣的動作。

words.d: To rupture means to tear or burst open.
words.e: Sarah put too much water into the balloon, and it ruptured.
words.zh: 破裂意指撕裂或爆開。

words.d: If something or someone is savage, they are very violent or cruel.
words.e: Wild animals are very savage when defending their babies.
words.zh: 若某人或某物野蠻，則表示其非常暴力或殘忍。

words.d: To stun someone means to make them unable to speak due to surprise or shock.
words.e: Tim fell off the swing and was too stunned to cry.
words.zh: 使某人震驚意指使其因驚訝或衝擊而無法說話。

words.d: If one is susceptible to something like disease, they are easily harmed by it.
words.e: Not eating enough vegetables makes you more susceptible to getting sick.
words.zh: 若某人易受疾病等影響，即表示其容易被傷害。

words.d: To accumulate something is to collect a lot of it over time.
words.e: The mail accumulated in their mailbox while they were on vacation.
words.zh: 累積某物意指隨著時間收集大量該物。

words.d: When something is aerial, it relates to being in the air or flying.
words.e: The aerial photographer took pictures from the air balloon.
words.zh: 當某事物為空中時，即與在空中或飛行相關。

words.d: An apparatus is a device used for a particular purpose.
words.e: The campers had an apparatus that showed them their exact location.
words.zh: 裝置是指用於特定目的的設備。

words.d: An avalanche is a large amount of snow, ice, and rock falling off a mountain.
words.e: The avalanche destroyed the mountain village.
words.zh: 雪崩是指大量雪、冰和岩石從山上崩落。

words.d: Consistency is the state of always behaving in the same way.
words.e: Her consistency in archery meant that our team had a chance to win.
words.zh: 一致性是指始終以相同方式行事的狀態。

words.d: Convection is the flow of hot air through gas or liquid.
words.e: The weather changed because of convection in the atmosphere.
words.zh: 對流是指熱空氣經由氣體或液體流動。

words.d: To discharge someone is to allow them to leave from a place, usually a hospital.
words.e: I was discharged from the hospital after three days.
words.zh: 讓某人出院是指允許他們離開某個地方，通常是醫院。

words.d: When something is intact, it is complete and not damaged.
words.e: Despite being over ten years old, my father’s model ship is still intact.
words.zh: 當某物完好無損時，表示它完整且未受損壞。

words.d: When a person is mortal, they cannot live forever.
words.e: All people are mortal.
words.zh: 當人會死亡時，表示他們無法永生。

words.d: An omen is a sign of what will happen in the future.
words.e: He thought that seeing a black cat in the street was an omen of bad luck.
words.zh: 預兆是未來將發生之事的徵兆。

words.d: When the sky is overcast, it is full of clouds and is not sunny.
words.e: The sky was overcast in the morning, but by noon it was bright again.
words.zh: 當天空陰沉時，表示烏雲密布且沒有陽光。

words.d: When something is poignant, it causes a very strong feeling of sadness.
words.e: The girls cried at the end of the poignant movie.
words.zh: 當某事令人心酸時，它會引起非常強烈的悲傷感。

words.d: A ranger is a person who protects the forest or parks.
words.e: Peter wanted to be a ranger because he liked spending time outside.
words.zh: 護林員是保護森林或公園的人。

words.d: Rubble is piles of broken stone and wood created after a building is destroyed.
words.e: There was rubble all over the city after the earthquake.
words.zh: 瓦礫是指建築物被摧毀後堆積的碎石和木塊。

words.d: To seclude someone means to keep them away from other people.
words.e: She was secluded on an island for over a year.
words.zh: 隔離某人是指讓他們遠離其他人。

words.d: If something moves sideways, then it moves to or from the side.
words.e: Jim turned sideways in order to slow down and stop his snowboard.
words.zh: 如果某物橫向移動，則表示它朝側面或從側面移動。

words.d: To sob is to cry loudly.
words.e: I sobbed when my youngest daughter got married.
words.zh: 啜泣是指大聲哭泣。

words.d: When something or someone is sober, they are serious and calm.
words.e: After the funeral, everybody felt very sober.
words.zh: 當某人或某物冷靜時，表示他們嚴肅且平穩。

words.d: A speck is a very small mark or amount.
words.e: A speck of blood appeared where the mosquito bit him.
words.zh: 斑點是指非常小的標記或數量。

words.d: An upbringing is the way that someone is taught to behave by their parents.
words.e: He had a strict upbringing and was never allowed to watch television.
words.zh: 教養是一個人被父母教導如何行為的方式。

words.d: To advocate a plan or idea is to support or suggest it in public.
words.e: The group advocated increased spending on education and schools.
words.zh: 倡導某項計劃或理念，是指在公開場合支持或提出它。

words.d: To authorize something means to give permission for it.
words.e: The mayor authorized the construction of a new statue in the park.
words.zh: 授權某事，是指給予其許可。

words.d: A civilian is someone who is not in the military.
words.e: It was Todd’s job to keep civilians from entering the army facility.
words.zh: 平民是指不在軍隊中的人。

words.d: A commodity is something that can be bought or sold.
words.e: Fran’s uncle made most of his money trading commodities.
words.zh: 商品是指可以買賣的東西。

words.d: A conquest is an event by which one country takes over another country.
words.e: After the conquest, the people of the small city had no freedom.
words.zh: 征服是指一個國家佔領另一個國家的事件。

words.d: To disclose something means to tell it to someone else.
words.e: John came home late, so he had to disclose his activities to his wife.
words.zh: 揭露某事，是指將其告訴他人。

words.d: The dynamics of a situation are the way that parts of it affect each other.
words.e: A good psychologist needs to know a lot about the dynamics of brain disorders.
words.zh: 局勢的動態，是指其中各部分相互影響的方式。

words.d: To enroll in something is to put one’s name on a list as a member of a group.
words.e: The students had to enroll in the class one semester in advance.
words.zh: 註冊某事物，是指將自己的名字列入某個群體的成員名單中。

words.d: When someone is envious, they want something that another person has.
words.e: They were envious of their neighbor’s front yard.
words.zh: 當一個人嫉妒時，他們想要別人擁有的東西。

words.d: Euphoria is a feeling of extreme happiness.
words.e: The athlete felt euphoria after he won the important game.
words.zh: 欣快感是一種極度快樂的感覺。

words.d: When something is festive, it is happy and related to a party or celebration.
words.e: Dean’s favorite part of Christmas is the festive clothing that people wear.
words.zh: 當某事物具有節日氣氛時，它是快樂的，並且與派對或慶祝活動相關。

words.d: When someone is jolly, they are cheerful and happy.
words.e: My grandmother’s jolly attitude always made me smile.
words.zh: 當一個人歡樂時，他們是開朗且快樂的。

words.d: Lentils are very small beans that people cook and eat.
words.e: Danni made her special soup with lentils when her husband was sick.
words.zh: 扁豆是非常小的豆子，人們會烹煮並食用。

words.d: To marshal a group means to assemble them in order.
words.e: The students were marshaled outside and put into groups.
words.zh: 將一群人集合起來，意指將他們有序地聚集。

words.d: Morale is the amount of hope that people have during a difficult situation.
words.e: To improve our family’s morale, mom suggested going on a picnic.
words.zh: 士氣，是人在困境中所懷抱的希望程度。

words.d: A prophecy is a prediction about what will happen in the future.
words.e: Some people believe that prophecies are actually true.
words.zh: 預言，是關於未來將發生之事的預測。

words.d: A sage is someone who is very wise.
words.e: Hal always visited the local sage for help with love.
words.zh: 聖賢，是指極具智慧的人。

words.d: A senate is a part of the government in some countries.
words.e: Everybody liked Caroline and chose her to speak for them in the senate.
words.zh: 參議院，是某些國家政府的一部分。

words.d: A sentiment is an opinion based on your feelings.
words.e: Tess told everyone at the table her sentiments about eating meat.
words.zh: 情感，是基於感受而產生的看法。

words.d: Unrest is a state of anger about something among the people in a place.
words.e: High taxes caused much of the unrest that led to the Revolutionary War.
words.zh: 動盪，是指某地民眾因某事而處於憤怒的狀態。

words.d: To adorn something means to make it more beautiful by decorating it.
words.e: Small glass beads adorned the vase.
words.zh: 裝飾某物，意指透過點綴使其更美麗。

words.d: Bliss is a state of complete happiness.
words.e: Every time he sees his girlfriend, he feels a sense of bliss.
words.zh: 極樂，是完全幸福的狀態。

words.d: A butler is the most important male servant in a wealthy house.
words.e: Whenever he needed something, he rang a bell, and the butler appeared.
words.zh: 管家，是富貴人家中最重要的男性僕人。

words.d: A cramp is a strong pain caused by a muscle after a lot of physical use.
words.e: After the marathon, Jenny got a terrible cramp in her calf muscle.
words.zh: 抽筋，是肌肉在大量體力使用後引起的劇烈疼痛。

words.d: When a building is dilapidated, it is old and in bad condition.
words.e: Paint peeled off of the old dilapidated apartment building.
words.zh: 當建築物破舊不堪時，表示它老舊且狀況不佳。

words.d: To evoke a memory or emotion means to make it occur.
words.e: The picture evoked memories of when grandmother was a girl.
words.zh: 喚起記憶或情感，意指使其浮現。

words.d: A farewell is an instance of saying goodbye or a way to say it.
words.e: She got on the plane after we said our farewells.
words.zh: 告別，是說再見的時刻或方式。

words.d: A faucet is a device that controls the flow of a liquid or gas.
words.e: Turn off the faucet when you are done brushing your teeth.
words.zh: 水龍頭，是控制液體或氣體流動的裝置。

words.d: Filth is a large amount of dirt or dirty things that disgust you.
words.e: There was tons of filth and trash on the shore of the river.
words.zh: 污穢是指大量令人厭惡的灰塵或髒東西。

words.d: A flaw is a mistake in something that causes it to be less effective or correct.
words.e: We discovered a major flaw in the metal chain.
words.zh: 缺陷是事物中的錯誤，導致其效果或正確性降低。

words.d: To grin means to smile broadly.
words.e: That joke makes me grin every time I hear it.
words.zh: 咧嘴笑是指張大嘴巴微笑。

words.d: Housekeeping is the maintenance of a house or an establishment like a hotel.
words.e: Housekeeping is not much fun, but it has to be done.
words.zh: 家政管理是維護房屋或旅館等場所的工作。

words.d: A mound of something is a large pile of it.
words.e: There was a mound of clothes on the messy teenager’s floor.
words.zh: 一堆東西是指大量的堆積物。

words.d: When a body part is numb, it does not have any feeling.
words.e: After holding my hand under the icy water, my fingers went numb.
words.zh: 當身體部位麻木時，它沒有任何感覺。

words.d: When people are reckless, they act in an unsafe way.
words.e: The reckless driver posed a threat to everyone else on the road.
words.zh: 當人們魯莽時，他們會以不安全的方式行動。

words.d: Slate is a dark grey rock that can easily be split into layers.
words.e: The roof of the church was made of slate.
words.zh: 板岩是一種深灰色的岩石，容易分裂成薄片。

words.d: A stool is a seat with legs but no support for a person’s arms and back.
words.e: I don’t find stools very comfortable to sit on.
words.zh: 凳子是一種有腿但無扶手和靠背的座位。

words.d: A testament to something shows that it exists or is true.
words.e: The beautiful performance was a testament to the singer’s natural talents.
words.zh: 某事物的證明顯示它存在或屬實。

words.d: Timber is wood that is used for building houses and making furniture.
words.e: Trees in this area are grown specifically to be used for timber.
words.zh: 木材是用於建造房屋和製造家具的木料。

words.d: A valve is a device attached to a pipe that controls the flow of liquid or air.
words.e: The mechanic removed the dirt to clear the engine valve.
words.zh: 閥門是安裝在管道上控制液體或氣體流動的裝置。

words.d: To certify something means to confirm that its results are true.
words.e: The photograph on her passport certified that she was indeed Jolene Sawyer.
words.zh: 認證某事是指確認其結果屬實。

words.d: To collaborate means to work together on something.
words.e: When they collaborated, they managed to finish their chores early.
words.zh: 合作是指共同完成某件事。

words.d: To compile things means to collect a variety of them into a group.
words.e: She compiled a list of people who she wanted to attend her birthday party.
words.zh: 彙編事物是指將多種事物收集成一個群組。

words.d: To counteract something means to act against it in order to reduce or stop it.
words.e: Medicine is supposed to counteract illnesses.
words.zh: 抵銷某事物意指採取行動以減少或阻止它。

words.d: To curb something means to prevent it from happening or increasing.
words.e: She curbed her anger by listening to a relaxing song.
words.zh: 遏制某事物意指防止其發生或增長。

words.d: To diagnose someone means to identify the medical condition they have.
words.e: Several of the children were diagnosed with the flu.
words.zh: 診斷某人意指辨識其所患的醫療狀況。

words.d: To enact something means to make it into a law.
words.e: The council enacted a law that would only allow buses to drive downtown.
words.zh: 頒布某事物意指將其制定為法律。

words.d: A federation is a group of states or businesses working for a common cause.
words.e: The United Nations is a federation designed to prevent war, disease, and famine.
words.zh: 聯邦是一群為共同目標合作的州或企業。

words.d: If something is gross, then it is disgusting.
words.e: The food was so gross that the dog couldn’t eat it without feeling sick.
words.zh: 若某事物令人作嘔，則表示它令人厭惡。

words.d: If something is humane, then it is good and kind.
words.e: Helping build homes for poor people is very humane.
words.zh: 若某事物是人道的，則表示它善良且仁慈。

words.d: If something is intolerable, then it is so bad that people cannot bear it.
words.e: The weather was so intolerable that I had to put on my warmest clothes.
words.zh: 若某事物無法容忍，則表示它糟糕到人們無法承受。

words.d: If someone is needy, they are very poor.
words.e: After he lost his job, he became very needy.
words.zh: 若某人貧困，則表示他們非常窮。

words.d: The onset of something unpleasant is the beginning of it.
words.e: At the onset of the battle, the enemy wasn’t prepared for such a large attack.
words.zh: 某不愉快事物的發作是其開始的階段。

words.d: To pledge means to make a promise to do something.
words.e: Her mother pledged that she would find her daughter’s lost kitten.
words.zh: 承諾意指做出保證去做某事。

words.d: To prohibit something means to not allow it.
words.e: She prohibited the students from speaking until their work was done.
words.zh: 禁止某事物意指不允許它。

words.d: A rash is an infected area of the skin with redness, bumps, itching, or dryness.
words.e: The new perfume left a horrible rash on my skin.
words.zh: 皮疹是皮膚上出現紅腫、丘疹、搔癢或乾燥的感染區域。

words.d: To render something means to make it become something else.
words.e: His report was rendered unimportant by the release of new information.
words.zh: 使某事物轉變意指讓它變成另一種狀態。

words.d: Smallpox is a disease that causes tiny bumps on the skin and high fevers.
words.e: When she saw the tiny bumps, she thought her son might have smallpox.
words.zh: 天花是一種會在皮膚上引起細小丘疹並伴隨高燒的疾病。

words.d: To transmit something means to pass it from one person or place to another.
words.e: The radio tower transmits a signal to all the radios in a 20-kilometer radius.
words.zh: 傳遞某物意指將其從一人或一處傳給另一人或另一處。

words.d: To vow means to make a promise to do something.
words.e: Before they are allowed to work, all senators must vow to never accept bribes.
words.zh: 發誓意指承諾去做某事。

words.d: Camouflage is something used to hide people and things.
words.e: The green and brown camouflage was best used for hiding in forests and jungles.
words.zh: 偽裝是用來隱藏人與物的東西。

words.d: To contemplate something means to think about it.
words.e: Mark took a moment to contemplate the math problem before solving it.
words.zh: 沉思某事意指仔細思考它。

words.d: To contend with something means to struggle to overcome it.
words.e: Stacy had to contend with a learning disability throughout high school.
words.zh: 與某事抗爭意指努力克服它。

words.d: A cot is a small portable bed.
words.e: At the camp, the boys’ cabin was lined with cots.
words.zh: 行軍床是一種小型可攜帶的床。

words.d: To enlist means to join the military.
words.e: In their final year at school, the students were asked to enlist in the military.
words.zh: 入伍意指加入軍隊。

words.d: A frontier is a border between two regions or countries.
words.e: A fence was built along the frontier where the river curved.
words.zh: 邊疆是兩個地區或國家之間的分界線。

words.d: A handbook is an item that gives specific information or instructions.
words.e: If you look at the handbook, it will tell you which wires to connect to the TV.
words.zh: 手冊是一種提供特定資訊或說明的物品。

words.d: If someone is hesitant, then they are not sure or slow in acting or speaking.
words.e: Though he knew the answer, he was hesitant to say it because he might be wrong.
words.zh: 若某人猶豫不決，則表示他們在行動或說話時不確定或遲緩。

words.d: If something is lush, then it is full of a variety of large, healthy plants.
words.e: The lush jungle was filled with plants, trees, and vines.
words.zh: 若某物茂盛，則表示它充滿多種大型、健康的植物。

words.d: Marrow is the soft substance in the center of bones.
words.e: Dissolved marrow is a common ingredient in soups.
words.zh: 骨髓是骨頭中央的柔軟物質。

words.d: An outfit is a set of clothes worn together, often for a certain job or event.
words.e: Kelly’s new outfit made her look so glamourous.
words.zh: 套裝是一套搭配穿著的衣物，常用於特定工作或場合。

words.d: A paw is an animal’s foot that has claws or soft bottoms.
words.e: The kitten cleaned its paws with its tongue.
words.zh: 爪子是動物具有爪或柔軟底部的腳。

words.d: To quiver means to tremble or shake.
words.e: The flame on the candle quivered whenever someone opened or closed a door.
words.zh: 顫抖意指發抖或搖晃。

words.d: If something or someone is splendid, then they are very good.
words.e: From his head down to his shoes, his clothes looked splendid.
words.zh: 如果某事物或某人非常出色，那便是極好的。

words.d: To stray means to go in a wrong direction and often become lost.
words.e: He found himself lost because he had strayed from the tour group.
words.zh: 迷失方向意味著走錯路，並時常因此迷路。

words.d: If something is substantial, then it is of great importance, size, or value.
words.e: The bank said that he owed it a substantial amount of money.
words.zh: 如果某事物是重大的，那便具有極高的重要性、規模或價值。

words.d: A torch is a stick with one end on fire that can be carried in order to give light.
words.e: He grabbed a piece of wood and stuck it in the fire in order to make a torch.
words.zh: 火炬是一端燃燒、可手持照明的棍棒。

words.d: A tract is a large area of land.
words.e: On the other side of the mountains was a long tract of forest.
words.zh: 地域是指一大片土地。

words.d: A vigil is a period of watchful attention at night fora specific purpose.
words.e: He had a vigil in front of the tomb for three days.
words.zh: 守夜是為了特定目的而在夜間保持警覺的一段時間。

words.d: If someone is weary, then they are tired.
words.e: Jane was weary after a long day of work.
words.zh: 如果某人感到疲憊，那便是累了。

words.d: To adhere means to act in the way that a rule or agreement says is right.
words.e: If the new employees wish to succeed, they must adhere to the boss’s rules.
words.zh: 遵守意味著按照規則或協議所規定的正確方式行事。

words.d: To administer means to take responsibility for organizing something.
words.e: The teacher’s assistant will administer the test.
words.zh: 管理意味著負責組織某項事務。

words.d: When people are compassionate, they feel pity and sympathy for others.
words.e: The compassionate nurse tried to make the sick man feel comfortable.
words.zh: 當人們富有同情心時，他們會對他人感到憐憫與同理。

words.d: To contaminate something means to put dirty or harmful chemicals into it.
words.e: The lake was contaminated when pollutants entered into the water.
words.zh: 污染某物是指將骯髒或有害的化學物質摻入其中。

words.d: A deficiency is a lack of something, especially something that is needed.
words.e: Your snack has a deficiency of any real nutrients.
words.zh: 缺乏是指缺少某物，尤其是所需的東西。

words.d: An epidemic is an outbreak of a disease that spreads quickly.
words.e: It was difficult to stop the flu epidemic.
words.zh: 流行病是一種迅速擴散的疾病爆發。

words.d: A hazard is something that could be dangerous to a person’s health or safety.
words.e: Smoking cigarettes poses many health hazards.
words.zh: 危險是指可能對人的健康或安全構成威脅的事物。

words.d: When something is imperative, it is extremely important and must be done.
words.e: It was imperative for him to find a job.
words.zh: 當某事是必要的，它便極其重要且必須完成。

words.d: Intestines are tubes through which food passes after it leaves the stomach.
words.e: Whatever you eat goes into your intestines.
words.zh: 腸道是食物離開胃後通過的管道。

words.d: To manifest means to make something visible or obvious.
words.e: The holiday season manifested joy in the children.
words.zh: 顯現意指讓某事物變得可見或明顯。

words.d: When something is metabolic, it relates to a person’s or animal’s metabolism.
words.e: People who have a low metabolic rate will gain weight.
words.zh: 當某事物與代謝相關時，它涉及人或動物的新陳代謝。

words.d: When a place is overcrowded, it has too many people or things in it.
words.e: The lobby was overcrowded with people.
words.zh: 當一個地方過度擁擠時，表示其中有太多人或物品。

words.d: When something is paramount, it is more important than anything else.
words.e: The safety of passengers must be paramount when designing a car.
words.zh: 當某事物至關重要時，它比任何其他事物都更重要。

words.d: A practitioner is a doctor.
words.e: She made an appointment with the practitioner to treat her cough.
words.zh: 從業者即醫生。

words.d: The provision of something is the act of giving it to people in need or want.
words.e: That department is responsible for the provision of emergency supplies.
words.zh: 提供某物是指將其給予有需要或匱乏的人們的行為。

words.d: To replenish something means to make it full or complete again.
words.e: We planted nearly one hundred seeds to replenish the garden after the fire.
words.zh: 補充某物意指使其再次變得充足或完整。

words.d: When something is sterile, it is completely clean and free from germs.
words.e: The hospital room looked quite sterile.
words.zh: 當某物無菌時，它完全潔淨且不含細菌。

words.d: To upgrade something means to improve it or make it more efficient.
words.e: He upgraded to a real fancy car.
words.zh: 升級某物意指改進它或使其更有效率。

words.d: When something is viable, it is capable of doing what it is intended to do.
words.e: Her method of solving the math problem seemed viable.
words.zh: 當某物可行時，它能夠達成預期目的。

words.d: When something is voluntary, it is done by choice but is not required.
words.e: Many people attended the voluntary boat safety class.
words.zh: 當某事物是自願的，它是出於選擇而非強制執行。

words.d: If something is amid something else, then it is in the middle of it.
words.e: The bee was busily flying amid the flowers in the garden.
words.zh: 如果某物處於另一事物之中，則表示它在其中間。

words.d: If something happens backstage, it occurs behind a theater’s stage.
words.e: After the show, the director went backstage and thanked the actors.
words.zh: 如果某事發生在後台，則是在劇院舞台後方進行。

words.d: A billionaire is someone who has at least one billion dollars.
words.e: The sale of his inventions made the inventor a billionaire.
words.zh: 億萬富翁是指擁有至少十億美元的人。

words.d: A brute is someone who behaves or looks like a violent animal.
words.e: My older brother can act like a brute when he doesn’t get his way.
words.zh: 暴徒是指行為或外表像暴力動物的人。

words.d: If someone is clumsy, then they are awkward in handling things.
words.e: The businessman was clumsy and dropped his work files.
words.zh: 如果某人笨手笨腳，則表示他們在處理事物時顯得笨拙。

words.d: To collide with something means to hit into it while moving.
words.e: The two cars collided with each other because their drivers were not careful.
words.zh: 與某物碰撞，是指在移動中撞上它。

words.d: A culprit is someone who did a crime or other bad deed.
words.e: The police were still searching for the culprit from the robbery.
words.zh: 罪犯是指犯下罪行或其他惡行的人。

words.d: To evacuate means to leave a place of danger to a place of safety.
words.e: During the flood, many families were evacuated to higher ground.
words.zh: 撤離是指從危險的地方前往安全的地方。

words.d: If something is flammable, then it is able to catch on fire.
words.e: Be careful with that blanket near the candle. It is extremely flammable.
words.zh: 如果某物易燃，則表示它能夠著火。

words.d: A mob is a large crowd of people that often wants to cause violence.
words.e: The copier was destroyed by a mob of angry workers.
words.zh: 暴民是一大群常想引發暴力的人。

words.d: If something is premature, then it is done too early or before the proper time.
words.e: Mark’s celebration was premature because the ball hadn’t fallen in the hole.
words.zh: 如果某事過早，則表示它做得太早或未在適當時間進行。

words.d: To resent something means to have bad feelings about it.
words.e: She resented the fact that she had never been able to play an instrument.
words.zh: 怨恨某事物，是指對它懷有負面情緒。

words.d: A satire is a work of art that uses humor and irony to make fun of something.
words.e: This book is a satire of what life was like in the army.
words.zh: 諷刺作品是一種運用幽默與反諷來嘲弄某事物的藝術作品。

words.d: Scrutiny is the careful examination of something.
words.e: A scientist should always practice scrutiny with their work.
words.zh: 審查是指對某事物的仔細檢查。

words.d: To segregate something means to place it in a group apart from other things.
words.e: In gym class, the children were segregated into two groups: boys and girls.
words.zh: 隔離某物，是指將其置於與其他事物分開的群體中。

words.d: To subject someone to something means to force them to do or experience it.
words.e: The officers subjected everyone to a careful search before they left the plane.
words.zh: 使某人遭受某事，是指強迫他們去做或經歷它。

words.d: To testify means to give evidence as a witness.
words.e: The judge listened while the victim testified about the robbery.
words.zh: 作證是指以證人身分提供證據。

words.d: A tumult is a loud and confused noise made by a large crowd of people.
words.e: She couldn’t hear her friend over the tumult of the other excited guests.
words.zh: 喧囂是指一大群人發出嘈雜混亂的聲音。

words.d: To underestimate something or someone means to think they are not important.
words.e: Ne lost the game because we underestimated the other team’s skill.
words.zh: 低估某事物或某人，意指認為其不重要。

words.d: Uproar is loud noise caused by people who are very angry or upset.
words.e: The fans made a great uproar when their team lost the game.
words.zh: 喧囂是指因人們極度憤怒或沮喪而產生的巨大噪音。

words.d: To accelerate means to increase in speed.
words.e: When he stepped on the gas pedal, the motorcycle accelerated.
words.zh: 加速意指增加速度。

words.d: If you do something anew, you do it again and in a different way.
words.e: Though he had failed his driving test, he decided to try it anew.
words.zh: 若你重新做某事，便是以不同方式再做一次。

words.d: A defect is a part of something that is wrong or missing.
words.e: All these bottles have a defect and must be sent back to the warehouse.
words.zh: 缺陷是指某事物中錯誤或缺失的部分。

words.d: If something is dreary, then it is dull, dark, and lifeless.
words.e: After the fire, this section of forest is rather dreary.
words.zh: 若某事物陰沉乏味，則表示其單調、黑暗且毫無生氣。

words.d: To duplicate something means to copy it.
words.e: She duplicated her friend’s movements like she was in front of a mirror.
words.zh: 複製某事物意指將其拷貝。

words.d: If something is electromagnetic, it is related to electricity and magnetic fields.
words.e: Different colors of light come from different levels of electromagnetic energy.
words.zh: 若某事物是電磁的，則與電場和磁場相關。

words.d: An electron is a particle in all atoms that has a negative electric charge.
words.e: The number of electrons in an atom determines the substance the atoms make.
words.zh: 電子是所有原子中帶負電荷的粒子。

words.d: To glide means to fly on extended wings with little or no effort.
words.e: When the wind is blowing, birds can glide easily through the sky.
words.zh: 滑翔意指伸展翅膀幾乎不費力地飛行。

words.d: If someone is ingenious, then they are very smart.
words.e: Charles was the only person ingenious enough to repair the plane’s engines.
words.zh: 若某人足智多謀，則表示其非常聰明。

words.d: An innovation is a product or an idea that is new or very original.
words.e: Mrs. Johnson made a great innovation to the company’s business plan.
words.zh: 創新是指新穎或極具原創性的產品或想法。

words.d: If something or someone is innovative, they can think in creative ways.
words.e: Since Peter was so innovative, he was chosen to lead the science team.
words.zh: 若某事物或某人具有創新性，則能以創意方式思考。

words.d: To launch something means to make it go into motion.
words.e: The boat launched from the dock and floated down the river.
words.zh: 發射某事物意指使其開始運動。

words.d: If something is meteorological, it is concerned with the science of weather.
words.e: The thunderstorm was so large that it became a great meteorological event.
words.zh: 若某事物與氣象相關，則涉及天氣科學。

words.d: Meteorology is the science that studies the weather.
words.e: In order to understand the weather, you have to study meteorology.
words.zh: 氣象學是研究天氣的科學。

words.d: To penetrate something means to enter into it.
words.e: The knife easily penetrated the surface of the orange.
words.zh: 穿透某物意味著進入其中。

words.d: Propulsion is the force that moves something forward.
words.e: The propulsion lifted the rocket into the sky.
words.zh: 推進力是使物體前進的力量。

words.d: To simulate something means to copy its actions or characteristics.
words.e: The French language teacher could simulate the accent of a French citizen.
words.zh: 模擬某物意味著模仿其動作或特性。

words.d: To spur someone means to urge them into action.
words.e: The coach’s speech spurred her team into playing the best game of their lives.
words.zh: 激勵某人意味著促使其行動。

words.d: To stimulate something means to cause or to increase activity in it.
words.e: Doctors sometimes use electric shock to stimulate a patient’s heartbeat.
words.zh: 刺激某物意味著引發或增加其活動。

words.d: If someone is tenacious, then they do not easily give up.
words.e: I’m sure that he’ll finish that difficult sale. He is very tenacious.
words.zh: 若某人堅持不懈，則他們不易放棄。

words.d: If something is done beforehand, then it is done in advance.
words.e: He packed his luggage beforehand, so he was able to leave right away.
words.zh: 若某事預先完成，則表示提前做好。

words.d: If a temperature is centigrade, it is scaled, so water freezes at 0° and boils at 100°.
words.e: During the spring time, the temperature gets as warm as 26 degrees centigrade.
words.zh: 若溫度為攝氏，則其刻度為水在0°結冰、100°沸騰。

words.d: To chatter means to talk quickly about unimportant things.
words.e: The children chattered in the back of the classroom.
words.zh: 嘮叨意指快速談論不重要的事。

words.d: A concerto is music for a solo instrument and played with an orchestra.
words.e: The piano concerto has a section in which only the piano plays.
words.zh: 協奏曲是為獨奏樂器與管弦樂團合奏的音樂。

words.d: To condense a gas means to make it a liquid.
words.e: The cool air made tiny drops of water condense on the tops of the grass.
words.zh: 凝結氣體意味著使其變成液體。

words.d: A cove is a part of land where it curves inward around the sea.
words.e: They hid the boat in a small cove on the south side of the island.
words.zh: 小海灣是陸地沿海向內彎曲的部分。

words.d: To deteriorate means to become steadily worse.
words.e: The nation’s economy continued to deteriorate despite the politicians’ efforts.
words.zh: 惡化意味著持續變得更糟。

words.d: An exterior is the outside surface of something.
words.e: The exterior of the nut was hard and woody, but the inside was soft and delicious.
words.zh: 外部是某物的外表面。

words.d: A freeway is a road with no stops where cars travel at high speeds.
words.e: Once you get on the freeway, it only takes an hour to drive to my parent’s farm.
words.zh: 高速公路是一條沒有停車點、車輛高速行駛的道路。

words.d: If someone or something is hearty, then they are loud and happy.
words.e: The grandmother ended her story with a hearty laugh that pleased her grandchild.
words.zh: 若某人或某事是「熱烈的」，則表示他們喧鬧而快樂。

words.d: If someone is hospitable, they are friendly to strangers.
words.e: At dinner, Dad was very hospitable to my friends.
words.zh: 若某人「好客」，則表示他們對陌生人友善。

words.d: A manor is a large house with many rooms.
words.e: The manor had over forty rooms in addition to beautiful gardens.
words.zh: 莊園是一棟有許多房間的大房子。

words.d: A monastery is a building in which monks live.
words.e: The monks at this monastery are famous for their bread and music.
words.zh: 修道院是僧侶居住的建築。

words.d: A nursery is a room where babies and children sleep, play, or are cared for.
words.e: The children kissed their parents goodnight and went upstairs to the nursery.
words.zh: 育嬰室是嬰兒與孩童睡覺、玩耍或接受照護的房間。

words.d: If something is outstretched, then it is extended to its full length.
words.e: The cat stood on its back legs with its front legs outstretched.
words.zh: 若某物「伸展的」，則表示它被延伸至全長。

words.d: A parcel is a package of things to be carried or mailed somewhere.
words.e: The parcel looked like it had been stepped on by someone.
words.zh: 包裹是一組要運送或郵寄的物品。

words.d: A profile is an outline of a face, usually as seen from the side.
words.e: The drawing of Olivia’s profile came out very well.
words.zh: 側面輪廓是臉部的輪廓，通常從側面觀察。

words.d: If someone is vivacious, they are talkative and cheerful.
words.e: Everyone at the party was very vivacious and having a good time.
words.zh: 若某人「活潑的」，則表示他們健談且開朗。

words.d: If something is winding, then it follows a twisting course.
words.e: The winding river turned and looped around the bases of the hills.
words.zh: 若某物「蜿蜒的」，則表示它沿著曲折的路線前進。

words.d: To zip something means to close it with a zipper.
words.e: She zipped her backpack closed after putting her books inside it.
words.zh: 「拉上拉鍊」意指用拉鍊將其關閉。

words.d: An airway is the passage by which air reaches a person’s lungs.
words.e: Since he was sick, mucus would occasionally get stuck in his airway.
words.zh: 氣道是空氣進入人體肺部的通道。

words.d: The Almighty is a name for a god in a religion.
words.e: Before eating, they thanked the Almighty for their food.
words.zh: 「全能者」是宗教中對神的稱呼。

words.d: An advent is the arrival or beginning of an important person, thing, or event.
words.e: During the advent of radio, very few programs actually played music.
words.zh: 「降臨」是指重要人物、事物或事件的到來或開始。

words.d: A constellation is a group of stars with a recognizable shape.
words.e: That constellation in the shape of a square is named after a flying horse.
words.zh: 星座是指一群具有可辨認形狀的恆星。

words.d: If something is definitive, then it is the most official.
words.e: This library has the definitive collection of books on ancient Egypt.
words.zh: 若某事物是「確定性的」，則表示它是最官方的。

words.d: To equate one thing with another is to compare them and consider them very similar.
words.e: She equated the man’s messy appearance with a lack of responsibility.
words.zh: 將一事物與另一事物「等同」，即是比較它們並認為兩者非常相似。

words.d: To inhale means to take air or a smell into the lungs.
words.e: The camper inhaled the cool and fresh mountain air and felt relaxed.
words.zh: 「吸入」意指將空氣或氣味吸進肺部。

words.d: To invoke something is to mention it to support an argument or ask for help.
words.e: Before going into battle, the soldier invoked the name of his god for protection.
words.zh: 「援引」某事物，是指提及它以支持論點或請求幫助。

words.d: If something is lunar, then it is related to the moon.
words.e: During a lunar eclipse, Earth’s shadow darkens the moon’s surface.
words.zh: 若某事物是「月球的」，則表示它與月亮有關。

words.d: A novelty is something that is new, original, or strange.
words.e: Ice cream was a novelty for him since he rarely had any.
words.zh: 「新奇事物」是指新穎、原創或奇特的事物。

words.d: If something is outmoded, then it is no longer in fashion or use.
words.e: Black and white television sets are outmoded.
words.zh: 若某事物是「過時的」，則表示它不再流行或不再被使用。

words.d: To personalize something means to design it to meet a person’s unique needs.
words.e: He personalized the woman’s exercises to give her a better workout.
words.zh: 「個人化」某事物，是指將其設計以符合個人的獨特需求。

words.d: To pertain to something means to be related or connected to it.
words.e: All of the lawyer’s questions pertained to events around the night of the crime.
words.zh: 「歸屬於」某事物，意指與其相關或相連。

words.d: If something is primal, then it is very basic or related to the origin of something.
words.e: Cooking the food over the camp fire felt like a primal way of making a meal.
words.zh: 若某事物是「原始的」，則表示它非常基本或與某事物的起源有關。

words.d: If something is psychiatric, then it is related to mental illness or its treatment.
words.e: The doctor said the patient might have a psychiatric problem.
words.zh: 若某事物是「精神科的」，則表示它與精神疾病或其治療有關。

words.d: Psychiatry is the study and treatment of mental illness.
words.e: Studying psychiatry has taught me a lot about mental illness.
words.zh: 「精神醫學」是研究與治療精神疾病的學科。

words.d: A reflex is an unconscious action in which a body part responds to an event.
words.e: There was a reflex when the doctor hit his knee with a rubber hammer.
words.zh: 「反射」是一種無意識的動作，身體部位會對某事件作出反應。

words.d: A seizure is sudden illness in which the body loses control and shakes.
words.e: Bright flashing lights can cause seizures in children.
words.zh: 「癲癇發作」是一種突發性疾病，身體會失去控制並顫抖。

words.d: A session is a meeting at a regular time in order to discuss and solve an issue.
words.e: Every three months the managers met in a session to discuss regional sales.
words.zh: 會議是指定期舉行的聚會，目的是討論並解決問題。

words.d: A utensil is a common tool or container.
words.e: It seemed that a fork was the wrong utensil for trying to eat the soup.
words.zh: 器具是一種常用的工具或容器。

words.d: An adhesive is a substance used for sticking things together.
words.e: The carpenter used an adhesive to hold the two pieces of wood together.
words.zh: 黏合劑是用來將物品黏在一起的物質。

words.d: If something is adverse, then it is harmful.
words.e: Not eating healthy foods can have an adverse effect on your health.
words.zh: 如果某事物是不利的，則它是有害的。

words.d: A dependency is a strong need for someone or something.
words.e: The child still has a dependency on her mother.
words.zh: 依賴是指對某人或某物的強烈需求。

words.d: To dump something means to throw it away or get rid of it.
words.e: She dumped the garbage into the trash can.
words.zh: 丟棄某物意味著將其扔掉或處理掉。

words.d: If something is eternal, then it lasts forever.
words.e: She said that her love for him was eternal.
words.zh: 如果某事物是永恆的，則它永遠持續。

words.d: To fluctuate means to rise and fall in number or amount.
words.e: Prices for gasoline have been fluctuating all month.
words.zh: 波動是指在數量或數額上上升和下降。

words.d: If something moves to and fro, it moves backward and forward or side to side.
words.e: Her long hair went to and fro as she swung on the swing.
words.zh: 如果某物來回移動，則它前後或左右移動。

words.d: Inclusion is the act of including someone or something in a group.
words.e: Her inclusion into the photo club was well received.
words.zh: 包容是指將某人或某物納入群體中的行為。

words.d: If something is intermediate, then it is in the middle of two levels, places, or times.
words.e: He selected the intermediate ski hill to begin his afternoon of skiing.
words.zh: 如果某事物是中等的，則它處於兩個層級、地點或時間之間。

words.d: If something is intermittent, then it happens in a way that is not constant.
words.e: It was hard to focus because intermittent noises came from the workers outside.
words.zh: 如果某事物是間歇性的，則它以不持續的方式發生。

words.d: A mentor is a person with experience or knowledge who advises someone.
words.e: The students each chose a mentor to help them with the experiment.
words.zh: 導師是指有經驗或知識並提供建議的人。

words.d: A phoenix is an imaginary bird that burned to ashes and was reborn.
words.e: Some believe the phoenix is a symbol for rebirth and a new beginning.
words.zh: 鳳凰是一種想像中的鳥，會燃燒成灰燼後重生。

words.d: Photosynthesis is the process in which plants change sunlight and air.
words.e: The chemical in plants that makes them green is used in photosynthesis.
words.zh: 光合作用是植物利用陽光和空氣進行轉化的過程。

words.d: Pollen is the tiny yellow powder made in the flowers of plants.
words.e: During the spring, the pollen in the air makes some people become sick.
words.zh: 花粉是植物花朵中產生的微小黃色粉末。

words.d: To regain something means to get it back.
words.e: The battery regained its power after being recharged.
words.zh: 重新獲得某物意味著將其取回。

words.d: To reverse means to change to the opposite direction.
words.e: They reversed their direction after seeing the sign.
words.zh: 逆轉意味著轉向相反的方向。

words.d: A swarm is a group of flying insects.
words.e: A beautiful swarm of butterflies filled the summer sky.
words.zh: 蜂群是一群飛行的昆蟲。

words.d: Texture is the quality of something that can be known by its touch.
words.e: The texture of her skin was very soft and smooth.
words.zh: 質地是能透過觸摸感知的物品特性。

words.d: To tickle someone is to touch them in a way that causes laughter.
words.e: The mother tickled the little boy’s foot, and he screamed with laughter.
words.zh: 搔癢某人是以某種方式觸碰他們，使其發笑。

words.d: If something is vibrant, then it is bright and full of color.
words.e: Their new shirts were a vibrant shade of red.
words.zh: 若某物充滿活力，則代表它明亮且色彩豐富。

words.d: To abolish something means to put an end to it, such as a system or law.
words.e: President Lincoln abolished slavery in the US.
words.zh: 廢除某物（如制度或法律）意味著終止它。

words.d: To amend something means to change it to improve or make it accurate.
words.e: The countries were in agreement that the treaty needed to be amended.
words.zh: 修正某物意味著改變它以改善或使其準確。

words.d: To aspire means to have a strong desire to achieve or do something.
words.e: George aspired to be a doctor from a young age.
words.zh: 渴望意味著強烈想要達成或做某事。

words.d: To censor information means to remove it if it is rude or rebellious.
words.e: To protect innocent people, the location of the bomb was censored.
words.zh: 審查資訊意味著移除粗魯或叛逆的內容。

words.d: A charter is a document that describes the rights of an organization or group.
words.e: The company charter explained that all employees had to pay a tax.
words.zh: 特許狀是描述組織或群體權利的文件。

words.d: A constitution is a document of principles for a government.
words.e: The country’s constitution said a prime minister could only serve three terms.
words.zh: 憲法是記載政府原則的文件。

words.d: When a place is cosmopolitan, it is full of people from many different places.
words.e: There are dozens of different types of restaurants in a cosmopolitan city.
words.zh: 當一個地方國際化時，代表那裡有來自許多不同地方的人。

words.d: To disseminate information or knowledge means to distribute it.
words.e: The organization disseminates information about the dangers of smoking.
words.zh: 傳播資訊或知識意味著將其散佈出去。

words.d: To flatter people means to praise them in an effort to please them.
words.e: He was just flattering me when he said that my new dress looked gorgeous.
words.zh: 奉承他人是指為了取悅對方而給予讚美。

words.d: When someone is infamous, they are well known for something bad.
words.e: That news channel is infamous for presenting biased information.
words.zh: 當一個人惡名昭彰時，表示他因壞事而廣為人知。

words.d: If one is lame, they cannot walk properly due to an injury to the leg or foot.
words.e: The terrible accident left many people dead and several others lame.
words.zh: 若某人跛腳，代表因腿或腳受傷而無法正常行走。

words.d: To limp means to walk with difficulty because someone’s leg or foot is hurt.
words.e: After the injury, the player limped off of the field.
words.zh: 跛行是指因腿或腳受傷而行走困難。

words.d: An outburst is a sudden, strong expression of an emotion.
words.e: There was an outburst of cheers when the comedian took the stage.
words.zh: 爆發是指情緒突然而強烈的表達。

words.d: When a behavior is pathological, it is extreme, unacceptable, and uncontrollable.
words.e: The pathological liar could not even tell the truth about unimportant matters.
words.zh: 當行為是病態的，代表它極端、不可接受且無法控制。

words.d: When something is phenomenal, it is unusually great.
words.e: The child’s ability to play the piano is nothing short of phenomenal.
words.zh: 當某事是驚人的，表示它異常出色。

words.d: A poll is a survey in which people give their opinions about important things.
words.e: The poll showed that many people support the plan to stop gang violence.
words.zh: 民意調查是一種讓人們對重要事物表達意見的調查。

words.d: Remorse is a strong feeling of sadness and regret.
words.e: When I realized what I did, I felt remorse for my actions.
words.zh: 懊悔是一種強烈的悲傷與遺憾之情。

words.d: Secrecy is the behavior of keeping things secret.
words.e: The secrecy of the big organization made the government nervous.
words.zh: 保密是指隱瞞事情的行為。

words.d: To tackle something means to deal with it in a determined and efficient way.
words.e: Such social problems need to be tackled right away.
words.zh: 處理某事是指以堅決且有效的方式應對。

words.d: A trance is a a state where people seem asleep and have no control of themselves.
words.e: The woman’s powerful eyes often put men in a trance.
words.zh: 恍惚是一種看似沉睡且無法控制自己的狀態。

words.d: A coward is a person who lacks courage to do risky or dangerous things.
words.e: A firefighter cannot be a coward. They have to be able to act quickly.
words.zh: 懦夫是指缺乏勇氣去做冒險或危險之事的人。

words.d: To delete something means to remove or erase written material.
words.e: Several lines had been deleted from her speech.
words.zh: 刪除是指移除或擦除書面資料。

words.d: If something is firsthand, then it is from an original source.
words.e: If you want firsthand knowledge, ask someone who saw it.
words.zh: 若某事是第一手的，則它來自原始來源。

words.d: If someone is earnest, then they are honest.
words.e: The child was very earnest when she told her mother how she broke the dish.
words.zh: 若某人誠摯，則其為人誠實。

words.d: If something is ethnic, then it is related to a group with a similar culture.
words.e: Many sections of the city are home to different ethnic communities.
words.zh: 若某事物屬族裔，則其與擁有相似文化之群體相關。

words.d: To exclude someone means to not accept them into a group.
words.e: Carol was excluded from the contest because her friend was a judge.
words.zh: 排斥某人意指不接受其加入某群體。

words.d: If someone is fluent in a language, then they are able to speak it very well.
words.e: She was so fluent in German that you’d have thought she was from Germany.
words.zh: 若某人精通某語言，則其能非常流利地使用該語言。

words.d: If something is imperial, then it is of, or related to an empire.
words.e: These old imperial coins were once used in the Roman Empire.
words.zh: 若某事物屬帝國，則其與帝國相關或為帝國所有。

words.d: If something is inclusive, then it is open to all groups and people in society.
words.e: A more inclusive event would have allowed children to attend.
words.zh: 若某事物具包容性，則其對社會中所有群體與人士開放。

words.d: A legislature is the section of a government that makes laws.
words.e: The senator had served ten years in the national legislature.
words.zh: 立法機構是政府中負責制定法律的部分。

words.d: If something is linguistic, then it is concerned with language.
words.e: A linguistic way of studying culture focuses on words within that culture.
words.zh: 若某事物屬語言學範疇，則其與語言相關。

words.d: If someone is monolingual, then they speak only one language.
words.e: In today’s global economy, being monolingual limits your opportunities.
words.zh: 若某人為單語者，則其只會說一種語言。

words.d: Nationality is an identity based on the nation from which you come.
words.e: His nationality is German, but he speaks French, Spanish, and Korean.
words.zh: 國籍是基於你所屬國家的一種身份認同。

words.d: A patriot is someone who loves, supports, and defends their country.
words.e: Every year young patriots join their countries’ militaries.
words.zh: 愛國者是熱愛、支持並捍衛其國家的人。

words.d: To prosecute someone means to take legal action against them.
words.e: They were prosecuted for fishing in the river without a permit.
words.zh: 起訴某人意指對其採取法律行動。

words.d: If something is racial, then it is related to a race or races.
words.e: The differences between racial groups are physical characteristics and culture.
words.zh: 若某事物屬種族範疇，則其與一個或多個種族相關。

words.d: If something is solemn, then it is serious and honest.
words.e: The professor preferred a solemn relationship between him and his students.
words.zh: 若某事物莊嚴，則其嚴肅且真誠。

words.d: Solidarity is a union formed from common responsibilities or interests.
words.e: All the citizens came together in a show of solidarity to create change.
words.zh: 團結是由共同責任或利益所形成的聯合。

words.d: Tact is the ability to avoid offending people when dealing with problems.
words.e: Since both sides would not agree, it required someone with tact to make peace.
words.zh: 得體是指在處理問題時避免冒犯他人的能力。

words.d: To undermine someone means to betray or weaken their efforts or authority.
words.e: The documents helped to undermine the workers’ trust in their bosses’ honesty.
words.zh: 暗中破壞某人意味著背叛或削弱其努力或權威。

words.d: To allocate something means to put it aside for a certain purpose or person.
words.e: The government allocated $100 million to aid the disaster relief effort.
words.zh: 分配某物是為了特定目的或對象而將其預留出來。

words.d: When food is appetizing, it looks and smells very good.
words.e: The appetizing plate of cookies was gone in half an hour.
words.zh: 當食物看起來開胃時，它的外觀和氣味都非常好。

words.d: A blizzard is a severe snow storm with strong winds.
words.e: We did not dare to go outside during the terrible blizzard.
words.zh: 暴風雪是伴有強風的嚴重雪暴。

words.d: A cavity is a hole or space in something.
words.e: There was a small cavity in the wall of the cave where an animal lived.
words.zh: 空洞是物體中的孔洞或空間。

words.d: If something moves clockwise, it moves in a circle in the same direction as a clock.
words.e: Turn the screw clockwise to tighten it.
words.zh: 若某物順時針移動，則它沿著與時鐘相同的方向繞圈。

words.d: When circles or rings are concentric, they have the same center.
words.e: The target was a series of concentric circles.
words.zh: 當圓圈或環狀物同心時，它們擁有相同的中心點。

words.d: Courtesy is the excellence of manners or social conduct.
words.e: Jenna always behaves with great courtesy when people visit her home.
words.zh: 禮貌是舉止或社交行為的卓越表現。

words.d: When food is crisp, it is hard or has a hard surface in a way that is pleasant.
words.e: My favorite snack is a bag of crisp, delicious potato chips.
words.zh: 當食物酥脆時，它質地堅硬或表面硬脆，令人愉悅。

words.d: Discord is disagreement or fighting between people.
words.e: There was much discord between the experts on the talk show.
words.zh: 不和是人們之間的意見分歧或爭鬥。

words.d: When something is frigid, it is extremely cold.
words.e: We decided not to go on the hike because the weather was too frigid.
words.zh: 當某物嚴寒時，它極度寒冷。

words.d: To generate something means to cause it to develop or begin.
words.e: The mayor promised to generate new jobs and programs to help the poor.
words.zh: 產生某物意味著促使它發展或開始出現。

words.d: When something is glacial, it relates to large masses of ice that move slowly.
words.e: Penguins thrive in the glacial regions of Antarctica.
words.zh: 當某物與冰川相關時，它涉及緩慢移動的巨大冰體。

words.d: An interchange of ideas between people is a discussion of each person’s idea.
words.e: There was an interchange of ideas between the groups.
words.zh: 人與人之間的思想交流，是對各自觀點的討論。

words.d: A locker is a small cabinet with a lock where people store their possessions.
words.e: I keep my school books in my locker.
words.zh: 置物櫃是一種帶鎖的小櫃子，用來存放個人物品。

words.d: When something is multicultural, it relates to many different cultures.
words.e: Everyone was welcomed to attend the multicultural celebration.
words.zh: 當某事物是多元文化的，它涉及許多不同的文化。

words.d: An omission is something that has been left out or not done.
words.e: Mike was upset because of the omission of his name during the ceremony.
words.zh: 遺漏是指某事物被忽略或未完成。

words.d: To oversee something means to make sure that it is being done properly.
words.e: His job was to oversee the progress of the construction project.
words.zh: 監督某事意味著確保其正確進行。

words.d: To pierce something means to make a hole in it using a sharp object.
words.e: The arrow pierced the target in the very center.
words.zh: 刺穿某物是指用尖銳物體在上面開一個洞。

words.d: To replicate something is to do it in the exact same way as someone before.
words.e: I would love to replicate my father’s achievements in school.
words.zh: 複製某事是指以與前人完全相同的方式進行。

words.d: When something is wavy, it is not straight but has a series of curves.
words.e: The child drew wavy lines all over the piece of paper.
words.zh: 當某物呈波浪狀時，它不是筆直的，而是有一系列彎曲。

words.d: To abide by something, like a rule, means to obey it.
words.e: If you want to play the game with us, you must be willing to abide by our rules.
words.zh: 遵守某事物（如規則）意味著服從它。

words.d: Conversion is the act of changing something into a different state or form.
words.e: The city discussed the conversion of the parking lot into a skateboard park.
words.zh: 轉換是將某事物改變為不同狀態或形式的行為。

words.d: To cram things means to put them into a place that can barely contain them.
words.e: He crammed all of his shirts into the top drawer of the dresser.
words.zh: 塞入東西是指把它們放進幾乎裝不下的空間。

words.d: To defer means to arrange for an action to happen at a later time.
words.e: The girl was very sleepy, so she chose to defer her bath until morning.
words.zh: 延後是指安排某行動在較晚的時間發生。

words.d: To export products means to sell them to other countries.
words.e: The United States exports many cereals and grains.
words.zh: 出口產品是指將它們賣到其他國家。

words.d: Fumes are unhealthy smoke and gases that are made by fires or chemicals.
words.e: The engine put unpleasant, black fumes into the air.
words.zh: 煙霧是火災或化學物質產生的有害氣體。

words.d: When something is habitual, it is a behavior that a person usually does or has.
words.e: The man was a habitual liar who was incapable of being honest with the police.
words.zh: 當某行為是習慣性的，它是一個人通常會做或擁有的行為。

words.d: To justify something means to show or prove that it is necessary.
words.e: The government tried to justify its decision to bring the country into a war.
words.zh: 證明某事合理是指顯示或證明它是必要的。

words.d: Output is the amount of something that a person or thing produces.
words.e: The boss hired more workers to increase the factory’s output of products.
words.zh: 產出是指個人或事物所生產的某種東西的數量。

words.d: Overpopulation is the state of having too many people in an area.
words.e: The organization is concerned with overpopulation of the world.
words.zh: 人口過剩是指某個地區有太多人的狀態。

words.d: A patent is a right to be the only person allowed to make or sell a new product.
words.e: He quickly established a patent for his brilliant invention.
words.zh: 專利權是指唯一被允許製造或銷售新產品的權利。

words.d: To penalize someone means to punish him or her.
words.e: The team was penalized when they broke the rules.
words.zh: 懲罰某人意味著對他或她進行處罰。

words.d: Petroleum is a liquid natural resource from which many fuels are made.
words.e: The petroleum at that factory is used to make gasoline.
words.zh: 石油是一種液態天然資源，許多燃料都由其製成。

words.d: A prototype is a new machine that is not ready to be made in large quantities.
words.e: He introduced his prototype to the motorcycle company.
words.zh: 原型機是一種尚未準備好大量生產的新機器。

words.d: A scrap of something is a small amount of it.
words.e: Chris liked to use many scraps of paper when thinking up ideas.
words.zh: 某物的碎片是指它的少量部分。

words.d: A sector is a part of a country’s economy in a specific type of industry.
words.e: We learned about just a few of the factories within the manufacturing sector.
words.zh: 部門是指一個國家經濟中特定行業類別的一部分。

words.d: To subscribe to something is to agree or to concur with it.
words.e: She subscribed to the view that musical education should be kept in schools.
words.zh: 認同某事是指同意或贊同它。

words.d: To subsist means to have the food, water, and money needed to stay alive.
words.e: Some people are forced to subsist on only a few dollars a month.
words.zh: 生存是指擁有維持生命所需的食物、水和金錢。

words.d: To suspend something means to delay or stop it from happening for a while.
words.e: The oil company suspended production until it was sure the factory was safe.
words.zh: 暫停某事是指延遲或阻止它發生一段時間。

words.d: A synthesis is a combination of different ideas or styles.
words.e: The band’s music was a synthesis of many different musical genres.
words.zh: 綜合是指不同想法或風格的結合。

words.d: An acrobat is a person who entertains people by doing amazing physical things.
words.e: There were acrobats at the circus that did impressive and complicated jumps.
words.zh: 雜技演員是指透過表演驚人體能動作來娛樂大眾的人。

words.d: Advocacy is the act of supporting or recommending something.
words.e: Janine’s strong advocacy for the event made people want to attend.
words.zh: 倡導是指支持或推薦某事的行為。

words.d: If something is communal, it involves a group of people.
words.e: On the weekends, Theo and his son picked up trash in the communal park.
words.zh: 如果某事物是公共的，則涉及一群人。

words.d: If something is fluid, it is smooth and moves gracefully.
words.e: Boris was a famous ballet dancer because people admired his fluid movements.
words.zh: 若某物流暢，則表示其平滑且動作優雅。

words.d: To harmonize means to make different things go well together.
words.e: The sweet and sour flavors of the dish harmonized well and tasted great.
words.zh: 和諧化意指讓不同事物良好地協調共存。

words.d: If someone is industrious, they work hard.
words.e: Dennis was very industrious, so he never had problems finding a job.
words.zh: 勤奮的人工作努力。

words.d: When someone is inventive, they are good at creating new things.
words.e: The inventive student built a robot to help her with her chores.
words.zh: 富有創造力的人擅長創造新事物。

words.d: If something is judicial, it is related to judges or courts of law.
words.e: The laws changed the way the judicial system was structured.
words.zh: 司法相關的事物與法官或法院有關。

words.d: Mandarin is one of the two main Chinese languages.
words.e: Although John’s parents both spoke Mandarin, he could only speak English.
words.zh: 官話是中文的兩大主要語言之一。

words.d: If something is metropolitan, it relates to a large city.
words.e: All of the metropolitan excitement of New York City made Rufus nervous.
words.zh: 大都會的意指與大城市相關。

words.d: To mimic something means to copy the way it sounds or moves.
words.e: The hunter mimicked a duck’s call and shot the two ducks that called back.
words.zh: 模仿某物即複製其聲音或動作方式。

words.d: When something is misguided, it is based on bad judgment or wrong beliefs.
words.e: The snake bit Molly during her misguided attempt to grab it.
words.zh: 誤導的事物基於錯誤判斷或信念。

words.d: To rehearse means to practice and prepare for a performance in front of people.
words.e: Vick’s band rehearsed all week before their first show.
words.zh: 排練意指為公開表演練習與準備。

words.d: To scorn someone means to behave without respect toward them.
words.e: The criminal was scorned by everyone in the community.
words.zh: 輕視某人即對其表現不敬。

words.d: If something is sensory, it is related to the senses.
words.e: Iris and Eric preferred the sensory thrill of watching movies in the theater.
words.zh: 感官相關的事物與知覺有關。

words.d: If something is staple, it is standard or basic.
words.e: Hamburgers, steaks and hotdogs are staple foods to cook on a grill.
words.zh: 基本的事物是標準或基礎的。

words.d: A statute is a law that is official and has been written down.
words.e: The new statute made it illegal to walk dogs in the park.
words.zh: 法規是正式且書面化的法律。

words.d: A veteran is someone who has a lot of experience doing something.
words.e: Hank was the team veteran and had played in more games than anyone else.
words.zh: 資深者指在某領域經驗豐富的人。

words.d: A villain is an evil person who breaks the law or hurts others.
words.e: The villain escaped with half of my family’s valuables.
words.zh: 反派是指違法或傷害他人的邪惡之人。

words.d: A vine is a plant that has long, twisting stems and climbs upward.
words.e: The branches of the trees were covered with vines.
words.zh: 藤蔓是一種具有長而纏繞的莖、向上攀爬的植物。

words.d: To constrain something means to limit its development.
words.e: Jim cannot join us because he is constrained by previous plans.
words.zh: 約束某事物意味著限制其發展。

words.d: A depot is a bus or train station.
words.e: He waited for his mother to arrive at the depot.
words.zh: 車站是巴士或火車的停靠站。

words.d: To emulate people means to imitate them because they are greatly admired.
words.e: As a small boy, he always tried to emulate his big brother.
words.zh: 效仿他人是指因為極度欽佩而模仿他們。

words.d: The forefinger is the finger between one’s thumb and middle finger.
words.e: He shouted, “ There it is!” and pointed with his forefinger.
words.zh: 食指是位於拇指與中指之間的手指。

words.d: The guts are all the organs inside a person or animal.
words.e: The doctor can tell you every process that happens in one’s guts.
words.zh: 內臟是一個人或動物體內的所有器官。

words.d: When something is inherent, it is a natural part of something else.
words.e: Sweating is an inherent bodily function when exercising.
words.zh: 當某事物是固有的，它便是其他事物的自然組成部分。

words.d: To intimidate means to frighten others.
words.e: My dad intimidates my friends whenever they visit.
words.zh: 恐嚇意味著使他人感到害怕。

words.d: A janitor is a person who makes repairs and takes care of a building.
words.e: The school janitor cleaned up the messy cafeteria.
words.zh: 管理員是負責維修與照管建築物的人。

words.d: When something is moist, it is slightly wet.
words.e: The ground is still moist from the rain last night.
words.zh: 潮濕時，表示稍微濕潤。

words.d: Nope is an informal way of saying “ no.”
words.e: He asked if I had any money, and I had to say, “Nope. ”
words.zh: 「不」是「不是」的非正式說法。

words.d: To prod means to push someone or something with a finger or pointed object.
words.e: The bully prodded me in the chest with his finger.
words.zh: 戳是指用手指或尖物推某人或某物。

words.d: A ransom is a sum of money paid to a kidnapper to set the person free.
words.e: He kidnapped the prince and demanded $1 million as ransom.
words.zh: 贖金是支付給綁架者以換取人質自由的款項。

words.d: To restrain someone or something means to use physical strength to stop them.
words.e: Mike restrained Allen from reaching the door.
words.zh: 制止某人或某物是指使用體力來阻止他們。

words.d: Saliva is the watery liquid in people’s mouths that helps in digestion.
